acceptance:
1. A reusable delimiter helper exists for untrusted artifact bodies.
2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.
- Do not rewrite unrelated server text egress paths.

description:
PROBLEM
store.go:2172 re-filters Status==Open when iterating Blocked() results, but EvaluateLifecycleQueue already classified the bucket correctly. The double-filter creates a discrepancy: the queue classification is the source of truth, but the Status check can disagree if queued beads have status != StatusOpen in the LifecycleBucketWaitingDependencies bucket.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:2140-2161 readyFiltered delegates to classifyLifecycleQueue for bucket classification.
cli/internal/bead/store.go:2163-2175 Blocked() calls classifyLifecycleQueue and re-filters Status==Open at line 2172.
lifecycle.go:EvaluateLifecycleQueue correctly identifies LifecycleBucketWaitingDependencies beads regardless of Status.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Remove the Status==Open check at store.go:2172.
2. Update Blocked() comment to clarify it returns beads in LifecycleBucketWaitingDependencies; the bucket designation is the single source of truth.
3. Verify Blocked() behavior is unchanged by running ReadyExecutionBreakdown breakdown tests.

NON-SCOPE
Do not refactor EvaluateLifecycleQueue. Do not change the bucket classification algorithm.
title: code: remove redundant Status==Open filter from store.go:2172

description:
PROBLEM
When the run-detail page exposes raw prompts, responses, stderr, and tool args to anyone with project read access, there is no audit trail recording that a raw transcript was viewed. The trust boundary documented in FEAT-008 requires an emitted audit event so access to sensitive raw transcript material is observable.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go:29-145 resolves the Run fields (prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls, bundleFiles, bundleFile) but emits no audit event when raw transcript content (prompt/response/stderr/transcript) is accessed.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs_test.go:340-357 covers run resolution but has no assertion that a 'raw transcript viewed' audit event is emitted on access.

PROPOSED FIX
- Emit a 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in the Run resolver when raw transcript content (prompt, response, stderr, or transcript) is accessed, carrying run id and viewer identity, consistent with the existing run_detail_view audit path.
- Add TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed in cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs_test.go asserting the event is emitted exactly once on raw-transcript access and not emitted when only non-transcript fields are read.

NON-SCOPE
- Spec documentation edits to FEAT-008/010/019 (separate child).
- Frontend / Playwright changes (separate child).
- New auth or RBAC beyond emitting the audit event.
title: server: add raw-transcript audit event for run-detail access

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
`ddx doctor` must surface both install tiers so operators can see whether a plugin is installed globally, per-project, or lazy-resolving to global. The governing spec/docs must also document the dual install topology and lookup precedence so the behavior is discoverable and durable.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:418 `reportSkillInstallTopology(projectRoot)` computes a global path and a project path with statuses (doctor.go:436/441/444 `lazy-resolves-to-global`) and prints two lines at doctor.go:447-448: `Global Install (%s) — %s` and `Project Install (%s) — %s`. These lines lack a regression test.
- The dual-topology decision and precedence are not yet fully reflected in the spec/docs: ADR-027 (skill-install-topology), library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works", and CLAUDE.md "Persona System".

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a test asserting `ddx doctor` output contains both the `Global Install (` and `Project Install (` lines with a correct status (`ok` | `missing` | `lazy-resolves-to-global`) given seeded/absent tiers.
- Update docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md to document install-to-agent (global) vs install-in-project (in-tree + convention) and the project→global→baked-in precedence.
- Update CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section to state the install precedence, and library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" to document install locations. Land all spec/doc edits in this bead's commit.

NON-SCOPE
- Install/init/update behavioral changes — sibling beads (this bead consumes their behavior for the doctor report only).
- Removing the per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Sibling install/update beads (doctor reports the layers they create); land this bead after they merge so the report reflects real behavior.
title: doctor + docs: report both install layers and land skill-install-topology spec updates

acceptance:
1. `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` returns `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global`, honoring an overridden XDG_DATA_HOME, verified by TestGlobalDir_HonorsXDGDataHome.
2. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer "project" with the project path when both project and global copies exist — TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts the project copy wins.
3. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer "global" with the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists — TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal.
4. `registry.ResolvePlugin` returns layer "baked-in" for the default `ddx` plugin when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name — TestPluginLookup_BakedInDefaultOnly.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/ddxroot/... ./internal/registry/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx has no runtime concept of a "global" plugin install nor a precedence order for resolving a plugin name. Every install/lookup path is hardcoded to the per-project tree via ddxroot.Path(). Before `--global` install (sibling bead) or `ddx init --global` (sibling bead) can exist, there must be a single resolver that, given a plugin name, returns its on-disk path by checking the project layer first, then a global layer, then the baked-in default.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go:66 `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, src, projectRoot)` only knows the project root; there is no global target.
- cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75 `Path()` resolves only the project root (in-tree `<project>/.ddx` or convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` at line 86). There is no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` helper.
- cli/internal/skills/install.go:28 `Install(src, projectRoot, opts)` and all callers assume a single project destination.
- No `ResolvePlugin`-style function exists anywhere under cli/internal/registry.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `GlobalDir()` to cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go returning `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` (reuse the existing `xdgDataHome()` helper at ddxroot.go:89). The plugins root is `GlobalDir()/plugins`.
2. Add `registry.ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, error)` implementing precedence:
   - `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name>` if the dir exists → layer "project".
   - `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>` if it exists → layer "global".
   - baked-in default (defaultplugin.FS, the `ddx` plugin only) → layer "baked-in"; return an error for any other unresolved name.
3. Export the layer string so doctor/init/install (sibling beads) can report which layer answered.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring `--global` into install/update commands (sibling bead: install/update --global).
- `ddx init --global` and agent-tier symlinks (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing the existing per-project install. Both layers coexist.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ddxroot.Path()` and the new `GlobalDir()` must share the same XDG_DATA_HOME convention agreed there. Sibling beads in this set depend on this resolver.
title: registry: add two-layer plugin resolution (project → global → baked-in) with global XDG path helper

acceptance:
1. Add `TestDetectInstalledBinaryBehindSourceIgnoresTrackerOnlyAheadCommit` or equivalent coverage proving a DDx checkout with only `.ddx/beads.jsonl`/execution evidence commits ahead of the installed binary does not produce a staleness warning/block.
2. Update existing stale-binary tests so the positive stale case advances a build-affecting path such as `cli/cmd/...`, not `README.md` or arbitrary docs.
3. `TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan` and `TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim` still prove work/try fail before queue scan, claim, readiness, or dispatch when a build-affecting CLI source commit is ahead of the installed binary.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run "TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan|TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim|TestVersionWarnsStaleSourceBinary|TestDetectInstalledBinaryBehindSourceIgnoresTrackerOnlyAheadCommit|TestVersion_StaleWarning" -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
6. After the fix is installed, committing a tracker-only bead update in the DDx repo does not make `/home/erik/.local/bin/ddx version` warn that the installed binary is behind source HEAD.
description:
PROBLEM
The `ddx-08b452ee` stale-source binary preflight now blocks `ddx work`/`ddx try` when the installed DDx binary commit is behind the DDx source checkout. During normal queue operation, DDx immediately creates tracker/evidence commits such as `chore(beads): update ...` after installing a verified build. Those commits do not affect the compiled CLI, but they make source HEAD advance past the installed binary commit. The next `ddx work --watch --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` restart can then fail before scanning the queue, forcing meaningless rebuilds after every tracker-only bookkeeping commit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/command_factory.go:671-715` treats any descendant commit between the binary commit and source HEAD as binary staleness. It counts all commits with `git rev-list --count <binary>..<head>` and does not check whether the diff contains CLI/build-affecting paths. `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions/**`, `.ddx/metrics/**`, and `.ddx/attachments/**` commits therefore trigger the same fail-fast path as real Go source changes.

PROPOSED FIX
Only report source-checkout binary staleness when commits ahead of the installed binary include DDx build-affecting inputs. Tracker, execution evidence, metrics, attachments, docs, and other non-binary bookkeeping commits must not block `ddx work`, `ddx try`, or `ddx version`. Keep the existing fail-fast behavior for Go/module/build/install changes that can change the installed CLI.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken the fail-fast behavior for real CLI source changes. Do not require operators to tag/install after tracker-only commits. Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; use `ddx bead` commands for tracker state.
title: work: ignore tracker-only commits in stale-binary preflight

4. TestClose_SupersededCascade_OneHopCycleGuard: creates Y, X (open, superseded-by:Y), Z (open, superseded-by:X). Closes Y. Asserts X closes, Z remains open (cascade deferred), no infinite loop.
5. TestClose_SupersededCascade_Idempotent: closing an already-closed Y a second time produces no new events and no mutations.
6. TestClose_SupersededCascade_ClaimedOrInProgress_NotCascaded: X has a live claim_id; closing Y does not cascade X.

description:
PROBLEM
Beads marked superseded-by:<Y> never close automatically when Y closes. ddx-847df529 (parent ddx-09d7d787) carries superseded-by:ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238 and a note "Do not dispatch this bead independently"; the superseder closed weeks ago but the leftover stays open and blocks its parent epic. This is a primary contributor to the stuck-queue state on the current project (RC-2 in the FEAT-010 reason taxonomy).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) closes only Y. It does not look for open beads X with superseded-by==Y and cascade-close them. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires this cascade with a strict scope guard.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) with a post-close cascade step. After Y closes, scan for open beads X where supersession-by-Y is the sole remaining reason to stay open. Strict scope contract (each must hold; if any fails, surface in ddx work focus Section A, do NOT cascade):
- X is open AND (X.superseded-by == Y OR X carries label superseded-by:Y) AND Y is now closed.
- X has no other open dependents (no open bead Z with Z.dependencies referencing X).
- X has no open dependencies of its own beyond the supersession marker.
- X has attempt_count == 0.
- X has no operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp.
- X is not currently claimed or in-progress (no live claim_id).
- X carries no manual-hold / no-auto-close / container override label.

Re-read X's current tracker state inside the same Close(Y) invocation before mutating — a concurrent worker may have changed X since the scan started.

Idempotent: re-running Close(Y) is a no-op for already-closed X.
Cycle-guarded: track visited IDs within one Close(Y) call; cascade is one hop deep. If X is a superseder for an open Z, Z's cascade is DEFERRED to a separate operator action (do not transitively cascade).
Audit: emit a superseded_close event on X with body {closed_by_cascade_of: Y, reason: "closed_as_superseded_via:Y"}. Operators can grep events to reverse.

Add operator override flag at the work command surface in a separate bead (--no-auto-supersede-close); the Close() code path always runs the cascade unless the flag has been threaded through. For this bead, accept the cascade always firing; the flag wiring is the work-loop bead's responsibility.

NON-SCOPE
- Cascading transitively beyond one hop (deferred to a future bead if ever needed).
- The work-loop wiring of --no-auto-supersede-close flag (separate bead).
- RC-3 dead-intermediate cleanup walk-up (separate bead, depends on this one).

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. No deps.
title: store: cascade-close superseded children inside Close()

description:
PROBLEM
DDx work can successfully land a bead and then stop in operator-attention while committing durable audit outputs. The observed Fizeau watcher failure after fizeau-737b19b1 was: staging durable audit outputs: fatal: pathspec 'ddx/beads.jsonl' did not match any files. That leaves .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, and .ddx/attachments dirty in the project checkout and stops autonomous work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:133-138 calls strings.TrimSpace(line) before parsing fixed-width git status --short output with line[3:]. For an unstaged tracked file line like ' M .ddx/beads.jsonl', trimming removes the leading status column, so line[3:] starts after the dot and produces 'ddx/beads.jsonl'. commitDurableAuditOutputsLocked then passes that invalid pathspec to git add/commit at cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:107-122.

PROPOSED FIX
Parse git status --short output without trimming away the two status columns. Preserve the leading dot for tracked dirty paths, continue to support untracked paths and rename arrows, and add regression coverage for both the parser and full CommitDurableAuditOutputs path.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change execute-bead landing, tracker storage format, Fizeau routing behavior, or the durable audit commit message format. Do not add an alternate manual cleanup path; the normal worker finalizer must commit these outputs correctly.

DEPS
None.
title: work: preserve leading dot in durable audit path parser

description:
PROBLEM
Two code paths write to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl as part of normal operation, and neither auto-commits the result. Operators end up with persistent untracked-and-modified worktree state that contradicts the project's intent (1083 attachment files ARE tracked across 11.6 MB of committed history — this is first-class audit-trail data, not detritus).

Concrete demonstration today:

1. Run `ddx try <id>` on 9 beads → 9 new untracked .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/ dirs appear in main repo. Each contains routing decisions, cost-per-attempt, execution-routing-intent, prose-check.findings, execute-bead outcome — exactly the audit data the convention says we keep.
2. Run `ddx bead migrate` → 142 events externalized into existing tracked attachment files (showing as M), 1 new untracked attachment dir, 292 beads archived (modifying .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl), and a fresh worktree of 151 dirty files. None committed.
3. The audit log clearly shows the historical pattern: commits like `878931ce3 chore(beads): commit missing event attachments` and `5d54258fd Merge bead ... attempt ... into main` periodically sweep up the accumulation — but it's recurring manual cleanup, not the steady-state design.

ROOT CAUSE
Two distinct writers, neither stages or commits its output:

A. The execute-loop emits per-attempt routing/cost/intent events. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go emitRouteFailureEvent (line 3872) and the routing/cost emitters all call store.AppendEvent → the bead store writes to .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl when the bead's events are externalized (or to the inline events array when not). The Store.AppendEvent path has no follow-up `git add`. The execute-loop's chore("add execution evidence") commit (visible in git log as `chore: add execution evidence [<run-id>]`) stages `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` per AGENTS.md convention — but NOT `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/`.

B. cli/internal/bead/migrate.go ArchiveWithEvents (line 561) externalizes inline events from closed/archived beads to attachments. After externalizing 142 events today across many files, the migrate command exits without staging or committing the resulting changes. The operator command surface (cli/cmd/bead.go migrate handler) has no auto-commit step analogous to the bead store's other auto-commit paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Execute-loop landing path: extend the existing post-merge "chore(beads): commit missing event attachments"-style commit (or the bead's own evidence commit) to ALSO stage .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/ for the bead just landed. Use the same git-index-lock recovery (cli/internal/gitlock per ddx-6a518fd9) so concurrent workers and the statusline poller do not race.
2. `ddx bead migrate` auto-commit: after a successful migrate that wrote any attachment changes (newly externalized + newly archived rows), call a single auto-commit at end-of-run with message `chore(beads): externalize and archive (<N> events to <M> attachments, <K> beads archived)`. Skip the commit entirely when no changes were written (idempotent re-run is a no-op).
3. Regression invariant: after `ddx try <id>` completes with `success`/`already_satisfied`, `git status --short` in the main repo MUST show zero untracked files under `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/` for that bead.
4. Operator escape hatch: `ddx bead reconcile-attachments` — single command that stages every untracked .ddx/attachments/ dir and any modified attachment files, commits as `chore(beads): reconcile missing attachment commits (<N> dirs, <M> modified)`. Useful for one-shot cleanup of pre-existing accumulation when this fix lands (today's 10 untracked + 141 modified dirs are an immediate use case).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change WHAT gets externalized (the migrate event-externalization logic is fine).
- Do not change ATTACHMENT FORMAT or location.
- Do not add attachments to .gitignore (they ARE valuable cross-machine audit data per characterization).
- Do not auto-commit .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ — that's per-machine and stays untracked under existing convention.

DEPS
ddx-6a518fd9 (landed) — required so the auto-commits route through index-lock recovery and don't bounce on operator/statusline git contention.
title: bead: auto-commit attachment writes from execute-loop and migrate

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- All five new files (`operation.go`, `id.go`, `errors.go`, `context.go`, `backend.go` expansion) exist and compile.
- `Operation` interface declared with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter implements `Operation`. Test: `TestOperation_MutateFuncAdapter_RoundTrip`.
- ID generation: tests `TestValidateID_AcceptsValidIDs`, `TestValidateID_RejectsInvalidCharset`, `TestValidateID_RejectsLengthBounds`, `TestRandomHexIDGenerator_ProducesValidIDs` (100 generations all pass `ValidateID`), `TestSequentialIDGenerator_ProducesUniqueOrdered` (1000 generations all distinct + lexicographically increasing).

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 1

acceptance:
AC1 TestReviewRunnerInjectsBead — after a cycle completes, Runner.Run calls tracker.Inject with kind=review-finding, correct parent and payload fields, and does not invoke the legacy hook writer.
AC2 TestNoAutoReviewFlagSuppressesInjection — invoking `helix review --no-auto-review` (and setting helix.review.auto_inject=false) causes Runner.Run to skip injection and return nil; tracker has no new review-finding bead afterward.
AC3 TestExecuteLoopConsumesReviewFinding — end-to-end: a parent bead completes, review-finding bead is injected, execute-loop pops it on the next tick, runs the review handler, and writes the evidence bundle to the canonical path.
AC4 TestDeprecatedHookKeyWarns — setting only the legacy hooks.auto_review key still works but emits a deprecation warning naming the new key.

description:
PROBLEM: HELIX `review` currently runs the review engine inline as a post-cycle hook on the parent bead and writes findings directly; this bypasses the ready queue, prevents scheduling/retry, and produces hook-shaped output rather than a standard evidence bundle. Operators cannot opt out except by disabling the entire hook system.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/helix/review/runner.go:1 — Runner.Run invokes engine.Execute synchronously after cycle completion and writes to .helix/reviews/ rather than calling tracker.Inject. cmd/helix/review.go:1 — CLI has no `--no-auto-review` flag; auto-injection toggle is buried in config under hooks.auto_review which is a runtime hook switch, not an injection switch.

PROPOSED FIX: (a) Replace the post-cycle hook invocation with `tracker.Inject(ctx, parent, BeadKindReviewFinding, payload)` using the shared API from child 1; payload carries parent bead id, base_rev, result_rev, and review profile id. (b) Add `--no-auto-review` flag to `cmd/helix/review.go` and equivalent `helix.review.auto_inject` config key (default true); when false, Runner.Run returns without injecting. (c) Delete the old hook-output writer call path once injection is wired; keep config key `hooks.auto_review` only as a deprecated alias that maps to the new key with a one-cycle warning.

NON-SCOPE: Alignment path (covered by child 3). Tracker bead-kind plumbing (covered by child 1). Reformatting the review-finding payload schema beyond the fields named above. Inherits all parent non-scope items. Governing artifact: parent helix-e26584d7. Depends on: child 1 (bead kind + Inject API + execute-loop dispatcher).

DDx maintainers should reinterpret against current DDx surface before acting on it.

description:
PROBLEM
The readiness producer and pre-claim intake consumer can drift on the `readiness_checks` JSON shape. That drift caused the parent incident when one side emitted `verdict` as a bool and the other side expected a string.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The pre-claim prompt specifies the expected output shape in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:616-621`, while the Go consumer decodes `ReadinessChecks` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:68` and `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:781-814`. Without a shared schema and CI gate, prompt text and decoder behavior can evolve independently.

PROPOSED FIX
Define `readiness_checks` in a shared JSON schema file in the agent package, make the prompt/schema/decoder tests consume the same schema, and add a CI-accessible Make target that fails on schema drift. The schema must allow exactly the verdict forms accepted by the decoder from the decoder child: bool, string, null, or absent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the decoder tolerance itself except as needed to align with the existing decoder contract; this child depends on the decoder child. Do not implement same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warnings, README documentation, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not introduce a network-dependent schema validation step. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
Depends on the decoder child that canonicalizes bool/string/null/absent verdicts.

description:
PROBLEM
`cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run TestWorkerReport_DistinguishesCandidateAndLandedRev -count=1 -v` fails on current `main`. The failure blocks broad `cd cli && go test ./... -count=1` proof for parent bead `ddx-06cbaa90` and any other full CLI gate.

Observed failure on 2026-05-15:
- `cli/internal/server/workers_gates_test.go:384` expects `res.LandedRev` to equal `refs/heads/main` after `evaluateGatesAndSubmit`, but `res.LandedRev` remains the candidate/implementation landing SHA while `refs/heads/main` has advanced.
- `cli/internal/server/workers_gates_test.go:419` repeats the same mismatch through the synthesized `WorkerExecutionResult`.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/workers_gates_test.go:347-389` documents and asserts that `LandedRev` is the final target branch tip after landing.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:128-132` defines `LandResult.LandedTip` as the target branch tip immediately after implementation landing, before any trailing audit-only evidence/final-result commit.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1631-1646` intentionally maps `ExecuteBeadResult.LandedRev` and the compatibility `ResultRev` alias to `land.LandedTip`, not necessarily `land.NewTip`.
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:1790-1799` submits through the land coordinator and applies that mapping, so the failing server test is exercising the existing land-result contract rather than a random flake.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the server worker report contract explicit and green. Either update `TestWorkerReport_DistinguishesCandidateAndLandedRev` to match the intended `LandedTip` semantics and add an assertion for the final branch/audit tip separately, or change the mapping contract if the correct product behavior is that `LandedRev` should always equal the final target branch tip. Do not paper over the failure by deleting the test; preserve separate evidence for implementation rev, landed implementation rev, and final target tip when they differ.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/server/workers_gates_test.go
- cli/internal/server/workers.go, only if worker-result construction needs a field/contract update
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go, only if the intended land-result mapping contract changes

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change queue selection, route selection, or readiness behavior.
- Do not remove evidence/final-result commits.
- Do not weaken unrelated worker gate tests.

None.

acceptance:

2. TestDDxRoot_InTreeMode asserts Path returns <projectRoot>/.ddx when that dir exists.
3. TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_XDGPath asserts Path returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/github.com/easel/ddx for a project without .ddx/ and with `git remote get-url origin = git@github.com:easel/ddx.git`.
4. TestDDxRoot_ConventionMode_LocalFallback covers the no-remote `local/<basename>-<sha>/` form.
5. TestDDxRoot_URLParsing covers https, ssh, custom-host, missing-remote cases (table-driven).
6. TestDDxRoot_BootstrapInitsGitRepoInXDG asserts the XDG dir is created with `git init` on first bootstrap and a HEAD commit exists.
7. TestDDxRoot_BootstrapIdempotent asserts two concurrent calls do not race-create.
8. TestWorktreeRegistry_FirstWorktreeIsMaster asserts auto-master on first access.
9. TestWorktreeRegistry_SubsequentRegisterNonMaster asserts later worktrees register but do not overwrite master.
10. TestWorktreeRegistry_OperatorRePin asserts `ddx project worktree master <path>` updates the pin.
11. Static check via lefthook: `rg 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"' cli/ --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits. The lint rule is wired into pre-commit.
12. Specs listed under SPEC IMPACT are updated in the same commit as the code refactor. Each updated spec references this bead ID.
13. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 green. Existing tests pass without modification.
14. lefthook run pre-commit passes (including the new no-hardcoded-.ddx-paths rule).
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.

SPEC IMPACT — the following docs reference .ddx/ paths and must be updated in this bead:
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md (state paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md (project identity)
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021-operator-prompt-beads-web-write-path.md (operator-prompt paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md (worker status paths)
- docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md (FEAT-010 runs/)

Each spec update: replace .ddx/<path> citations with ddxroot.Path()/<path> AND add a "project identity" subsection where applicable referencing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Skills/personas install strategy (principle 2 — separate bead).
- Test matrix (separate bead).
- Migrating today's in-tree ddx-repo to convention mode (out of scope; this repo stays in-tree as the dogfooding canon).

DEPS
None. This is the foundational refactor.

description:
PROBLEM
  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).
  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).
  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.
  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.
  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: guard denials should skip the candidate rather than wedge the queue.
- P2: each guard owns only its own skip policy.
- P4: a denied bead stays isolated from other beads.
title: C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps + pick() derives from drain.result

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx install` and `ddx update` only ever target the per-project plugin tree. There is no way to install a skill/persona/plugin once per machine, so operators must repeat installs in every project. Three install destinations must be supported: global (XDG), project in-tree (`<project>/.ddx`), and project convention (XDG projects/...).

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go:36 `RunE: f.runInstall` and its flag set (install.go:38-39) have no `--global` flag; install always targets the project under `.ddx/plugins/<name>/` (install.go:27-28).
- cli/cmd/update.go:57 `runUpdate` and update.go:194 `performUpdate` operate only on the working dir's project tree; update.go:112 `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets(workingDir)` is project-scoped.
- Both route through ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and never reference the global layer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx install` (cli/cmd/install.go) and `ddx update` (cli/cmd/update.go).
2. install --global: write content to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` (via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from the resolver bead) and create symlinks/copies into agent-tier paths `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>` (copy on platforms lacking symlink support).
3. install (no --global): keep writing to `ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/` and link into `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` AND `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`. In in-tree mode the target is `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`; in convention mode it is `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>` and the project agent-tier links point into that XDG path.
4. update --global updates only the global plugin tree; update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode (in-tree vs convention) is active, via `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets`.

NON-SCOPE
- The plugin resolver/precedence (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution) — consume `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` from it.
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup (sibling bead: init topology).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Removing per-project install; both modes coexist.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution); sibling registry-resolver bead (provides `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` and layer semantics).

description:
PROBLEM
  Story 14 requires federated query resolvers that aggregate beads, runs, and projects across registered spokes. Today the GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql defines FederatedBead, FederatedRun, and FederatedProject types and the corresponding query fields, but write-routing (hub forwards mutations to owning spoke) is not implemented, and the resolver_federation.go file has no mutation forwarding.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:61 defines FederatedBeads, :124 defines FederatedRuns, and :184 defines FederatedProjects — all read-only fan-out via FederationProvider.FanOut (cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:31). cli/internal/federation/fanout.go:208 provides the FanOutClient.Execute fan-out transport. No mutation resolver in resolver_mutation_*.go calls FanOut or routes writes to an owning spoke; mutations remain hub-local. The ownerSpoke routing metadata fields (nodeID, spokeURL) are populated by existing fan-out code but no write-routing contract enforces "hub forwards, never broadcasts."

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add write-routing helper in cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go: routeToOwner(ctx, ownerSpokeURL, mutationQuery, variables) error that forwards mutations via HTTP to the owning spoke.
  - Wire routeToOwner into the relevant mutation resolvers in resolver_mutation_beads.go or resolver_mutation_runs.go for any mutation that operates on a federated resource.
  - Add schema.graphql comment block documenting the write-routing contract ("hub forwards to ownerSpoke; never broadcasts").
  - Name the ownerSpoke field on FederatedBead/FederatedRun/FederatedProject as the routing discriminator.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery and registration (covered by ddx-fe63969f).
  - Federated mutations on FederatedProject (out of Story 14 scope; read-only for projects).
title: federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields

description:
PROBLEM
First-time per-machine setup has no entry point. The intended model is that `ddx init --global` performs one-time machine setup by installing the default `ddx` plugin into the global layer, creating agent-tier skill links, and writing global convention defaults.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` has no `--global` mode branch.
- cli/cmd/init.go:271-284 only performs eager project-local default plugin installation via `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)`; there is no path that installs the default plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx`.
- There is no writer for `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml`.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a `--global` bool flag to `ddx init` in `cli/cmd/init.go`.
2. When `--global` is set, install the bundled default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx` using the install-global path from the sibling install/update --global bead.
3. Create agent-tier links `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/`, reusing the helper from the sibling install/update --global bead.
4. Write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.

NON-SCOPE
- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).
- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Migrating any `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- sibling install/update --global bead (reuse its install-global path and agent-tier link helper).

PARENT
- ddx-937de9fc.
title: init: add global machine setup for ddx init --global

description:
PROBLEM
The worker still treats `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` as a recoverable no-progress outcome even when the just-created children still point back to the attempted bead. That leaves the same bead getting reclaimed forever while the children are structurally blocked.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` already has no-progress and candidate-skip machinery, but it does not check for the specific back-edge case where a fresh child carries the attempted bead in its deps. Without that guard, the worker continues to re-claim the parent instead of escalating.

PROPOSED FIX
When a run ends with `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` and any freshly-created child still depends on the attempted bead, emit a durable `operator_attention` event with reason `parent_back_edge`, park the bead, and stop re-claiming it. Add a regression named `TestWorkHaltsOnParentBackEdge` that proves the worker exits the loop rather than retrying the parent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the dependency validation commands, doctor repair logic, prompt templates, or lifecycle docs in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_decompose_test.go (if the test fixture needs child deps)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go
title: Halt work on parent_back_edge no-progress loops

acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan|TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim|TestVersionWarnsStaleSourceBinary|TestCheckVersionGate' -count=1` passes.
2. `TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan` proves no bead is claimed and no readiness/run service call occurs when the DDx source tree is ahead of the binary commit.
3. `TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim` proves `ddx try <id>` fails before claim/attempt evidence when the same stale-source condition is present.
4. `ddx work` and `ddx try` error text includes the project root, binary commit, source HEAD, and `make install` recovery instruction.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators can run `ddx work` from the DDx source checkout with an installed binary built from an older commit. That produced live workers still using old readiness/routing behavior even after the local source tree had been fixed. `ddx version` can warn, but `ddx work` and `ddx try` still proceed to claim beads and perform actual builds with stale code.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:603` implements `warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource`, but it is only called from the `version` command path at `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:511`.
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:169` runs the global pre-run version gate, but it checks `.ddx/versions.yaml` semver only and does not block a stale installed binary inside the DDx source repository.
- `cli/cmd/work.go:130` and `cli/cmd/try.go:116` resolve the project root for execution, but neither refuses to build from an older DDx binary when the project root is the DDx source repo.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution preflight for `ddx work` and `ddx try`: when running inside the DDx source repository, and the binary reports a concrete build commit that is an ancestor of the source checkout `HEAD` with one or more commits ahead, fail before queue scan, claim, readiness, or dispatch. The error must name the binary commit, source `HEAD`, project root, and the exact recovery command (`make install` from the source checkout). Keep `ddx version`, `ddx doctor`, `ddx init`, `ddx upgrade`, `help`, and `completion` usable for recovery. Do not make this a warning for worker commands.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go`
- `cli/cmd/work.go`
- `cli/cmd/try.go`
- `cli/cmd/version_gate_test.go`
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go`
- `cli/cmd/try_test.go`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change release semver gating against `.ddx/versions.yaml`.
- Do not auto-run `make install` or mutate the operator's PATH.
- Do not block dev binaries with `Version=dev` or binaries without a known commit.
- Do not inspect workers from other projects; this preflight is scoped to the resolved project root.
title: work: fail fast on stale DDx source checkout binary

acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestWorkerClaimLeaseDoesNotMutateTracker|TestReadyExecutionExcludesFreshSidecarLease|TestReadyExecutionReclaimsStaleSidecarLease' -count=1` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestWorkLoop_ClaimAndHeartbeatDoNotMutateTrackerBeforeOutcome|TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity' -count=1` passes and asserts `.ddx/beads.jsonl` bytes are unchanged from before worker claim through pre-outcome heartbeat ticks.
3. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestCleanupRunStateDoesNotDirtyTrackedRepo|TestInitGitignoreRunStateMigration' -count=1` passes and proves tracked legacy `.ddx/run-state.json` / `.ddx/run-state/*.json` are removed from the index or otherwise cleaned without leaving git status dirty.
4. `rg -n 'work-heartbeat-at|claimed-at|claimed-pid|claimed-machine|claimed-session|claimed-worktree' cli/internal/bead cli/internal/agent` shows no production worker-live-state write to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; compatibility reads for stale legacy rows are allowed only when documented in comments and tests.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` currently dirties tracked files while a worker is merely alive. Operators then see `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and `.ddx/run-state*` changes even when no durable bead outcome has happened, and concurrent watchers can race the same moving state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go:869-925` `ClaimWithOptions` persists live claim state (`status=in_progress`, `owner`, `claimed-at`, `claimed-pid`, `claimed-machine`, `claimed-session`, `claimed-worktree`, `work-heartbeat-at`) into `.ddx/beads.jsonl` before touching the external lease.
- `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:15-24` and `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:64-75` already write an external lease, but the lease only carries `bead_id`, `updated_at`, and `pid`, so operator-facing claim metadata still depends on tracker mutation.
- `cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:100-125` writes live attempt state under `.ddx/run-state/` and `.ddx/run-state.json`; `cli/cmd/init.go:18-31` ignores these paths for new repos, but existing tracked run-state files still produce dirty deletions when cleanup runs.
- `cli/internal/agent/worker_liveness_sidecar_test.go:22-28` currently documents the tracker as the durable claim marker; that contract now conflicts with the desired live-state policy.

PROPOSED FIX
Move worker-owned live claim state fully into sidecar state. Extend the claim lease record to carry owner/session/worktree/machine/started_at/pid/updated_at and make the `ddx work` / `ddx try` worker claim path update only that lease until a terminal durable outcome occurs. `ReadyExecution` must exclude beads with a fresh sidecar lease and include beads whose sidecar lease is absent or stale. Manual tracker lifecycle operations can remain explicit tracker operations, but ordinary worker liveness must not dirty `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.

Also migrate run-state policy so live run-state files are never tracked: add a migration/cleanup path that removes tracked `.ddx/run-state.json` and `.ddx/run-state/*.json` from the git index, keeps the ignore rules, and proves cleanup does not create tracked deletions.

NON-SCOPE

description:
CRITICAL FINDING from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md: code added by recently-landed beads has tests but NO production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. The drain has been closing beads as success while the actual fix code sits unwired. This explains why operational pain (worker auto-exit, lock contention) kept recurring even after fixes 'landed.'

Affected functions (per audit):
- escalation.Ladder.Next (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go) — added by S10 ladder work, no caller
- EvaluateRetryPolicy (cli/internal/agent/retry_policy.go) — added by recent retry work, no caller
- RunWithLockBackoff (cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go or similar) — added by ddx-da11a34a lock contention, no caller
- DispatchPostAttempt — added by ddx-aede917d or sibling, no caller

For each: decide wire vs delete based on whether the BEAD that added it is still relevant (its acceptance criteria still describes desired behavior) OR whether subsequent design changes have made it obsolete.

Recommended decision: **WIRE all four**. The beads that added them are recently-landed and the acceptance criteria are still relevant. The unwired state is a process failure (the implementer wrote and tested the code but didn't replace the old code path). Wiring is the right fix; delete only if the underlying behavior is genuinely obsolete (none of these are).

After wiring, verify with: drain runs and observes the new code paths firing in execution evidence. Spot-check 3-5 beads' .ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json for evidence of escalation/retry/triage events.
notes: superseded by REACH-BACKFILL (ddx-83440482); the 4 unwired functions (escalation.Ladder.Next, EvaluateRetryPolicy, RunWithLockBackoff, DispatchPostAttempt) surfaced as initial production-reachability violations and are absorbed into child bead ddx-83b8994f (internal/agent backfill).

3. The final implementation leaves execution-ready beads to ddx work while making human-required queue intervention visible to interactive agents.
4. The regression suite includes TestDDxAgentModeGuidance_DocContract, TestDDxSkillQueueStewardRouting, TestExecuteBeadInstructionsContainsBeadExecutionMode, TestAgentRunRuntimePropagatesDDXMode, TestWorkFocusReportsInterventionQueue, and TestDDxWorkOnce_BeadExecutionIgnoresQueueStewardDefault.

description:
PROBLEM
Interactive agents opened inside a DDx project can treat broad DDx prompts like "what's next", "what's blocking the queue", or "do work" as permission to start code edits. That competes with ddx work, which is supposed to drain execution-ready beads, and leaves no crisp default for human-required queue intervention, planning, and worker-capacity decisions.

ROOT CAUSE
AGENTS.md:1-18 enters bead policy immediately without declaring a default interactive queue-steward posture. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:18-29 defines ddx run/try/work execution layers but not the interactive-agent boundary around them. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:28-32 routes phrases like "do work" and "what's on the queue" through the ddx skill without distinguishing queue stewardship from bead execution. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" to ddx bead ready, which exposes worker-ready beads rather than human-required intervention.

PROPOSED FIX
Define explicit DDx agent modes and implement the guardrails around them: queue_steward for broad interactive sessions, bead_execution for ddx work/try/execute-bead workers, direct_user_implementation for explicit human requests to edit code/docs, and review for read-only review asks. Add a focused operator command surface for intervention work and worker-capacity suggestions, while preserving ddx work as the owner of execution-ready implementation beads.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing ddx work's existing execution loop.
- Building a full TUI or chat UI.
- Rewriting bead tracker storage by hand.
- Changing merge-policy or tracker audit semantics.

CHILD QUEUE
- Spec guidance updates define the mode taxonomy and precedence.
- DDx skill/AGENTS guidance makes queue stewardship the broad interactive default.
- Agent execution plumbing marks ddx work/try prompts with bead_execution mode.
- A command surface exposes human-required intervention and worker-capacity advice.
- Regression tests and prompt evals prove interactive guidance does not suppress ddx work execution.
title: agent-modes: define queue-steward execution boundary

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 migration-tooling methods used by `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and related admin commands: `MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata`. These are admin/one-shot tooling, not steady-state Backend operations. After the BL bead moves concrete `*Store` to `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/`, `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` cannot reach these methods. They need an exposure path that doesn't violate the boundary lockdown.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 migration methods. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` imports `cli/internal/bead` and calls them on `*Store` concretely. Per TD-027 §11.5 + Migration row in the mutation rules table, these are admin tools not on the `Backend` interface. After BL, the only public surface in `cli/internal/bead/` is what's exported there — concrete `*Store` won't be reachable.

# PROPOSED FIX

Per the converged design recommendation (separate factory rather than `BeadMigration` sub-interface on Backend, to avoid forcing every backend to stub source-specific migrations):

1. Create `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` with a `Migrator` interface and `NewMigrator` factory:
   ```go
   type Migrator interface {
       MigrateLifecycle(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateLifecycleDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       MigrateFromHelix(ctx context.Context, src string) error
       MigrateToAxon(ctx context.Context, opts MigrateToAxonOptions) error  // deprecated; replaced by ddx-IMP
       MigrateDryRun(ctx context.Context, opts MigrationOptions) (MigrationResult, error)
       DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired(ctx context.Context) (bool, MigrationReport, error)
       ReconcileLifecycleMetadata(ctx context.Context) (ReconcileReport, error)
   }

   func NewMigrator(opts MigratorOptions) (Migrator, error)
   ```
   The concrete implementation lives in `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` after BL; the public factory returns a `Migrator` interface.

2. Move the 7 methods' bodies from `*Store` (in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `migrate.go`) onto an unexported `*storeMigrator` type that holds a `*Store` reference and implements `Migrator`. The `NewMigrator` factory returns this.

3. Update `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` and any related admin commands:
   ```go
   mig, err := bead.NewMigrator(bead.MigratorOptions{Dir: workspace})
   if err != nil { ... }
   mig.MigrateLifecycle(ctx, opts)
   ```

4. Remove `*Store.MigrateLifecycle`, `MigrateLifecycleDryRun`, `MigrateFromHelix`, `MigrateToAxon`, `MigrateDryRun`, `DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired`, `ReconcileLifecycleMetadata` from the `*Store` public method set. Logic moves to the `*storeMigrator` type.

5. `MigrateToAxon` retained as a deprecated method on `Migrator` that returns `ErrDeprecated` with a pointer to the IMP bead's new importer. Final removal happens after IMP lands.

# NON-SCOPE
- The new JSONL→Postgres importer (replaces `MigrateToAxon`'s JSONL-writing behavior) — separate IMP bead.
- Schema-versioning migration ladder framework — deferred until first breaking change is proposed.
- Other concerns — separate beads.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/migrator.go` declares the `Migrator` interface (7 methods, ctx-aware) and `NewMigrator(opts) (Migrator, error)` factory. Compile-time assertion: `var _ Migrator = (*storeMigrator)(nil)`.
2. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 migration methods directly. Verified by `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (MigrateLifecycle|MigrateLifecycleDryRun|MigrateFromHelix|MigrateToAxon|MigrateDryRun|DetectLifecycleMigrationRequired|ReconcileLifecycleMetadata)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returning nothing.
3. `cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go` constructs `Migrator` via `bead.NewMigrator(...)` and invokes operations. Holds `Migrator` interface, not `*storeMigrator` concrete.
4. End-to-end CLI invocation works: `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --dry-run`, `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle --apply` produce identical output to today. Test: `TestMigrator_LifecycleMigration_RoundTrip`, `TestMigrator_FromHelix_PreservesAllFields`.
5. `MigrateToAxon` on the Migrator returns `ErrDeprecated` with message pointing at the new importer (will be implemented in IMP). Test: `TestMigrator_MigrateToAxon_ReturnsDeprecated`.
6. Existing migration tests in `cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go` continue to pass against the new Migrator-mediated path.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:migration, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: ddx-c6317784.
title: MIG: Migration tooling factory — Migrator interface + NewMigrator + remove 7 *Store migration methods

acceptance: After `ddx install <name> --force --local <path>` succeeds, `ddx installed` reports STATUS=ok with VERSION matching the source's plugin.json `version` field and INSTALLED set to today's date.
description:
## Summary

Observed against ddx v0.6.2-alpha103-12-ge6379ebc5 on 2026-05-18.

After `ddx install helix --force --local /home/erik/Projects/helix` succeeds (output: 'Linked .ddx/plugins/helix -> /home/erik/Projects/helix; Installed helix from local path; recorded plugin pin unchanged'), `ddx installed` reports:

```
NAME   VERSION  TYPE      STATUS  INSTALLED
helix  0.3.3    workflow  BROKEN  2026-05-12
```

Three things are wrong:
1. STATUS=BROKEN despite the install reporting success.
2. VERSION=0.3.3 despite the linked source's plugin.json declaring 0.4.1.
3. INSTALLED=2026-05-12 (date of the prior successful install) — not updated by today's --local install.

The 'recorded plugin pin unchanged' message in the install output is the smoking gun: DDx doesn't update the version pin when installing from --local. It leaves the registry at whatever the last registry install pinned, even though the on-disk plugin is now at a different version.

## Expected behavior

After a successful `ddx install --local`, `ddx installed` should:
- Report STATUS=ok (not BROKEN) if the install succeeded.
- Report VERSION matching the plugin.json of the linked source.
- Report INSTALLED date of today (the actual install).

## Workaround

None known. The metadata is misleading but the file content is correct, so runtime works.
title: ddx installed reports BROKEN status after a successful --local install that creates a symlink-style plugin

2. `TestWorkerFailurePathsReleaseClaimAtomically` injects heartbeat-removal failure and proves the open bead is immediately visible to `ReadyExecution` or otherwise not hidden by a fresh sidecar lease.
3. Existing `TestStoreReleaseClearsClaimAtomically`, `TestReadyExecutionExcludesFreshSidecarLease`, and `TestReadyExecutionReclaimsStaleSidecarLease` still pass.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkerFailurePathsReleaseClaimAtomically|TestStoreReleaseClearsClaimAtomically|TestReadyExecutionExcludesFreshSidecarLease|TestReadyExecutionReclaimsStaleSidecarLease'` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Some worker failure paths still call `Store.Unclaim`, which updates the bead row and then removes the claim heartbeat outside the same critical section. If heartbeat removal fails, `ReadyExecution` can keep excluding an open bead until the sidecar lease TTL expires, reducing multi-worker throughput and making failures look like queue starvation.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1081-1100 implements `Unclaim` as row update followed by separate heartbeat removal.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1103-1130 implements `Release`, which atomically updates the row and clears the heartbeat under one lock.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2365-2368 still uses `w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID)` on a worker refresh failure path; other worker paths should be audited for the same pattern.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit worker-owned failure/unclaim paths and replace them with `Release` where the current worker owns the claim.
- Add a fault-injection test that makes heartbeat removal fail and proves the bead is not left with a fresh sidecar lease after a worker failure path releases it.
- Preserve CLI/operator `ddx bead update --unclaim` semantics where direct `Unclaim` is still appropriate.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change claim ownership rules or sidecar lease TTLs in this bead.
- Do not close beads on failure; failed attempts must remain open/unclaimed unless existing outcome policy says otherwise.
title: agent: release worker claims atomically on failure paths

description:
PROBLEM
  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. There is no injection seam, so tests cannot stub the ticker to verify heartbeat cadence or cancellation without real wall-clock delays.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extract to withHeartbeat(ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store interface{ Heartbeat(string) error }, fn func() (ExecuteBeadReport, error)) error in a new file cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat.go.
  - withHeartbeat starts the internal goroutine + WaitGroup, calls fn(), cancels the ticker goroutine on fn return, and returns fn's result.
  - Drain loop body (execute_bead_loop.go:732) replaces the inline goroutine block with a single withHeartbeat call.
  - Add TestWithHeartbeat_TickerFiredBeforeReturn and TestWithHeartbeat_CancelPropagates to cli/internal/agent/work/heartbeat_test.go using a stub ticker channel injection.

NON-SCOPE
  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).
  - Changes to heartbeatInterval policy or the Store.Heartbeat call itself.
  - Any behavior change to the drain loop; this is a pure extraction.

INTERSECTIONS
- P2: heartbeat timing and lifecycle belong in one helper, not inline with candidate execution.
- P4: one bead heartbeat bug must not leak into the rest of the drain loop.
- P5: extracted heartbeat behavior supports always-visible worker state.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- The orchestrator's 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning, when emitted alongside the parent-back-edge state, is upgraded from WARN to ERROR with the offending bead ID logged. Test: dispatch_logs_error_on_root_mutation_with_back_edge.
- Lifecycle schema documents that 'parent' and 'deps' MUST be disjoint and that 'deps' MUST be acyclic with the parent-tree. docs/ddx/lifecycle-rules.md (or equivalent) updated; ADR file if no governing artifact already exists.
- Existing valid graphs continue to pass 'ddx bead doctor' without --fix and without regression — regression test against a pre-existing tracker snapshot.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 3

description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:739 prepareDockerAttemptHome() copies host creds (~/.codex/auth.json, ~/.codex/config.toml, ~/.claude/.credentials.json) into ws.DockerHome under ws.DockerRun, which only reach the container via the /ddx-runtime HOST bind-mount (:636). On a de-nested/remote daemon that bind is invisible, so the agent inside the container has no credentials.

PROPOSED FIX
Deliver credentials without a host bind: prefer key-based env injection (extend dockerCredentialEnv() to pass e.g. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY when present), and for file-based creds docker cp the prepared cred files into the container HOME after docker create / before start. Remove reliance on the /ddx-runtime bind for credential delivery.

IN-SCOPE
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go (prepareDockerAttemptHome, dockerCredentialEnv, DockerCloneAttemptBackend.Run cred step)
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_test.go

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- caches/runtime volumes (sibling bead)
- workspace/ddx binary (keystone bead)

ACCEPTANCE
1. Update TestPrepareDockerAttemptHomeCopiesMinimalAuth (attempt_backend_test.go:154) to reflect cp/env delivery rather than bind reliance.
2. Add TestDockerCredentialDelivery_NoRuntimeBind asserting creds are delivered via env and/or docker cp targets (no dependence on a /ddx-runtime host bind).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: docker backend: inject agent credentials via env/docker cp instead of the runtime-home bind-mount

6. Any actual violations found in run/work/try paths are fixed or annotated with routinglint:legacy-rejection reason="...".
7. cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/routinglint/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  The run/try/work dispatch paths must not set Profile, Harness, or Model directly on ResolvedConfig or Runtime structs — those are durable knobs that belong in config.LoadAndResolve. Without a mechanically-enforced lint rule, future edits can silently reintroduce the anti-pattern and bypass the fizeau routing contract.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go (package routinglint) currently forbids retired compensating-routing identifiers (ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef, etc.) and flag literals (--escalate, profile_ladders). It does not yet forbid direct field assignment of Profile/Harness/Model on Runtime structs inside run/try/work command code paths. The SD-024 runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go:41) forbids durable-knob fields on *Runtime structs generically, but does not name a per-command allowlist or produce a test that verifies the run/work/try paths specifically. No TestRoutingLint* test exercises this invariant.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Extend routinglint (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go) to flag direct Profile/Harness/Model field assignment on types matching *Runtime in cli/cmd/ run/work/try command files, or add a new rule in runtimelint covering this.
  - Alternatively, add a targeted integration test TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly that runs the analyzer against the live tree and asserts zero diagnostics for the assignment pattern.
  - Fix any existing violations: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1893-1897 passes overrides.Profile/Harness/Model through CLIOverrides to config.LoadAndResolve — verify this is compliant (CLIOverrides is not a Runtime struct).

NON-SCOPE
  - Changes to agent_run or agent_run_overrides command behavior.
  - Changes to the fizeau or ladder routing logic.
  - Lint rules for non-routing fields on Runtime structs (covered by runtimelint).

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-cc298105 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store read surface to be typed and ctx-first, but the read methods still accept untyped variadic arguments. This keeps the local bead package API ambiguous and makes the ddx-464feba2 caller sweep too large to execute as one bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:80-84 declares BeadReader with ReadAll(args ...any), ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), and Get(args ...any).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:181-240 exposes Store.ReadAll(args ...any) and Store.ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), using storeCallContext from cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:599-625 exposes Store.Get(args ...any), using argument scanning for the bead ID.
- cli/internal/bead/archive.go:158-174 exposes Store.GetWithArchive(id string) and currently supplies context.Background() internally instead of accepting caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the read-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), Get(ctx context.Context, id string), and GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadReader in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only read-method callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests that prove canceled contexts are honored and GetWithArchive forwards the supplied context instead of creating a background context.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and mutation signature children.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStore(ReadAll|ReadAllFiltered|Get|GetWithArchive)_' passes.
- cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

Child 2 AC1 covers TestStoreGetHonorsCanceledContext and TestStoreGetUsesTypedIDArgument with cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStoreGet_'. Child 3 AC1 covers TestStoreGetWithArchiveHonorsCanceledContext and TestStoreGetWithArchiveForwardsCallerContext with cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStoreGetWithArchive_'."},{"parent_ac":"2. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/... passes.","coverage":"Child 1 AC2, Child 2 AC2, and Child 3 AC2 each require cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/... after their scoped callsite migrations."},{"parent_ac":"3. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","coverage":"Child 1 AC3, Child 2 AC3, and Child 3 AC3 each require lefthook run pre-commit."}],"child_ids":["ddx-ec9fef0a","ddx-d93e463a","ddx-1cf1edb2"],"rationale":"Split by read API family so each child has a narrow compile boundary and can be executed independently: list reads, single-bead Get, and archive-aware GetWithArchive.
