acceptance:
1. `TestExecutorLinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessOnParentSIGKILL` starts an execute-bead executor path against a fake harness binary that forks a long-lived grandchild, SIGKILLs the parent worker process rather than sending graceful SIGTERM, and asserts the harness child and grandchild are gone within a bounded grace instead of becoming PID-1 orphans.
2. `TestCmdSetProcessGroupLinuxSetsPdeathsigAndSetpgid` verifies the Linux executor command configuration includes both process-group isolation and a parent-death signal.
3. `TestExecutorStartParentDeathSignalThreadGuard` or an equivalent focused test/rationale covers the `Pdeathsig` parent-thread caveat using `runtime.LockOSThread` or a parent-PID guard.
4. `TestExecutorGracefulSignalStillTerminatesHarnessTree` proves existing SIGINT/SIGTERM or context-cancellation cleanup still terminates in-flight harness children.
5. Non-Linux behavior is covered by `TestExecutorParentDeathSignalNonLinuxFallbackDocumented` or build-tagged documentation explaining the fallback and why `Pdeathsig` is unavailable.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
When a `ddx work` orchestrator dies abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM, or supervising-process loss) while an execute-bead attempt is running, the spawned harness child can reparent to PID 1 and continue indefinitely. Observed 2026-05-27: a `claude --print` execute-bead child remained orphaned with PPID 1 for ~9h53m after its `ddx work` parent exited.

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 spawns the harness via `exec.Command(binary, args...)` and then calls `cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd)`.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 sets only `SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}`. There is no Linux `Pdeathsig`, so the kernel does not signal the harness when the parent dies abnormally.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 kills the process group only from graceful executor paths.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 uses `signal.NotifyContext` for os.Interrupt and SIGTERM only. SIGKILL and crashes cannot be caught, so executor cleanup does not run.

PROPOSED FIX
On Linux, set `SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig` to SIGKILL or SIGTERM for execute-bead harness commands in `cmdSetProcessGroup`. Handle the Go runtime thread-migration caveat with `runtime.LockOSThread` around process start or an equivalent parent-PID guard so the parent-death signal is attached to the intended parent thread. Preserve process-group setup so existing graceful cancellation and timeout cleanup still kill the whole harness tree. Document the macOS/Windows fallback because `Pdeathsig` is Linux-only.

NON-SCOPE
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child bead is only the DDx orchestrator-to-harness-child boundary.
- Startup orphan reaping is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.
- docker-clone backend cleanup is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.

CROSS-REF
- fizeau-50b6f055: fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup.
- ddx-8f2e0ebf: lease release / operator-attention.
title: fix executor: enforce parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness children on Linux

2. `make install` installs a DDx binary whose `ddx --version` output includes the current commit and does not include `-dirty`.

description:
PROBLEM
Running `make install` from a clean DDx checkout builds and installs a binary stamped with `-dirty` even when no source edits were pending. During the 2026-05-15 monitor restart, `make install` reported `v0.6.2-alpha81-63-gffe356c1b-dirty`, and `git status --short` showed only `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` modified.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/Makefile:24-31` runs `bun run build`, which empties `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/`, then recreates `internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` using `touch`. The tracked `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` currently contains a newline byte, but `touch` recreates an empty file after Vite removes the directory contents. That one-byte mismatch makes the source checkout dirty before `go build` computes `git describe --dirty` in `cli/Makefile:4-6`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Normalize the tracked placeholder `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep` to the same empty-file content produced by the build target, or otherwise make `frontend-build` recreate the exact tracked bytes.
- Verify `make install` from a clean checkout leaves no tracked `.gitkeep` diff and installs a DDx binary whose version string does not end in `-dirty`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not track generated frontend build output under `cli/internal/server/frontend/build/`.
- Do not change the frontend bundle, Vite config, or go:embed target.
- Do not bypass the stale-binary guard in `ddx work`.

DEPS
No deps. This is a build hygiene fix discovered while restarting the DDx watcher.
title: build: keep frontend placeholder stable during install

description:
PROBLEM
Pre-merge checks turn an attempt-context cancellation or an infrastructure failure into a quality BLOCK, stranding good work as preserved-not-landed. Observed signature: pre-merge check "production-reachability" failed to start: context canceled -> Blocked. cli/internal/checks/runner.go runOne classifies failed-to-start, ctx-cancel, AND a check that ran-but-crashed / wrote no result all as StatusError; cli/internal/agent/checks_premerge.go (~143) maps StatusError->Blocked=true. So a cancelled attempt (or a local-box check that cannot start) blocks landing of changes the agent correctly produced.

PROPOSED FIX (fail-open ONLY on unconfirmed infra; a crashed check still blocks)
1. Add checks.StatusInfra to cli/internal/checks/protocol.go.
2. In runner.go runOne: ONLY ctx.Err()!=nil (cancellation) and the failed-to-start branch (the non-ExitError case where the command could not launch) return StatusInfra. A check that DID start but exits non-zero, or wrote no result file, STAYS StatusError (indistinguishable from a real failure — a crashing security/secrets check must not silently land bad code). A check declares "inconclusive" only by writing a result file with explicit StatusInfra, never by crashing.
3. In checks_premerge.go: set Blocked on StatusBlock OR StatusError; StatusInfra is NON-gating (surface it as an advisory/report, do not preserve on it).

NON-SCOPE: do not weaken land-side preserve gates (execute_bead_land.go large-deletion/syntax-sanity/post-land) — those stay fail-closed.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestRunOne_ContextCancelled_Infra: a check run under an already-cancelled ctx returns StatusInfra (not StatusError/StatusBlock).
2. TestRunOne_FailedToStart_Infra: a check whose command cannot launch returns StatusInfra.
3. TestRunOne_StartedNonZero_StillError and TestRunOne_NoResultFile_StillError: a check that started but exits non-zero / wrote no result stays StatusError (gating).
4. TestRunPreMergeChecks_InfraDoesNotBlock: a StatusInfra result does not set Blocked; a real StatusBlock still does.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./internal/agent/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: checks: cancellation/failed-to-start are StatusInfra (non-gating); crashed check still blocks (D2)

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 5 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

description:
PROBLEM
The pre-dispatch checkpoint path is still committing transient runtime files. After `ddx-b6fea19a` landed and `.ddx/run-state*` had been removed from the index, the next `ddx work --watch` cycle created checkpoint commit `344d95aac` and reintroduced `.ddx/run-state.json`, `.ddx/run-state/20260515T110023-a0db107f.json`, and `.ddx/executions/20260515T120840-2c0aa694/embedded/git-bin/git` into git history. That puts live worker state and embedded private git-isolation artifacts back into `git status` during normal monitoring.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:713-775` asks `git status --ignored=matching --untracked-files=all` for checkpoint candidates. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:778-793` explicitly allows `.ddx/executions/`, `.ddx/run-state/`, and `.ddx/run-state.json`, so ignored runtime files are eligible for checkpoint commits. Tests currently encode that bad contract: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go:47-66`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go:115-129`, and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go:136-156` assert `.ddx/run-state.json` is committed. The runtime file is live scratch, while durable evidence should exclude `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/**` private harness/git isolation material.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove `.ddx/run-state.json` and `.ddx/run-state/**` from the pre-dispatch checkpoint allowlist. Keep run-state observable on disk and ignored by git. Add an explicit recursive exclusion for `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/**` in checkpoint candidate filtering. Update checkpoint tests so tracker/evidence/metrics are still checkpointed, but run-state and embedded private files are not staged or committed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove durable `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`, `usage.json`, or `result.json` evidence. Do not change worker scheduling or liveness sidecar semantics. Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
title: execute-bead: stop checkpointing live run-state and embedded private files

acceptance:
1. Add `TestExecuteBeadLandingCommitsFinalResultArtifact` (or equivalent named regression) proving a successful land rewrites `result.json` to final orchestrator fields and leaves no unstaged or staged diff for `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/result.json` in the project root.
2. The same test must assert the final committed `result.json` contains `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, `outcome: "merged"`, and `orchestrator_status: "success"`.
3. Add or extend an existing evidence/cleanup test proving the final-result commit does not stage embedded session logs or `.ddx/run-state/**`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestExecuteBeadLandingCommitsFinalResultArtifact|Test.*FinalResult.*RunState|Test.*FinalResult.*Embedded" -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Successful `ddx work` landings still leave `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/result.json` modified in the operator checkout after the worker exits or self-replaces. This happened twice during the current monitor run: `ddx-8a0aea8e` left `.ddx/executions/20260515T154605-7c45a217/result.json` dirty with `outcome` changing from `task_succeeded` to `merged`, and `ddx-7e422ff6` left `.ddx/executions/20260515T161644-6d9781bd/result.json` dirty the same way. In both cases the operator had to create an extra `chore: finalize execution result ...` commit manually. That violates the no-dirty-root invariant for normal worker success.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1582-1588` documents that the worker writes a preliminary `result.json` before committing so the implementation commit message can be sourced from tracked artifact data. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:850-868` commits the evidence bundle during landing, but that commit happens before the orchestrator result rewrite. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2605-2626` then rewrites `result.json` in the project root with final fields such as `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, `outcome=merged`, and `orchestrator_status`. No follow-up staging/commit path captures that final rewrite, so the tracked `result.json` remains dirty after otherwise successful landings.

PROPOSED FIX
After `WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact` rewrites a tracked execution `result.json` in the project root, stage and commit just that final result artifact (or fold it into the evidence commit by moving the final rewrite before evidence commit) using the same git/index-lock recovery path as other DDx-managed audit commits. The final branch history must contain the final orchestrator result fields and `git status --short` must be clean for that attempt.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` only if final-result timing belongs in landing
- Focused execute-bead landing tests under `cli/internal/agent/`

NON-SCOPE
- Do not track embedded session logs or `.ddx/run-state/**`.
- Do not change the result JSON schema.
- Do not remove the preliminary result write used for commit-message provenance unless the replacement still preserves that contract.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` or existing evidence files.
title: work: auto-commit final execution result rewrite

Asserted by `TestReadinessVerdict_AcceptsBoolStringOrNull` (add if absent).
2. `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult` decodes a payload whose `readiness_checks[].verdict` is `true`/`false` without the `cannot unmarshal bool` error and yields the expected failed-reason set. description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder rejects the readiness payload when a readiness_checks entry's `verdict` is a JSON bool, emitting `decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string`. Under warn-only policy this skips every bead, idling a full fizeau queue (23 ready beads, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC). The identical error fingerprint (15155a1c...) across distinct bead_ids confirms a producer/consumer schema mismatch, not a bead defect.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go` — `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` (struct at :75) bound `verdict` to a plain string, so `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResultWithMode` (:688) and `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult` (:681) fail when the readiness producer emits `verdict:true`. A verdict may legitimately be a JSON bool, a JSON string (e.g. "fail"/"pass"/"ready"), or be omitted.

PROPOSED FIX
Give the field a dedicated `readinessVerdict` type (:91) with a custom UnmarshalJSON that accepts a JSON bool (coerced to a documented canonical lowercased string), a JSON string (trimmed), and JSON null/absent (empty), and rejects any other JSON kind with a descriptive error. Downstream comparisons in `failedReadinessReasons` (:823) and `readinessCheckEvidence` (:848) operate on the canonical lowercased string.

NON-SCOPE
Does NOT add the shared JSON schema or CI drift gate (covered by the schema-lock child). Does NOT change the worker same-fingerprint escalation (escalation child), the rolling claim-success-rate signal (claim-rate child), or README docs (docs child). Preserves the FEAT-010 / ADR-023 readiness vocabulary and cross-refs ddx-57c40485, ddx-8f2e0ebf, ddx-9714eaac.
title: pre-claim intake: decode readiness_checks[].verdict as JSON bool, string, or absent

acceptance:
1. Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
2. 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.
3. ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.
4. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.
- ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. `ddx init --global --silent` exits 0 and creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ with the bundled library content.
2. `ddx init --global` is idempotent: running twice does not error and does not destroy local-only files under global/.
3. TestInitGlobal_PopulatesXDGGlobalDir asserts the directory structure post-install matches what convention mode expects.
4. TestInitGlobal_IsIdempotent asserts a second run does not error and re-syncs the plugin content.
5. TestConventionMode_ReadsGlobalSkillsBeforeHardcoded asserts that with --global installed, convention mode picks up the global plugin instead of the hardcoded one.
6. ddx doctor's "Global Install" check exists and reports the install path + status.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/config/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Convention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:
   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).
   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).
   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.

2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree > global > hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.

3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check "Global Install" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.

4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/<name>/). Both coexist.

NON-SCOPE
- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).
- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).
- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).

DEPS
ddx-564ea1cf (E1) — resolver must exist.
ddx-ad338ec3 (E2) — convention mode reads from this path.
notes: Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.
title: config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas to XDG global

description:
PROBLEM
Story 16.3 needs an audit event when raw transcript content is accessed, but the current run resolver only records `run_detail_view` on canonical detail loads. There is no dedicated raw-transcript access event or test coverage for that behavior.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go:100-145 appends `run_detail_view` from `recordRunDetailView`, and that hook is only called from `Run`; prompt/response/tool/evidence access does not emit a distinct raw-transcript event.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs_test.go:332-357 covers the existing `run_detail_view` behavior, but there is no `TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed` coverage.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a dedicated raw-transcript audit event path in the runs GraphQL/server layer.
- Emit the event when prompt/response/transcript data is accessed, not on tab switches or unrelated navigation.
- Add `TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed` covering the new event and its body fields.

NON-SCOPE
- UI layout changes.
- Full Playwright stack coverage.
- Spec prose changes beyond the minimum needed to keep event naming consistent.

ACCEPTANCE
1. `TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed` exists and verifies the event emission trigger and body schema.
2. The server code emits the raw-transcript event from the run-detail access path that actually reads prompt/response/transcript content.
3. Existing `run_detail_view` coverage remains green.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/...` green.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
title: server: add raw-transcript audit event for run-detail access

description:
PROBLEM
Adding cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ (from ddx-1a9cc01f) creates a new production entry package. The REACH-PROTO reachability checker (ddx-a946c744) uses a list of entry roots to determine which code is reachable. Without updating that root list, the new executeloop package will be incorrectly flagged as unreachable dead code.

ROOT CAUSE
- The REACH-PROTO tool (ddx-a946c744) maintains an explicit list of entry packages in its root configuration. Currently cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ does not exist; once ddx-1a9cc01f lands, it will be a production package reachable from cli/cmd/ but the reachability root list will not reflect this until this bead updates it.
- Parent note: ddx-29058e2a §REACHABILITY-PROTO explicitly calls out that REACH-PROTO must know about cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ as an entry root.

PROPOSED FIX
- After ddx-1a9cc01f lands: locate the REACH-PROTO root configuration (likely under cli/internal/reachability/ or in the REACH-PROTO tool referenced by ddx-a946c744).
- Add cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ as an entry root.
- Verify: reachability checker reports executeloop package as reachable (not dead code).

NON-SCOPE
- Reachability analysis of other packages added by later children (each child handles its own roots).
- Changes to the executeloop package itself.
title: agent: update reachability roots for ExecuteLoopSpec package

description:
PROBLEM
The fixture-backed app e2e suite currently fails in the project documents and beads flows. description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch on a full queue can sit at loop.idle indefinitely while claiming nothing, with no top-level operator-visible signal — silent idle masquerading as healthy (fizeau, 2026-05-18: 23 ready beads, 0 claims in 30 minutes, empty commit log).

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` (:86-222) and `cli/cmd/work.go` (:88-101) — the loop did not compute or surface a rolling claim-success rate, so there was no operator-visible warning when claim attempts fail consistently over a window.

PROPOSED FIX
Track claim attempts and successes over a configurable rolling window (`ClaimSuccessRateWindow`, default 10) and emit an operator-visible stderr warning when the rate over a full window is at or below a configurable threshold (`ClaimSuccessRateThreshold`, default 0.0). Expose `--claim-rate-window` and `--claim-rate-threshold` flags on `ddx work`.

NON-SCOPE
Does NOT cover the decoder (decoder child), the schema gate (schema child), or the same-fingerprint escalation (escalation child — a distinct trigger), or README docs (docs child).
title: ddx work --watch: warn when rolling claim-success rate falls below threshold

acceptance:
1. TestWorkWatch_ReexecsOnNewerInstalledBinaryBetweenBeads: with --watch, after a bead completes, if the installed binary commit differs from the running build commit, the worker re-execs (via an injectable re-exec seam) preserving argv; the next bead runs on the new binary.
2. TestWorkWatch_DoesNotReexecMidBead: re-exec happens only at the between-bead boundary, never while a bead executes.
3. TestWorkWatch_NoReexecWhenBinaryUnchanged: no re-exec when the installed binary matches the running build commit.
4. A --self-refresh flag (default on for --watch) gates the behavior; --self-refresh=false disables it.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestWorkWatch_.*Reexec|SelfRefresh' -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM: `ddx work --watch` runs the binary it was launched with for its ENTIRE lifetime. When newer code is installed (the factory merges fixes + `make install`), the worker keeps executing STALE logic until manually killed+restarted. It only WARNS ("installed ddx is built from X; source HEAD is Y (N commits ahead); run make install") — no action. Observed live: a 2-worker drain ran ~75 commits behind, missing ALREADY-LANDED concurrency fixes (ddx-33530e68 post-land-outside-lock, ddx-0b659710 atomic-claim-release, ddx-274943fc worktree-leak) and repeatedly colliding on the durable-audit commit — the very fixes were sitting in source but not in the running binary. Operators must manually cycle workers. Operator expectation: "the workers are supposed to know how to cycle automatically between tasks." (A stopgap supervisor that runs `ddx work --once` + `make install` between cycles is in place; this bead is the durable native fix.)

ROOT CAUSE: the --watch loop loads its binary once at startup; there is no between-task self-refresh / re-exec. The staleness warning (grep "installed ddx is built from" / "source HEAD") is informational only.

PROPOSED FIX: At a SAFE boundary in the --watch loop — after a bead completes/unclaims and BEFORE claiming the next — detect whether the on-disk installed binary differs from the running process's build commit (compare the embedded build commit vs the installed binary's reported commit, or hash/stat the executable resolved via os.Executable/PATH). If newer, re-exec the process in place (syscall.Exec) on the fresh binary, preserving argv/flags (--watch, --min-power, etc.) so the next bead runs on the latest code. NEVER re-exec mid-bead. Gate behind --self-refresh (default ON for --watch). This delivers "workers cycle automatically between tasks."

NON-SCOPE: triggering `make install`/the build from the worker (separate concern — a supervisor/build pipeline produces the binary; this bead re-execs onto it); the durable-audit collision classifier (ddx-83361480/e8bf0695/2965925e).
title: ddx work --watch: self-cycle between tasks — re-exec on a newer installed binary so workers never run stale code

description:
PROBLEM: land_post_land_finalize held the main-git lock up to 4m37s (retries=12) under 2 workers, blocking the sibling that entire time (also observed: land_ref_update hold=20-24s, durable_audit hold=19s). 4m37s is 9.2x the 30s TP-021 tracker cap.

ROOT CAUSE: withMainGitLock("land_post_land_finalize", ...) at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1404 wraps landEvidence (execute_bead_land.go:1417), which does  of the whole per-attempt evidence directory (StageDir, execute_bead_land.go:756-768) + On branch main
Your branch is ahead of 'origin/main' by 1 commit.
  (use "git push" to publish your local commits)

nothing to commit, working tree clean (CommitStaged, :771-789) INSIDE the lock. Large evidence dirs (multi-GB per ddx-274943fc) on a large repo make this dominate the hold. ddx-33530e68 moved post-land COMMANDS out of the lock (runPostLandCommand now unlocked at :1375); ddx-3d5678fc moved attempt PREP out of pre_dispatch_commits; NEITHER touched the evidence add+commit — it is the third slow op still locked.

PROPOSED FIX: Perform the evidence  (and evidence working-tree writes) in the private finalization worktree (landingFinalizationWorktree, execute_bead_land.go:1052) OUTSIDE the lock; reacquire withMainGitLock only for the short ref-advancing publish (the evidence commit's ref update / result.NewTip). Preserve preserveAfterEvidenceFailure semantics (execute_bead_land.go:1467) by briefly reacquiring the lock for the preserve/revert ref writes on failure.

NON-SCOPE: the checkout-sync lock window (sibling bead L1); cap enforcement (sibling bead); worktree cleanup (ddx-274943fc).
title: agent: move evidence directory staging+commit out of the land_post_land_finalize main-git lock

2. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry): with <name> present in BOTH ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name> and GlobalPluginsDir()/<name>, ResolvePluginDir returns the project dir and scope 'project'.
3. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry): with the project copy absent and the global copy present, ResolvePluginDir returns the global dir and scope 'global'.

description:
PROBLEM (foundation child of ddx-937de9fc)
Plugins resolve only to project-local paths today. internal/registry has no concept of a machine-global plugin tree (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>) nor a precedence order for runtime lookup. This child lands the path resolver and precedence that the --global install/update/init/doctor children all build on.

ROOT CAUSE
- No resolver maps (projectRoot, name) -> a concrete plugin dir with precedence.
- cli/internal/registry/registry.go exposes BuiltinRegistry/Find only; there is no GlobalPluginsDir / ResolvePluginDir.
- The XDG data-home helper xdgDataHome() in cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:89 is unexported; project root comes from ddxroot.Path() (ddxroot.go:75).
- cli/internal/registry/installer.go rejects any home-rooted (~) install target at installer.go:43, :79, :135 (FEAT-015). That guard must stay for project-scope installs; this child does NOT write any global tree, only resolves paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add registry.GlobalPluginsDir() string returning filepath.Join(<xdg-data-home>, 'ddx', 'global', 'plugins'), honoring XDG_DATA_HOME and the ~/.local/share fallback (mirror ddxroot.xdgDataHome semantics; export a shared helper rather than duplicating).
2. Add registry.ResolvePluginDir(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, name string) (dir, scope string, ok bool) with precedence:
   a. ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> if it exists -> scope 'project'.
   b. GlobalPluginsDir()/<name> if it exists -> scope 'global'.
   c. baked-in default plugin (only name=='ddx', via internal/registry/defaultplugin) -> scope 'baked-in'.
   d. otherwise ok=false.
3. Leave FEAT-015 ~-target rejection in installer.go untouched.

NON-SCOPE: wiring --global into install/update/init/doctor (sibling children); writing/symlinking the global tree.

DEPS: none new (parent dep ddx-06cbaa90 closed; ddxroot.Path already lands).
title: registry: global plugins dir resolution + project→global→baked-in plugin lookup precedence

description:
PROBLEM
Execute-loop execution fields are duplicated across cobra extraction, REST/GraphQL request structs, server ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec persistence, and runWorker consumption. Adding a worker mode or idle interval can silently drop it on one transport.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as a server-local struct while cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts a wider flag set, cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 has another request struct, and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 has a third. The type boundary is not shared, and the old polling field name conflicts with the desired public semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small import-safe package under cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ that defines ExecuteLoopSpec with JSON tags and fields ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, and SpecVersion.
- Define Mode values for once, drain, and watch. CLI parsing may keep --once, but the persisted/API spec uses Mode rather than a boolean once field.
- IdleInterval is the sleep duration used only when Mode=watch and the queue has no execution-ready work.
- Do not add or preserve a PollInterval field, poll_interval JSON field, or public compatibility alias in the new spec.
- Add DispatchOptions with only Local and JSON control-plane fields; do not persist it.
- Add Duration JSON support that marshals as strings and unmarshals both strings and numeric nanoseconds.
- Add ApplyDefaults and Validate methods, including SpecVersion support and a code comment documenting the single-version client/server forward-compat stance.
- Do not wire cobra, REST, GraphQL, or runWorker callers in this bead; later children consume this package.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change LabelFilter from string.
- Do not change work validation policy beyond moving existing checks behind the spec lifecycle.
- Do not modify server or cobra call sites except imports needed by tests.
notes: Unblocked after fresh-eyes cleanup removed stale PollInterval/poll_interval compatibility-alias requirements. The bead is now a standalone canonical ExecuteLoopSpec package slice with Mode + IdleInterval semantics and no unresolved investigation item.
title: agent: introduce canonical ExecuteLoopSpec package

description:
PROBLEM
The decomposition path that fans a too-large bead into executable children still wires the parent into the child dependency set. That recreates the exact back-edge this parent bead is about.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4675-4701` currently creates each child with `Parent: parent.ID` and then explicitly adds dependency edges from the parent to every child. The static prompt text in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2490-2577` and the guardrail snapshots in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go` still teach the decomposition flow to use the parent as a dependency edge, so the bug survives both the runtime path and the emitted instructions.

PROPOSED FIX
Change the programmatic decomposition path and the tracked prompt/guardrail text so generated children never add the parent to their deps. Keep only legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges. Add a golden e2e test named `decompose_does_not_self_dep` that creates a parent with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` still stops the entire drain when the project checkout contains implementation-file dirt before the next bead dispatch. During the Fizeau monitor on 2026-05-15, `fizeau-126f03c0` landed successfully, then the watcher attempted `fizeau-f02ada30` and exited with operator attention: project worktree `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau` had uncommitted implementation changes in internal/routing/engine.go, internal/routing/score.go, service.go, service_aliveness.go, service_execute.go, service_execute_dispatch.go, and service_routing.go. The operator had to stash/restore files by hand before the queue could continue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:575-599` treats any non-DDx dirty implementation path as a hard pre-dispatch checkpoint refusal. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1880-1912` converts that refusal into `operator_attention`, unclaims the candidate, logs “commit or clean”, and returns from the work loop. That behavior is safe for arbitrary user WIP, but it is not robust for DDx-managed watch drains where leaked lifecycle/root edits or generated artifacts must be preserved and the worker should keep draining.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the hard stop with an audited preserve-and-clean path for pre-dispatch dirt in managed `ddx work --watch` flows: write a named preservation artifact or git stash/ref containing the dirty patch, record the dirty paths and preserve handle in the attempt/work log, restore the affected paths to HEAD, unclaim the candidate without losing the patch, and keep the watch loop alive so the next scan can proceed. The implementation must still avoid silently discarding operator WIP: the preserve handle and affected paths must be visible in events/log output and the root must be clean before dispatching the next bead.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. Do not weaken checkpoint filtering for `.ddx` bookkeeping paths. Do not squash/rewrite execute-bead history. Do not hide dirty root evidence without a recoverable preservation handle.
title: work: preserve and clear pre-dispatch implementation dirt instead of stopping watch

acceptance:
1. `TestWork_WarnsStaleSourceBinaryButProceeds` (renamed from `TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan` in `cli/cmd/work_test.go`) — same fixture as before; asserts: warning text on stderr contains "WARNING: installed ddx is built from"; `stub.executeCalled` is true; the seeded bead is claimed; no error returned.
2. `TestTry_WarnsStaleSourceBinaryButProceeds` (renamed from `TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim` in `cli/cmd/try_test.go`) — analogous assertions for `ddx try`.
3. `TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr` (`cli/cmd/version_staleness_test.go`) still passes — `ddx version` warning unchanged.
4. `grep -n "failIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource" cli/cmd/*.go` returns nothing (function and all callsites deleted).
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestWork|TestTry|TestVersion|TestCheckVersionGate' -count=1` green.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work` and `ddx try` refuse to start when run from inside the DDx source repo and HEAD has commits ahead of the installed binary that touch `cli/`, `Makefile`, or `install.sh`. The operator sees a usage dump and a "installed ddx binary is stale for this DDx source checkout" error; nothing executes. This makes `ddx work --watch` unusable in an active-development workflow where the source moves often.

The hard fail was introduced by ddx-08b452ee with the rationale "do not silently run stale code during execution". But:
- It only fires inside the DDx source checkout (origin matches `DocumentDrivenDX/ddx`), so production users never see it.
- The in-loop `buildWorkBinaryRefreshCheck` (`cli/cmd/work_binary_refresh.go`) already exits + re-execs when the installed binary changes mid-loop. So the staleness gap is bounded to "until the next `make install` is run", not "forever".
- The startup gate strictly subsumes a runtime warning: there is no scenario where the runtime check would let stale code run that the startup gate catches.

So the startup gate's only effect is to be louder than a warning at the cost of being completely unusable during iteration.

ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/cmd/work.go:133` — `if err := f.failIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd, projectRoot, ExitCodeGeneralError); err != nil { return err }`
- `cli/cmd/try.go:120` — same pattern with `tryExitFailed`.
- The implementation `failIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource` lives in `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:667`; the warning-only equivalent `warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource` is at `cli/cmd/command_factory.go:732`.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace the hard fail in `cli/cmd/work.go:133` and `cli/cmd/try.go:120` with a call to `warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource` (print the existing warning to stderr, return nil). The in-loop refresh check stays as-is and will re-exec to the new binary once `make install` runs. Delete the now-dead `failIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource` and the recovery-command-only branch of `sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness` (`blockingMessage`).

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/work.go`
- `cli/cmd/try.go`
- `cli/cmd/command_factory.go`
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go` (rewrite `TestWork_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeQueueScan` -> `TestWork_WarnsStaleSourceBinaryButProceeds`: assert warning to stderr, assert work proceeds and claims the bead)
- `cli/cmd/try_test.go` (analogous rewrite of `TestTry_BlocksStaleSourceBinaryBeforeClaim`)

NON-SCOPE
- Do not touch the in-loop `buildWorkBinaryRefreshCheck` behavior.
- Do not touch the `.ddx/versions.yaml` semver gate (`checkVersionGate`).
- Do not auto-run `make install`.
- Do not change `ddx version`'s existing warning.
title: work/try: stop hard-failing when DDx source checkout is ahead of installed binary

TestReadinessChecksSchema (cli/internal/agent/readiness_schema_test.go:45) asserts every verdict form the decoder accepts also validates against the schema and decodes to the canonical readinessVerdict, and that object verdicts are rejected.

3. CI fails on schema drift: the `readiness-schema` Makefile target (cli/Makefile:152-156) runs TestReadinessChecksSchema and TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema and is invoked by .github/workflows/ci.yml:66-67 (`make readiness-schema`).

description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder once failed to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when readiness_checks[].verdict arrived as a JSON bool, because the producer (readiness prompt) emitted bool while the consumer (Go decoder) parsed string. Under warn-only policy every claim attempt produced an identical-fingerprint intake_error warn and the worker idled the whole fizeau queue (23 ready beads, one daemon) indefinitely. The detail string was: "decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string". This child owns the schema-gate slice of parent ddx-61f6c57d: make the readiness payload shape a single enforced contract so this class of producer/consumer drift fails the build instead of silently wedging the queue at runtime.

ROOT CAUSE
The verdict shape contract lives in two places that can drift independently: the producer prompt string assembled in buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639-642) and the Go consumer readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125), which coerces bool true->"pass", bool false->"fail", string->lowercased, null/absent->"". The canonical shape is declared in cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json (readiness_checks[].verdict is oneOf boolean/string/null). Without a CI gate binding all three together, a change to any one side reintroduces the wedge.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth for the readiness payload. Ensure (a) the schema accepts exactly the verdict forms readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON accepts, (b) the producer prompt continues to cite readinessChecksSchemaPath (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21,639-642) as authoritative, and (c) a CI gate fails on drift: the `readiness-schema` Makefile target (cli/Makefile:152-156) runs the schema tests and is invoked from .github/workflows/ci.yml:66-67 via `make readiness-schema`. The gate must fail if the schema, the prompt, or the decoder diverge on the verdict contract.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement the same-fingerprint warn escalation / silent-idle guard — that is the sibling child (worker loop escalation). Do not change the warn-only policy_mode semantics. Do not implement any other parent acceptance criterion. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint.
title: Enforce shared readiness_checks schema gate in CI so producer prompt and Go decoder cannot drift on verdict type

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 2 of 4)

Operators who want one Claude Code / Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so a plugin is written once to the machine-wide tree and linked into the home-level harness skill directories, available across every project without per-project setup.

ROOT CAUSE
- `runInstall` (cli/cmd/install.go:54) reads the `--global` flag (install.go:49,57) and routes target resolution through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184). When global is true it sets `installRoot = ddxroot.GlobalDir()` and skill dirs to `~/.agents/skills` and `~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-192); state file selection is via `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202-215). Local installs route the same way in `installLocal` (install.go:358, global branch install.go:450-507).
- Update: `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300) operates exclusively on `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/` via `registry.InstallPackage(adjustedPkg, ddxroot.GlobalDir())` (update.go:337) and saves the global state (update.go:346); the `--global` flag is parsed in `extractUpdateOptions` (update.go:395) into `UpdateOptions.Global` (update.go:35) and dispatched from `runUpdate` (update.go:65).

PROPOSED FIX
Ensure `ddx install <name> --global` writes the plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/` and creates symlink-or-copy entries under `~/.claude/skills/<name>` and `~/.agents/skills/<name>`, and does NOT touch the project's `.claude/skills` / `.agents/skills`. Ensure `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and global state, never the project tree. Expose `--global` consistently on the plugin query surface (`ddx plugin list` / `installed`).

NON-SCOPE
- The no-flag project install in either mode (slice 3).
- `ddx init --global` first-time setup and doctor reporting (slice 4).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) — global installs are resolved via `ResolvePlugin`'s global layer.
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — depends on `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
title: global install surface: ddx install --global and ddx update --global write the machine-wide plugin tree + agent-tier links

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/ddx` retried parent bead `ddx-c191e91a` after the parent had already been decomposed and marked execution-ineligible. On 2026-05-15 the first attempt `20260515T081727-1e2bf23c` preserved a no-change decomposition/parent update result, and `ddx bead show ddx-c191e91a --json` reported `execution-eligible: false` plus labels `area:agent,area:execution,kind:cleanup,decomposed`. `ddx bead ready --execution` no longer listed the bead. Despite that, the same live watcher started a second implementation attempt at `20260515T082640-623a9c00` against the same parent, burning model time and showing false progress.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1073-1113` claims the candidate selected from the queue snapshot without a post-selection guard that rechecks execution eligibility after tracker changes made by a prior attempt. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1681-1696` passes the originally selected `candidate` into `agenttry.Attempt`, so a stale candidate can still execute even if the store row has since been parked. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2367-2408` appends the terminal result and continues watch mode after a preserved/land-conflict/no-change result, allowing an immediate retry. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2603-2627` delegates selection to `PreviewQueue` and `ReadyExecution`, but the live incident proves this path can still select or retain an execution-ineligible decomposed parent. `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:131-134` documents the intended invariant: successful decomposition creates children and sets the parent `execution-eligible=false`.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an execution-eligibility recheck immediately before claim and again before invoking `agenttry.Attempt` in the watch/drain loop. If a candidate is now execution-ineligible, decomposed, superseded, or otherwise absent from the fresh execution-ready view, release/skip it without dispatching an implementer and continue to the next ready bead or idle. Ensure preserved no-change decomposition and mixed no-change/land-conflict outcomes do not allow the same live watcher to retry the parent in the same loop run.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go, only if needed for fixture reuse

NON-SCOPE
Do not change merge policy or preserved-iteration storage. Do not re-enable execution of decomposed parents. Do not hide land conflicts generally. Do not close the parent automatically unless existing child-closure policy already authorizes it.

DEPS
None. This is a worker-loop correctness bug independent of the child implementation beads for `ddx-c191e91a`.
title: work: stop retrying execution-ineligible decomposed parents

acceptance:

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` can reject every pre-claim readiness result when `readiness_checks[].verdict` is emitted as a JSON bool instead of the consumer's expected string shape. In the observed Fizeau queue, each ready bead produced the same `pre_claim_intake.warn` decode error and the worker idled indefinitely despite ready work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The readiness classification decoder consumes `readiness_checks` through `preClaimReadinessCheck.Verdict` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:75-80`, then uses `failedReadinessReasons` in `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:823-834` to interpret failed checks. The contract must explicitly tolerate bool, string, null, and absent verdict values at this boundary; otherwise producer/consumer schema drift turns a readiness payload into `intake_error` before a bead can execute.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement or verify a custom `readinessVerdict` decoder that accepts `true`, `false`, strings, `null`, and absent verdicts, coercing to a documented canonical form (`true` -> `pass`, `false` -> `fail`, string -> trimmed string, null/absent -> empty). Ensure the classification decode path exercises that type and that failed-check detection still treats canonical `fail` as the failure signal.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Fizeau route or harness configuration. Do not implement same-fingerprint escalation, claim-success-rate warnings, CI schema locking, README documentation, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not rely on `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/...` as load-bearing test input; inline fixtures in tests. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
None.
title: Fix pre-claim intake verdict decoding for bool/string/absent readiness checks

5. TestLand_IsNetworkFree in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go wires a fake LandingGitOps with no fetch/push methods (compile-time proof) and asserts Land() succeeds end-to-end.
6. Existing tests that asserted push-race recovery (e.g. TestLandPushAutoRecover_*) are removed or rewritten; no test references LandResult.PushFailed.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
  Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go invokes git fetch
  origin, git push --ff-only, and a push-race auto-recovery loop as part of
  the core drain lifecycle. This couples Land() to remote I/O, holding
  withMainGitLock for 30-120s on remote round-trips, which produced the
  observed contention failures (ddx-08e90869, ddx-15a51f8c). Per the user's
  architectural position and the sister doc-only bead amending FEAT-010,
  Land() must perform ONLY the local worktree-merge into local main; origin
  sync (fetch) and push are separate operations, never invoked from Land()
  or the drain loop.

  This bead removes all network I/O from Land() and from the LandingGitOps
  interface. Origin-sync and push become operator responsibilities (raw
  git commands today; a future ddx sync helper is downstream of multi-node
  work and explicitly out of scope per operator direction).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:27-29 file-header comment
    declares "If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip" as
    step 5 of the canonical Land flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1036-1043 Land() wraps the
    entire landLocked() call under withMainGitLock, including push.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1146-1216 landLocked merge
    path issues git fetch origin (FetchBranch), then git push (PushFFOnly),
    then on push race calls landPushAutoRecover.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1479 landPush triggers
    landPushAutoRecover on non-FF rejection.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1538-1580 landPushAutoRecover
    runs fetch + temp-worktree merge + update-ref + push, all inside the
    same lock.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:239-297 LandingGitOps
    interface includes FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, and FetchOriginAncestryCheck
    methods that exist solely to support the in-Land network I/O path.
  - LandResult fields PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered, and the
    fetchOriginError type all exist to communicate network-I/O outcomes
    that Land() should no longer be responsible for.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Strip Land() to local-only operations:
      a. fetch-origin call (FetchBranch) at the start of landLocked:
         REMOVE.
      b. FetchOriginAncestryCheck before merge: REMOVE (the merge proceeds
         on local main as-is; if origin diverged, that is discovered when
         the operator pushes, by raw git push rejecting non-FF, which is
         the operator's problem).
      c. PushFFOnly at end of landLocked: REMOVE.
      d. landPushAutoRecover function: REMOVE entirely.
  - Update LandingGitOps interface:
      a. Remove FetchBranch method.
      b. Remove PushFFOnly method.
      c. Remove FetchOriginAncestryCheck method.
      d. Keep merge / update-ref / worktree / commit methods.
  - Update LandResult:
      a. Remove PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered fields.
      b. Remove fetchOriginError type.
      c. Keep merge / commit / preserve outcomes.
  - Update the file-header comment at lines 27-29 to reflect the
    network-free flow.
  - Update Land() critical section: withMainGitLock now wraps only the
    local-index slice (merge-or-update-ref + evidence commit + worktree
    sync). Sub-second hold expected.
  - Existing tests for push-race recovery (and any LandResult.PushFailed
    assertions) MUST be removed or rewritten as no-network tests.
  - Add a regression test TestLand_IsNetworkFree in
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go that wires a
    LandingGitOps with no Fetch/Push methods (proving they are no longer
    called) and asserts Land() succeeds.

  Operator-side note (NOT in scope of this bead): users push with raw
  `git push origin main` after a drain session. A future ddx sync helper
  is downstream of multi-node coordination work and is explicitly
  deferred.

NON-SCOPE
  - Designing/building a ddx sync helper (deferred until multi-node).
  - Federation work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is single-node
    only.
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact; file separate beads if any
    surface friction).
  - Changing TD-031 bead state machine.
  - Reducing the bead.Store WithLock primitive (different layer).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on doc-only spec amendment bead ddx-3d5197d8 landing first.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-c019e48a (now scoped to local-only stale-lock
    recovery) depends on this implementation landing.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-eab41b94 (push-race scenarios) is now obsolete
    and should be cancelled.
title: agent/land: release withMainGitLock around network I/O (push, fetch, merge)

1. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_SerializesGitOperations asserts both instruction variants contain a direct prohibition on parallel git/staging/commit operations and require sequential git add/git commit handling.
2. TestExecuteBeadInstructions_RequiresStagedPreCommitGate asserts both instruction variants say lefthook run pre-commit must be rerun after staging when staged files drive hooks and that no-staged-files runs are insufficient evidence.
3. Prompt snapshot or guardrail coverage is updated so future prompt edits preserve both rules.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestExecuteBeadInstructions_(SerializesGitOperations|RequiresStagedPreCommitGate)|TestExecuteBeadInstructionsText' -count=1 passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the current monitor on 2026-05-15, two Codex execute-bead workers recovered from avoidable tool-sequencing errors. For ddx-b6859802, the worker staged ordinary files and ignored evidence with parallel git add calls; one failed with: fatal: Unable to create ... index.lock: File exists. For ddx-bb8e7d07, the worker ran lefthook run pre-commit before staging and got a no-staged-files skip, then had to rerun after staging; it also attempted to interrupt a completed test-poll session. These recoveries waste worker time and can look like DDx dirty-tree failures even when the implementation is otherwise fine.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2337-2341 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2369-2373 tell rich-prompt and embedded-agent workers to run tests, stage specific paths, and commit once. They do not explicitly prohibit parallel git/index operations, do not say that lefthook run pre-commit must be run after staging when hooks are staged-file-sensitive, and do not warn that a no-staged-files hook run is insufficient acceptance evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update both executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and executeBeadInstructionsAgentText to require git/staging/commit commands to run sequentially, not through parallel tool calls.
- Require workers to rerun lefthook run pre-commit after staging the exact commit set when pre-commit depends on staged files; an earlier no-staged-files hook run is not sufficient AC evidence.
- Add or extend prompt guardrail/snapshot tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_prompt_snapshot_test.go so the rule cannot regress.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change core gitlock recovery.
- Do not change execute-bead lifecycle, result schema, or merge policy.
- Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl.

DEPS
None.
title: execute-bead: serialize worker git commands and staged hook gates

No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.
- Context typed accessors implemented. Tests: `TestWithIdentity_RoundTrip`, `TestIdentityFromContext_AbsentReturnsFalse`, `TestWithTrace_RoundTrip`.
- Foundational sub-interfaces (`BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle`, `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter`) declared with ctx-first signatures. `*Store` satisfies only the non-event foundation interfaces in this bead — compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadInitializer = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadLifecycle = (*Store)(nil)`. No `BeadEventReader`/`BeadEventWriter` assertion is added until E.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

3. A unit test asserts `isTransientGitContention` returns true for the literal substring "tracker lock timeout" as a defense-in-depth fallback.
4. A unit test of `finalizeDurableAuditOrStop` injects a tracker-lock-timeout error and asserts: (a) `result.OperatorAttention` is NOT set, (b) the durable_audit_transient event is emitted, (c) the function returns false (don't stop).
5. `cd cli && go test ./... -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: When two or more `ddx work` workers run concurrently against the
same project and worker B reaches `FinalizeDurableAudit` at the same moment
that worker A holds the tracker lock, worker B times out, and the loop
classifies the timeout as `operator_attention` and exits — even though
nothing requires operator action. The other worker just needs to finish.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain with two concurrent workers:
- Worker1 (pid 424293) was committing tracker state for niflheim-996afcc8.
- Worker2 (pid 681801), simultaneously finalizing niflheim-e43f04bb (an
  ADR-051 cold-tier PUT concurrency implementation that had already run to
  completion — 66091 tokens, 32m8.5s), hit `tracker lock timeout (max
  elapsed, lock: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock,
  owner pid: 424293)` while trying to commit durable audit outputs.
- Worker2 exited with `operator attention required`, releasing
  niflheim-e43f04bb back to `open` without committing the audit trail,
  and leaving these paths dirty: `.ddx/beads.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/attachments`.

The work was successful — 32 minutes of agent time produced a clean
implementation and commit. The lock race nuked the audit attribution and
forced the worker to stop. Re-running it would either repeat the agent run
(burning more tokens) or hit `already_satisfied` (with the worktree-leak
documented in `ddx-274943fc`).

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1309`
(`finalizeDurableAuditOrStop`) calls `runtime.FinalizeDurableAudit` and
branches on `isTransientGitContention(err.Error())` to decide retry vs
operator-attention. The matcher at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1019-1034` covers `.git/index.lock`
(via `gitlock.IsIndexLockError`) and the strings "unable to write new index
file", "cannot lock ref", "unable to update the ref" — but **does not cover
the DDx-specific tracker-lock error format** emitted by
`cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:243`:

  "tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)"

The tracker lock is a DDx-internal ordering lock around tracker writes
(`.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`), held briefly during tracker-state mutations.
Concurrent worker contention on this lock is structurally identical to
the `.git/index.lock` case the existing matcher handles, and the comment
on `isTransientGitContention` explicitly states the design intent:

  "A persistent cause (e.g. ENOSPC) simply keeps releasing+retrying the
   bead, which is still preferable to halting the whole unattended drain."

PROPOSED FIX: Extend `isTransientGitContention` to recognise the DDx
tracker-lock-timeout string class. Recommended implementation:

  1. Export a stable marker constant (or recogniser) from
     `cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go` rather than string-matching
     across packages. Suggested: a sentinel error type
     `TrackerLockTimeoutError` wrapping the existing fields (lockDir,
     ownerPID), with an `Is(err)` helper.
  2. Update `FinalizeDurableAudit` and its callers to wrap the timeout
     error with this sentinel, so callers can use `errors.Is` instead of
     substring matching.
  3. In `isTransientGitContention` at `execute_bead_loop.go:1019`, add a
     branch that returns true for `errors.Is(err, TrackerLockTimeoutErr)`.
     Keep the existing substring fallbacks for defense in depth.
  4. Confirm via test that worker2 in the observed scenario survives the
     lock timeout, re-enters the loop, and successfully commits on a
     subsequent iteration when the lock releases.

NON-SCOPE:
- Changing the tracker-lock retry policy itself (timeout duration, backoff
  curve) — only changing how the loop classifies the lock-timeout outcome.
- Changing concurrent-worker safety guarantees on tracker writes.
- Adding queue-level fairness or worker coordination (out of scope for
  this bead).
- Fixing the orphan-worktree leak that compounds this issue — tracked
  separately in `ddx-274943fc`.

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go (export sentinel error type)
- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go (wrap timeout with sentinel where
  it bubbles up — verify by reading line 41 and 127)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (extend `isTransientGitContention`)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go (new test for tracker-lock
  classification)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- gitlock package (already correct for .git/index.lock; do not modify)
- cli/cmd/work.go (exit-reason rendering unchanged)
- Any other isTransientGitContention call sites — only the durable-audit
  branch matters for this bug.

acceptance:
1. A transient .git/index.lock contention failure during the durable-audit/tracker commit does NOT exit the worker: it is classified transient, the bead is released, and the drain loop continues. Verified by a new test (e.g. TestWork_IndexLockContentionDuringAuditCommitIsTransientNotFatal in cli/internal/agent) that injects an index.lock failure on the tracker/audit commit and asserts the worker continues rather than returning the operator-attention exit.
2. A genuine, non-lock git failure during the audit commit still surfaces as operator attention (regression).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Under multi-worker operation, a ddx work worker EXITS with "operator attention required: DDx could not commit durable audit outputs; resolve the git failure before continuing autonomous work" when the durable-audit/tracker commit hits transient .git/index.lock contention. Observed 2026-05-27: with 2 ddx workers + a fizeau worker + the server sharing one project .git, a worker died on `git add` tracker -> ".git/index.lock: File exists ... no owner found but lock is fresh (age 19s < 30s)". A transient lock conflict stops the whole worker; the more workers, the more often this fires, directly defeating unattended steady-state drain.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- git .git/index.lock carries no pid, so gitlock.IndexLockOwner finds no owner. RecoverGitIndexLock (cli/internal/gitlock/gitlock.go:144-158) only reaps an ownerless lock once age >= StaleAge (30s, gitlock.go:26); below that it returns Removed=false ("no owner found but lock is fresh").
- RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery retries only RecoveryAttempts=3 (gitlock.go:30) with LiveOwnerWait=500ms (gitlock.go:34) ~= 1.5s total, far short of the 30s reap floor, then returns the index.lock error.
- commitTrackerLocked (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2153) calls runGitWithIndexLockRecovery for `git add`; its error becomes "staging tracker: ...". That propagates to the durable-audit-commit exit at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1175, which stops the worker (operator attention) instead of treating transient lock contention as retryable.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Do NOT exit the drain on transient .git/index.lock contention during the durable-audit/tracker commit. Classify index.lock contention as transient (mirroring the pre-claim tracker-contention class from ddx-df77e668), release the bead, and continue the loop so the next iteration retries. A transient lock must never stop a worker.
2. AND/OR raise the recovery patience for tracker/audit commits to a bounded backoff that can outlast typical contention (up to ~the StaleAge window) before failing.
3. Optional: reap a provably-orphaned lock sooner (e.g. confirm no live git holds the repo via lsof) while keeping the 30s floor when liveness is unknown.
Recommend (1) as the primary fix: transient lock contention is retryable, not operator-attention-worthy.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken safety against removing a live git process index.lock.
- Do not change the landing-path read-tree retry (already has backoff, ddx-7e659c95).

CROSS-REF: same multi-worker-resilience theme as ddx-57c40485 (lock scoping) and ddx-df77e668 (pre-claim tracker-contention). This is the post-fix gap on the COMMIT side.
notes: Already fixed by operator direct-fix (bootstrap-hostile to dogfood): transient index.lock during durable-audit commit now classified retryable via gitlock.IsIndexLockError, worker continues instead of exiting. Landed 48631b2ed; TestWork_IndexLockContentionDuringAuditCommitIsTransientNotFatal + regression pass; full internal/agent gate green; reinstalled.
title: execute-bead: worker exits on transient index.lock contention during durable-audit commit instead of retrying — kills unattended multi-worker drain

description:

The current e2e suite still treats `/sessions` as a standalone page, but the frontend has already shifted sessions into the run-layer experience. `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.ts` redirects to `/runs?layer=run`, and the fallback Svelte page says the same. That redirect contract is breaking `app.spec.ts` TC-005, `sessions.spec.ts`, and several screenshot fixtures that still assume the old standalone Sessions page.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.ts:1-10` redirects sessions into runs.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/sessions/+page.svelte:1-7` documents the redirect fallback.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/sessions.spec.ts:107-289` still exercises the old standalone sessions contract.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts:133-137` still snapshots the old sessions-driven pages, including the agent / project-overview / efficacy coverage that is currently failing.

PROPOSED FIX
Bring the sessions e2e coverage and the screenshot baselines up to the current run-layer redirect contract. Update the tests to assert the redirect into runs, the run-layer session expansion, and any screenshot baselines that changed because sessions are now surfaced in runs. Keep the fix within the frontend/e2e tree and the sessions route files.

NON-SCOPE
- The documents/beads fixture regressions from app.spec.
- The 2k scale fixture bead.
- The broader Store API migration.

ACCEPTANCE
1. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/sessions.spec.ts` passes against the current redirect contract.
2. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` passes for the affected pages.
3. `cd cli && bun run test:e2e -- --grep "TC-005|screenshots"` passes.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

description:
PROBLEM: Running 2 concurrent ddx workers on the same project, tracker/audit commits repeatedly fail with 'fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128' and 'Unable to create .git/index.lock: File exists'. This fails attempts and eventually trips the resource_exhausted breaker (worker exits), throttling the multi-worker drain. Observed continuously this session in the ddx worker logs.
ROOT CAUSE: tracker commits and durable-audit-output commits run git add/commit against the MAIN working tree's shared .git/index. Workers serialize via .ddx/.git-tracker.lock, but the underlying git index.lock still collides — the tracker lock does not cover the full git index mutation window, OR the durable-audit-commit path runs outside the tracker lock. Prior lock-scoping beads (ddx-329a08b8, ddx-90d41915, ddx-23ac2796, ddx-0b743e7e, ddx-57c40485) are CLOSED but the collision persists.
PROPOSED FIX: close the remaining gap so concurrent main-repo index mutations never collide — e.g. hold a single per-project index mutex across the entire add+commit window for tracker + durable-audit + land, OR commit tracker/audit via a dedicated index (GIT_INDEX_FILE) so they never touch the shared working-tree index.
NON-SCOPE: switching attempt backend.
title: execute-bead: concurrent workers still collide on main-repo .git/index during tracker/durable-audit commits (exit status 128)

2. A unit test exists for each of these non-success outcomes asserting WorktreeRemove was called: provider_connectivity, no_evidence_produced, execution_failed, structural_validation_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed.
3. A unit test asserts the success-path classifier does NOT call WorktreeRemove from the new hook (landing path retains ownership).
