description:
PROBLEM: When a `ddx try`/`ddx work` attempt fails with any non-success
outcome (`provider_connectivity`, `no_evidence_produced`, `execution_failed`,
`structural_validation_failed`, `land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`),
its execute-bead worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<attemptID>/`
is not removed. Cleanup happens only when a *subsequent attempt of the same bead*
is launched and `RecoverOrphans` runs as pre-spawn sweep.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain: a 6.6 GB worktree
`.execute-bead-wt-niflheim-07f6f8ff-20260528T164209-f179349f` remained 9+
hours after its attempt failed with `provider_connectivity` (bragi 502).
The bead's retry resolved as `already_satisfied` without spawning a new
execute-bead worktree (the AC was already met by partially-landed commits
from the failed attempt), so `RecoverOrphans` was never called for that
bead — the leak is permanent until another attempt at the same bead runs.

The user reports seeing this pattern consistently across drains, not just
on the one observed bead.

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:393-411`
defines `RecoverOrphans` with the docstring "the parent's responsibility —
call it before spawning new workers so stale worktrees from crashed previous
attempts do not accumulate." The function matches strictly by
`basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + "-"` and is only invoked
on a new-attempt pre-spawn path. Two leak conditions follow:

  (a) Bead's retry succeeds via `already_satisfied` (no new worktree
      spawned because the AC check resolves before agent invocation).
      The orphan from the prior failed attempt is never matched.
  (b) Bead is closed for any reason after a failed attempt and is never
      re-attempted (most common in real flows — failures often resolve
      via decomposition into different child beads, never returning to
      the original).

The post-attempt outcome classifier at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1966` (Classify worker outcome:
task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes / ...) is the natural place
to call `WorktreeRemove` for non-success classes, but does not.
The existing landing path at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1088,1329,1679` calls
`gitOps.RemoveWorktree` correctly for landing-time temp worktrees, but
those are different (per-landing) worktrees, not the per-attempt execute-bead
worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.

PROPOSED FIX: Add an attempt-finalize cleanup hook that runs after outcome
classification (around `execute_bead.go:1966-2017`) and removes the
per-attempt execute-bead worktree when the outcome is anything other than
`task_succeeded`. Preserve only when:
  1. The operator explicitly opted in via `ddx try --no-merge` (preserve
     for inspection regardless of outcome).
  2. An `executions_mirror.go` write or evidence-capture step requires the
     worktree to persist past classification (verify by reading the call
     chain at `execute_bead.go:2069-2114`).

Implementation steps the executing agent should follow:
  1. Read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1423-2017` to confirm the
     outcome-classification flow and identify the right post-classify hook
     point.
  2. Add a helper `cleanupAttemptWorktree(gitOps, workDir, wtPath, outcome,
     preserveFlag)` that calls `gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, wtPath)` for
     non-success outcomes unless `preserveFlag` is set.
  3. Wire it into the outcome-classification path.
  4. Add a unit test asserting the cleanup branch for each non-success
     outcome class, and a negative test confirming success-path keeps the
     worktree (landing path owns that cleanup).
  5. Update `RecoverOrphans` docstring to reflect that it's now a
     belt-and-suspenders sweep, not the primary cleanup path.

NON-SCOPE:
- Changing `RecoverOrphans` behavior or call sites (keep it; it covers
  hard-crash cases the new hook can't reach).
- Adding a periodic background sweeper or `ddx doctor` flag for stale
  worktrees (potential follow-up).
- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.
- Changing outcome-classification logic or outcome taxonomy.
- Adding telemetry/metrics for leak rates.

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (add the cleanup hook + helper)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_test.go or sibling _test file (add
  cleanup tests for each non-success outcome)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (docstring update on
  RecoverOrphans only; behavior unchanged)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- execute_bead_land.go — landing-time temp-worktree cleanup is correct
  and untouched.
- Any code under cli/cmd/ — no CLI surface change required.
- docs/ — documentation update is downstream of the spec-evolution bead
  ddx-828a1b8f (worktree resource isolation) and not required here.

acceptance:
1. ExecuteLoopSpec struct defined in cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go. Exported fields include at minimum: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion. Audit: any field present in cobra extraction (agent_cmd.go:1582-1605) or workers.go:25-49 not in this list is a bug.

2. The unified spec does not contain a PollInterval field, a poll_interval JSON tag, or any public compatibility field for the removed polling flag. New CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.

3. parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) exists in the new package. Called only from cobra RunE in agent_cmd.go. No parallel cobra-extraction block remains. DispatchOptions holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) and is not persisted to spec.json.

4. parseExecuteLoopSpec maps --once to Mode=once, maps --watch to Mode=watch, defaults to Mode=drain, maps --idle-interval to IdleInterval only for Mode=watch, and returns a usage error when --idle-interval is provided without --watch.

5. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods exist. Lifecycle enforced: (a) client calls Validate() before HTTP submit or inline dispatch; (b) server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults() then Validate() after decode, before StartExecuteLoop; (c) spec.json on disk is fully resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran at write time).

6. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2390) decodes directly into ExecuteLoopSpec; the anonymous request struct (currently lines 2395-2409) is deleted. Mode, IdleInterval, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, and FromRev survive the REST round-trip.

7. graphql_adapters.go anonymous request struct (lines 63-76) and its parallel StartExecuteLoop literal (lines 127-141) are deleted. GraphQL dispatch goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.

8. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is replaced by or aliased to ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json files written by StartExecuteLoop use the unified struct and emit Mode plus IdleInterval.

9. runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD as the cost-cap ceiling, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD fallback when zero. No hardcoded DefaultMaxCostUSD reference outside the fallback case.

10. OpaquePassthrough: server-submitted spec with OpaquePassthrough=true skips both validation gates - workers.go:357-361 (StartExecuteLoop pre-flight) and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch inside runWorker. TestStartExecuteLoop_OpaquePassthrough_SkipsValidation asserts that StartExecuteLoop with OpaquePassthrough=true does not call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService and returns a WorkerRecord without error even when the harness name is unresolvable.

11. Reflection round-trip test: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection in cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or executeloop sub-package. Uses reflect to iterate exported fields of ExecuteLoopSpec; for each field sets a non-zero sentinel value; marshals to JSON; unmarshals; asserts equality. Test fails if any exported field has a zero-value sentinel after unmarshal. A future added field automatically falls under coverage.

12. Backward compatibility for disk only: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ load without error. Files containing unknown legacy fields are tolerated and ignored by json.Unmarshal default behavior. Do not enable json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads.

13. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field present. Server returns HTTP 400 with a capability description when incoming SpecVersion > server-supported version. SpecVersion==0 (unset) is treated as the current version. TestHandleStartExecuteLoopWorker_HighSpecVersion_Returns400 covers this.

14. Regression: inline execution path behavior is unchanged except for the explicit once/drain/watch mapping. Existing tests that cover the inline path pass without modification.

15. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green.

16. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ExecuteLoopSpec fields are declared in parallel structs across three independent code sites. When a flag or worker mode is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. The new work-loop contract requires one vocabulary across CLI parsing, worker persistence, REST, and GraphQL: mode plus idle_interval, with no public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag.

ROOT CAUSE
Each work field traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:

1. cobra flag extraction plus local vars: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once/watch mode, idleInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, and maxPower as naked locals.

2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 closes over these locals and assembles config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them.

3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition: cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is hand-maintained in parallel with cobra and has historically missed fields.

4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct: cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is a separate anonymous struct, so newly added fields can be silently lost for server-spawned workers.

5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 plus cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 hand-assembles the worker spec; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here.

6. GraphQL adapter request struct plus StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141 is a third parallel declaration; fixes to REST can leave GraphQL broken.

The consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has two validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).

PROPOSED FIX
- cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go: define ExecuteLoopSpec with all fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.
- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. Default is Mode=drain.
- IdleInterval is meaningful only for Mode=watch. --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error in the cobra layer and must not be represented as a separate polling mode in the persisted spec.
- Do not add, accept, document, or emit a public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag. Historical worker records may be read best-effort as unknown/legacy input, but new CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.
- Add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults(). Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) as the single cobra-to-spec conversion.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as REST.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD, with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero.
- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec_test.go: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. New field = automatic coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7) owns replacing runWorker with exec ddx work; this bead does not change the subprocess vs in-process dispatch decision.
- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, MCP agent dispatch) may have the same smell; covered by sister bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string is owned by ddx-9d55601f; keep LabelFilter as string.

SEQUENCING
ddx-29058e2a (this bead) vs ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7 - server-spawn migration to exec ddx work): lands independently and before ddx-eccc6efb. ADR-022 says ddx-29058e2a remains valuable as worker-side struct unification (cobra to in-process flow) and should land independently. When ddx-eccc6efb lands later, it will delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec from workers.go as part of the exec migration; the unified ExecuteLoopSpec should already live in the durable agent package. If ddx-eccc6efb lands first, the scope of this bead narrows to the cobra and worker-facing layers that still exist.

Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits.

## Summary

The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.

These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:
  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or
  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.

The bead's "SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.

## Evidence

1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).
2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: "Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands." Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.
3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).
4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM ("ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.
5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.

## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)

The bead also fails the size check on its own:

- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).
- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.
- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably >500 LOC across >5 files in unrelated packages.
- Per execution-bead protocol size check: "More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems" + "More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages" → DECOMPOSE.

## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt

The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:

  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.
  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.
  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.

Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.

## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage

1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.
2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead "add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).
3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.
4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.

## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)

- No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice.
- No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see "Why I am not decomposing" above.
- No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's status.

acceptance:
1. New test (e.g. TestExecuteBead_TrackerCommitIndexContentionIsTransientNotBeadFailure): simulate the tracker commit at execute_bead.go:2286 hitting "fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128" (and index.lock) under a held lock; assert the bead is NOT marked failed and the tracker commit retries/succeeds on a subsequent attempt.
2. The tracker-commit path and the durable-audit-commit path share one transient-contention classifier (no divergent copy); a test asserts both call-sites treat "unable to write new index file" as transient.
3. Regression: a genuine non-transient tracker-commit failure (e.g. permission) still fails the bead as before.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBead_TrackerCommit|TestIsTransientGitContention' -count=1 green.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -count=1 green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (captured live, 2nd 2-worker collision): With two `ddx work --watch` workers on /Users/erik/Projects/ddx, the DURABLE-AUDIT commit path correctly retried index contention (ddx-82c89a10: "transient git index/ref contention committing durable audit outputs ... will retry next iteration" -> merged), BUT a SEPARATE commit path — the tracker commit at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:2286 (`return fmt.Errorf("committing tracker: %s: %w", ...)`) — does NOT route through the transient-retry, so the SAME "fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128" contention FAILED 3 beads outright: ddx-3fd19af1, ddx-122e0ec5, ddx-e6f047cd ("committing tracker: fatal: unable to write new index file: exit status 128"). So the resilience added for the durable-audit commit (ddx-23ac2796, and pending ddx-83361480/ddx-e8bf0695) does NOT protect the tracker-commit call-site.

ROOT CAUSE: the tracker commit at execute_bead.go:2286 (and its caller) returns the git error directly as a hard failure; it does not classify "unable to write new index file" / index.lock / "signal: killed" as transient nor retry, unlike the durable-audit path's finalizeDurableAuditOrStop + isTransientGitContention (execute_bead_loop.go:1026,1288). Two workers committing tracker state to the same shared .git/index contend, and the loser fails its bead instead of retrying.

PROPOSED FIX: Route the tracker commit through the SAME transient-contention handling as the durable-audit commit — classify index-write/index.lock/ref-lock/SIGKILL/deadline contention (via the shared isTransientGitContention, extended by ddx-83361480) as transient and retry on a later iteration (the tracker commit should be idempotent — re-deriving and re-committing the same staged state), rather than failing the bead. Prefer a single shared resilient-commit helper used by BOTH the durable-audit path and the execute_bead.go:2286 tracker path so the two cannot diverge again.

NON-SCOPE: the durable-audit classifier/deadline (ddx-83361480, ddx-e8bf0695 — this bead reuses that classifier); worktree/file leaks (ddx-b320e794, ddx-274943fc).
title: execute-bead: 'committing tracker' commit path lacks transient-retry on index contention — fails beads under 2-worker load

acceptance:
1. .ddx/config.yaml documents bead_tracker.backend: axon plus the Axon connection block.
2. TestNewStore_DefaultsToJSONL, TestNewStore_SelectsAxonFromConfig, and TestNewStore_PreservesExternalBackends are added or updated.
3. config selection honors axon without requiring DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL, while jsonl remains the default.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update the store factory so bead_tracker.backend=axon selects the Axon backend from config without requiring the experimental env var.
- Update the checked-in .ddx/config.yaml example to show the backend and Axon connection block.
- Keep the default backend unchanged when backend is unset.
- Preserve bd/br behavior for the existing external backend path.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Axon backend CRUD internals.
- Subscription transport.

description:
PROBLEM
ddx work currently emits "No execution-eligible beads in the queue" and exits when the only ready beads are epics with open children, mislabeled closure candidates, superseded leftovers, dead intermediates, or epic-of-epic chains. Operators have to manually triage every blocker class. This violates the factory rule that ddx work must self-unstick (reliability principle P10, just added).

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:122-167 (post-edit) and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md "Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation" subsection (just added) now mandate diagnose+auto-remediate semantics. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today only reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, then sleeps/exits — no diagnoser, no remediation dispatch. cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:218 isOrdinaryEpicContainer conflates openChildCount with totalChildCount, mislabeling 6 of 10 ready epics in the current project as containers when they are actually closure candidates.

PROPOSED FIX
Build the diagnose+auto-remediate idle path per FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation: per-bead Diagnose function with the closed reason taxonomy; remediator handlers for RC-1 (lifecycle classifier fix), RC-2 (supersession cascade in Close), RC-3 (dead-intermediate cleanup), RC-6 (auto-epic-decompose wrapping runPreClaimDecomposer); idle-path wiring in execute_bead_loop.go that runs diagnoser, fires matching remediators, re-scans without sleeping on success; operator surface (ddx work focus) two-section output that NEVER silently hides auto-remediable beads; ddx work plan --explain/--json flags for inspection. All safety guards (attempt cap, cost budget, cooldown, cycle guard, operator overrides, audit events) per the spec.

NON-SCOPE
- Building the FEAT-004 epic-scoped worker mode (dedicated branch/worktree, sequential child execution). Explicitly deferred in the FEAT-004 update.
- Replacing the operator-invoked bead-breakdown skill. It stays the right tool for top-down planning from a fresh spec/plan.
- Two-worker concurrency redesign — current tracker serialization is reused.

CHILD QUEUE
- diagnose: per-bead reason classifier (cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go)
- RC-1: classifier split openChildCount/totalChildCount (cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go)
- RC-2: supersession cascade in Close() (cli/internal/bead/store.go)
- RC-3: dead-intermediate cleanup walk-up in Close() (cli/internal/bead/store.go)
- RC-6: auto-epic-decompose handler (cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go)
- idle-path wiring: diagnose+dispatch+rescan in execute_bead_loop.go:1263
- work focus: two-section output (cli/cmd/work_focus.go)
- work plan: --explain/--json flags (cli/cmd/work_plan.go)
title: epic: ddx work — idle-path diagnose and auto-remediate (factory mode)

description:
PROBLEM
  The pattern "park bead to proposed status" is duplicated 6 times in agent/*.go:
  - execute_bead_loop.go
  - execute_bead_post_review.go
  - execute_bead_review_terminal.go
  - execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go
  
  Each duplication performs the same sequence:
  1. Park the bead to status=proposed
  2. Update extra metadata (reason, findings ref, etc.)
  3. Append triage/recovery event
  4. Update labels
  
  This violates DRY and makes it harder to maintain consistent behavior across 
  the agent codebase.

PROPOSED FIX
  Extract a Store.ParkToProposed helper method covering:
  1. Status transition: in_progress → proposed
  2. Label updates (optional additional labels)
  3. Event appending with consistent event body
  4. Extra metadata updates (reason, recovery ref, cost, etc.)
  
  Refactor all 6 call sites in agent/*.go to use the helper instead of duplicating 
  the pattern.
  
  The helper should accept parameters for:
  - Bead ID
  - Reason (for event body and extra metadata)
  - Optional labels (overlaid on defaults)
  - Optional extra fields (merged into existing extra)
  - Context/tracing fields (session_id, attempt_id, etc.)

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the park-to-proposed behavior itself
  - Changing when/why beads are parked (that's in execute-bead logic)
  - Adding new persisted statuses
  - Refactoring other common patterns
title: extract Store.ParkToProposed helper for 6 agent duplicates

acceptance:
1. `TestInstall_InTreeWritesProjectTreeAndLinks` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:21) asserts `ddx install <name>` in in-tree mode writes to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and creates project-tier links under `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`.
2. `TestInstall_ConventionModeWritesXDGProjectTree` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:73) asserts `ddx install <name>` in convention mode writes to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/` and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path.
3. `TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode` (cli/cmd/update_test.go:247) asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 3 of 4)

Without `--global`, install and update must target the project's ddx root, which differs by mode: in-tree (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>`) versus convention (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>`). Either way the project-tier agent skill links (`<project>/.claude/skills` and `<project>/.agents/skills`) must point into whichever plugins tree is active.

ROOT CAUSE
- `resolveInstallDirs(global=false)` (cli/cmd/install.go:184, project branch install.go:195-197) sets `installRoot = ddxroot.Path(ctx, f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs to `<workingDir>/.agents/skills` and `<workingDir>/.claude/skills`. `ddxroot.Path` (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75) returns the in-tree `.ddx` path when `<project>/.ddx` exists and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` path otherwise, so mode selection is implicit.
- `installLocal` non-global branch derives `ddxRootDir := ddxroot.Path(...)` (install.go:453) for the same routing.
- Update: the non-global path `performUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:204) uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, ...)` (e.g. update.go:227) so it updates the project install in whichever mode is active; mode is not an explicit flag but follows `ddxroot`.

PROPOSED FIX
Ensure `ddx install <name>` (no `--global`) in in-tree mode writes to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and links `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`; in convention mode (no `<project>/.ddx/`) writes to the XDG project plugins path and links the project-tier skill dirs into that path. Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

NON-SCOPE
- The `--global` install/update surface (slice 2).
- `ddx init` behavior and doctor reporting (slice 4).
- Removing either mode — in-tree and convention coexist.

DEPS
- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) and ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project paths route through `ddxroot.Path()`.
title: project install surface: ddx install (no flag) and ddx update honor in-tree vs convention mode

acceptance:
1. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go:23) asserts that when both the project and global trees contain the plugin, ResolvePlugin returns the project path and layer "project".
2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go:52) asserts that when the project copy is absent, ResolvePlugin returns the ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name> path and layer "global".
3. A test asserts ResolvePlugin returns ("", "baked-in", nil) for name == "ddx" when neither project nor global copy exists, and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name.
4. The skill-install-topology ADR and the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" precedence note are landed in this bead's commit.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/registry/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills/personas/plugins can live in two install layers (project-scoped under ddxroot.Path() and machine-wide under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/). Runtime callers need a single authoritative resolver that picks the right on-disk plugin directory with a defined precedence, otherwise the dual-location install surface has no consumer and project installs cannot shadow global ones.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:22 ResolvePlugin must implement precedence project → global → baked-in. The project path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot), "plugins", name) (resolve.go:23); the global path is filepath.Join(ddxroot.GlobalDir(), "plugins", name) (resolve.go:28); baked-in (defaultplugin.FS()) is valid only for name == "ddx" (resolve.go:33). ddxroot.GlobalDir() is defined at cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:90.
- Before this layer existed, install/update targeted ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and there was no precedence rule, so an operator with both a project and a global copy had undefined resolution.

PROPOSED FIX
1. ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name) returns (path, layer, err): when both project and global copies exist, return the project path with layer "project"; when only the global copy exists, return its path with layer "global"; when neither exists and name == "ddx", return ("", "baked-in", nil) so callers fall back to defaultplugin.FS(); otherwise return a non-nil error naming all three layers.
2. The project path resolves through ddxroot.Path() so it is correct in BOTH in-tree mode (<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>) and convention mode (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>).
3. SPEC IMPACT: file/update the skill-install-topology ADR (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent) documenting the three-layer precedence, and update the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section to state the project-over-global-over-baked-in lookup precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).
- Does NOT migrate any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Does NOT add agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.
- Writing the plugin content / creating agent-tier links is handled by the install and init child beads, not here.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project path routes through ddxroot.Path() and the global path through ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on. Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2: skills/personas install-to-agent vs install-in-project).
title: registry: plugin lookup precedence resolver (project → global → baked-in) + topology spec

acceptance:
1. `TestWorkerPathDoesNotUseFetchOriginAncestryCheck` fails if worker/try/pre-claim execution paths use `FetchOriginAncestryCheck` instead of local ancestry.
2. Existing network-free landing/pre-claim tests still pass.
3. The test uses static source inspection or injected `recordingGitOps`; it does not create a network remote or call `git fetch` against a hosted origin.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkerPathDoesNotUseFetchOriginAncestryCheck|NetworkFree|Fetch'` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The worker path is intended to be network-free, but the fetch-based ancestry checker remains available and holds the main-git lock while running `git fetch`. Any future accidental worker-path wiring to that checker would violate TP-021/P9 and can turn multi-worker drain into a network bottleneck under lock.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:837 wires `FetchOriginAncestryCheck` through `withMainGitLock`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:876 runs `git fetch` in that ancestry checker.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:847 defines the local-only ancestry checker that the worker path should use.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a static or contract test that fails if `ddx try`, `ddx work`, pre-claim, or execute-loop paths wire `FetchOriginAncestryCheck`.
- Keep fetch-based ancestry available only for explicitly non-worker multi-machine coordination paths, if still needed.
- Document any remaining fetch-based use as non-drain and non-TP-021 scope.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove multi-machine coordination design in this bead.
- Do not add a real remote or network dependency to the test.
title: agent: guard worker paths against fetch ancestry under lock

acceptance:
1. `ddx try --help` and `ddx work --help` list `in-tree` alongside `worktree`, `local-clone`, and `docker-clone`.
2. Add targeted backend-selection tests proving `--attempt-backend in-tree` selects an in-project execution backend and does not create a linked worktree or local clone.
3. Add a dirty-tree guard test proving `in-tree` refuses to start when non-DDx project files are modified before claim.
4. Add an exclusive-lock test proving a second `in-tree` worker for the same project exits with an actionable lock/worker-in-progress message instead of running concurrently.
5. Add a success-path test proving only files changed by the attempt plus DDx tracker/evidence files are staged/committed.
6. Documentation in `docs/agent-execute.md` and FEAT-010 explains when to use `in-tree`, its clean-tree requirement, single-worker constraint, and failure/rescue behavior.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test.*(AttemptBackend|InTree|DirtyTree|WorkerLock)' -count=1` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Projects with very large local data, external services, or benchmark fixtures cannot safely rely on the existing DDx attempt backends for every bead. Niflheim is the concrete case: many ready beads are evidence/validation tasks that mostly run tests or long benchmark harnesses against data and services already present in the checked-out tree. The current backends all move execution away from that tree: `worktree`, `local-clone`, and `docker-clone`. That is correct for code-edit isolation, but it makes data-heavy validation fragile or impossible when the attempt needs untracked local datasets, running service state, or a checkout too expensive to duplicate.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/work.go:83` and `cli/cmd/try.go:96` expose only `worktree`, `local-clone`, and `docker-clone` as attempt backend choices.
- `docs/agent-execute.md:97-104` and `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:776-782` specify the same isolated-backend-only model.
- There is no documented or implemented backend that runs a single claimed bead directly in the project checkout while still preserving DDx's claim, evidence, review, commit, and cleanup contracts.

PROPOSED FIX
Add an explicit opt-in `in-tree` attempt backend for `ddx try` and `ddx work`. It should run the agent in the project checkout itself, not in a linked worktree, local clone, or Docker clone. The backend must be conservative because it trades isolation for access to local data:
- require a clean git index/worktree before claim, except for DDx-owned tracker/worker metadata that the orchestrator intentionally writes;
- take a per-project exclusive worker lock so two in-tree attempts cannot run concurrently in the same checkout;
- record the same attempt evidence and routing/review metadata as isolated backends;
- after success, stage and commit only files actually changed by the attempt plus the associated tracker/evidence changes;
- on failure, unclaim/release the bead and leave an explicit rescue message if the tree is dirty rather than silently discarding edits;
- keep the default backend unchanged (`worktree`) so parallel code-editing remains isolated by default.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing the default backend.
- Making in-tree attempts safe for concurrent workers in one checkout.
- Solving the existing `docker-clone` remote-daemon bind-mount defect; that is tracked separately by ddx-6967f6e6.
- Changing Fizeau model routing.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/work.go`
- `cli/cmd/try.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/*attempt*` and adjacent tests that select/setup attempt backends
- `docs/agent-execute.md`

acceptance:
1. TestReadinessChecksSchema in cli/internal/agent/readiness_schema_test.go validates every verdict form the decoder accepts (bool true, bool false, string, null, absent) against cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json AND asserts an object verdict is rejected, locking producer/consumer alignment.
2. The producer prompt buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:636-655) references the canonical schema path via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21), not a duplicated string literal.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes
description:
PROBLEM
The pre_claim_intake readiness payload schema can drift between the producer prompt and the Go decoder. That drift is exactly the class of bug that wedged the fizeau queue: the producer emitted readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string, so every claim attempt failed with a warn-only rejection and the queue idled. There must be a single shared schema that both sides consume, plus a CI-run test that fails the moment the schema and decoder diverge.

ROOT CAUSE
- The producer prompt references the schema only by a path string in prose: cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639 (buildPreClaimIntakePrompt) writes "Canonical schema: "+readinessChecksSchemaPath, and the constant lives at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21. Because the prompt text and the schema file are independent, instructions can drift from the schema with nothing catching it.
- The consumer decoder accepts bool/string/null/absent verdict forms: readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125 and preClaimReadinessChecksPayload.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416.
- The schema verdict union is cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json:44-50 (oneOf boolean/string/null). Nothing fails CI if someone tightens the schema, or changes the decoder, so that the two diverge.

PROPOSED FIX
Keep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth consumed by both the producer prompt (via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant) and the consumer decoder. Ensure a CI-run test validates every verdict form the decoder accepts against the schema and asserts that an object verdict is rejected, so any tightening of the schema or change to the decoder that breaks alignment fails the suite. Confirm the producer prompt references the canonical path constant rather than a duplicated literal.

NON-SCOPE
- Do NOT implement the runtime same-fingerprint warn escalation guard (owned by the sibling child bead).
- Do NOT change the verdict coercion semantics (bool true->pass, bool false->fail, string->lowercased, null/absent->empty).
- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.

DEPENDENCIES: no deps.
title: Lock readiness_checks schema as the shared producer/consumer source of truth with a CI drift gate

acceptance:
1. The docker-clone backend (cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:293) configures process-group and/or container teardown so its nested harness is reaped on worker exit, including abnormal exit. TestDockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup asserts the docker command is started with cmdSetProcessGroup applied (Setpgid set) and/or that container teardown runs on all exit paths.
2. If container teardown already guarantees reaping without process-group handling, the test instead documents and asserts the unconditional teardown path (e.g. --rm plus an always-run dockerRemoveContainer/`docker stop`), with the justifying code lines cited.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The docker-clone attempt backend spawns the per-attempt `docker` invocation WITHOUT a process group, so even graceful worker cleanup may not reach the harness running nested inside the container. On abnormal or even graceful worker exit, the container (and the harness it hosts) can be left running, mirroring the PID-1 orphan leak seen for the worktree/local-clone backends. (worktree/local-clone backends route through cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156-157 and DO get Setpgid via cmdSetProcessGroup; the docker path does not.)

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:293 — DockerClone backend builds `cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "docker", args...)` with NO call to cmdSetProcessGroup, unlike the executor spawn path. The only teardown is dockerRemoveContainer at attempt_backend.go:299-301, gated on ctx.Err() != nil, which does not run when the parent dies abnormally.
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:120-122 (worktree Run) and :182-184 (local-clone Run) dispatch through the executor and inherit Setpgid; the docker backend bypasses that path.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the docker-clone backend reap its nested harness on worker exit: apply cmdSetProcessGroup to the docker invocation at attempt_backend.go:293, and/or run the container with a teardown guarantee (run with --rm and ensure dockerRemoveContainer / `docker stop` runs on ALL exit paths, not only ctx.Err() != nil) so the container tree is torn down when the worker exits. If existing container teardown (e.g. --rm + an unconditional stop on cleanup) already guarantees reaping, document that justification with the specific code lines instead of adding redundant process-group handling.

NON-SCOPE
- The Pdeathsig change in cmdSetProcessGroup and the startup orphan reaper are separate children.
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown of the worktree/local-clone executor path (executor.go:231-255) is out of scope.
- The harness-runner-INTERNAL leak is covered by fizeau-50b6f055.

CROSS-REF: fizeau-50b6f055.
title: execute-bead: give the docker-clone attempt backend process-group/container cleanup so its nested harness is reaped on worker exit

2. Path A run: each fixture's bead replayed via ddx try (or ddx work --once) on a branch from base-rev~1; outcomes captured.
3. Path B run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with bead's auto-prompt verbatim; outcomes captured.

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).
description:
Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.
notes: Partial Path B-vs-C evidence: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome, supporting the bead-quality hypothesis side of the study.
title: study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)

acceptance:
1. TestExecuteBead_IndexLockNotHeldAcrossSubprocess asserts that during an execute-bead run with a mocked long-running harness subprocess, .git/index.lock does not exist (or its mtime is older than the subprocess start) for the duration of the subprocess wait.
2. TestExecuteBead_TrackerLockNotHeldAcrossSubprocess asserts the same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock.
3. TestExecuteBead_LocksReacquiredForMutation asserts the locks ARE acquired (briefly) when the post-subprocess git stage/commit and tracker update execute.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch holds .git/index.lock and .ddx/.git-tracker.lock for the entire execute-bead lifetime, including the multi-minute LLM subprocess wait. Observed 2026-05-17: PID 90753 held .git/index.lock for 6+ minutes while a claude subprocess ran, blocking concurrent bead create/update/close operations with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed)' errors.

ROOT CAUSE
The execute-bead path in cli/cmd/work.go and the tracker mutation path in cli/internal/bead/ acquire the git index lock / .git-tracker.lock once at the start of a worker iteration and release only at the end, spanning the subprocess.Wait() on the agent harness. Search for lock acquisition sites in cli/cmd/work.go (worker loop), cli/cmd/run.go (run harness invocation), and cli/internal/bead/store.go (tracker write path) — the os.Open/flock or LockFile call wrapping the harness invocation is the offender. Executing agent must grep for index.lock / git-tracker.lock acquisition and confirm exact file:line.

PROPOSED FIX
Refactor so each lock is acquired immediately before its corresponding git/tracker mutation and released immediately after. The LLM subprocess invocation (claude/codex/gemini exec.Cmd.Run or .Wait) must run with no DDx-held mutation lock. Stage-and-commit operations should batch mutations to minimize re-acquire churn.

NON-SCOPE
- Metric emission (separate child)
- Hold-time cap enforcement (separate child)
- Multi-worker concurrency test (separate child)
- Documentation (separate child)
- Changes to lock implementation itself (flock vs file-based); only acquisition scope is in scope here.

CROSS-REF
Parent observation from Fizeau alignment-review 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo).
title: scope git index.lock and tracker.lock to mutation window, not LLM subprocess lifetime
