description:
PROBLEM (regression found live 2026-06-03; D3 ddx-bd47e2c4 shipped this incomplete)
Operator-reopening a consecutive-wedge-parked bead via `ddx bead update <id> --status open` does NOT clear the work-consecutive-wedges marker, so on the next claim the loop's consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1745) reads the stale count>=threshold and instantly re-parks the bead ("consecutive-wedge guard: <id> wedged 2 consecutive claims; flagged for operator attention"), WITHOUT attempting it. Observed: 7 beads un-parked with --status open all re-wedged at claim until the marker was manually `--unset work-consecutive-wedges`. D3's AC #2 ("a reopened bead is claimable and attempted, not instantly re-parked") is therefore NOT satisfied in production even though the bead closed.

ROOT CAUSE
clearConsecutiveWedge (execute_bead_loop.go ~5844) is only called on a clean guard-free completion (~2604). Nothing clears the marker (or makes consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips return false) when a bead transitions back to an execution-eligible state by OPERATOR action (status proposed/blocked -> open). The reopen and the marker live in different layers (store update vs loop guard state).

PROPOSED FIX
When the loop encounters a ready bead whose status was reopened by an operator after the wedge was recorded (e.g. the wedge marker's timestamp predates the bead's last status->open transition, OR a dedicated reopened-since marker), consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips must return false AND clearConsecutiveWedge must run so the bead gets a fresh attempt. Simplest robust option: clear the work-consecutive-wedges marker at the store layer whenever a bead is transitioned to status=open from proposed/blocked (so any reopen path — CLI or programmatic — drops the stale wedge state). Add the operator-reopen clear so no manual --unset is ever needed.

ACCEPTANCE
1. New test: a bead with work-consecutive-wedges{count>=threshold} transitioned proposed->open (operator reopen) has NO wedge marker afterward (cleared at the store transition), OR consecutiveWedgeGuardTrips returns false for it on the immediately-following claim. Assert the loop ATTEMPTS it (claims + dispatches) rather than re-parking.
2. Regression: simulate the exact observed sequence (record wedge count=2, set status=open, run one drain iteration) and assert the bead is claimed/attempted, not re-flagged operator-attention.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: ddx work: operator-reopening a wedge-parked bead must clear the wedge marker (D3 follow-up; live regression)

description:
ExecuteBeadWorker.Run() is a 700-line function with 24 returns: 4 legitimate terminal exits, 1 legacy idle-loop exit, 1 routing-preflight-kills-worker exit, and 18 store-error escalations from the same handleOutcomeStoreError pattern repeated across outcome paths. The implicit state machine in the loop is real and coherent (Pick -> Gate -> Claim -> Execute -> Classify -> Reviewing -> Terminal) but written as conditional soup instead of explicit state.

## Three-package split aligned to FEAT-010

  cli/internal/agent/run/   - Layer 1: invocation atom (existing dispatch + runner)
  cli/internal/agent/try/   - Layer 2: bead attempt (worktree, prompt, single Execute, Land, conflict-recovery, post-merge review, triage)
  cli/internal/agent/work/  - Layer 3: queue drain (pick, claim, heartbeat, drain-loop control, stop conditions)

try.Attempt(beadID) returns sealed Outcome { Disposition (enum), Cooldown, ParkReason, RecordEvents }. Loop becomes approximately 30 lines: pick -> guard -> claim -> attemptWithHeartbeat -> applyOutcome. Single commitOutcome helper deletes the 18 store-error returns.

## Ordering: refactor-LAST per user decision

In-flight Group A beads (c6e3db02 rate-limit, b24e9630 no_changes, cfedee8e ladder wire, a8718bec cost-cap reviewer) land first against current code. dc157075 tactical worker-stay-alive lands first as operational bridge. The stdout/watch-mode beads (ddx-7d9e1e82, ddx-f9acb86d, ddx-7dcbd40b, ddx-8b7ed960, ddx-dc300be9) define the public work-loop contract this refactor must preserve. THIS refactor lands last and absorbs all intervening behavior into the new architecture. Per-child ACs explicitly call out which in-flight bead's behavior each child must preserve.

## Naming and public semantics

Package = try (not try_bead - Go idiom). Function = try.Attempt(beadID). Loop = work.Worker.Drain. CLI surface is ddx run / ddx try / ddx work. ddx work has three modes: once (one ready bead then exit), drain (default: run ready work until no execution-ready beads remain, then exit), and watch (stay alive indefinitely, sleep for idle interval when no execution-ready beads exist, and poll for newly arriving work). The removed polling flag is not preserved as a public compatibility alias; the durable vocabulary is mode plus idle_interval.

## Children

  C0  Pre-refactor JSONL fixture baseline for event-shape regression detection
  C1  try.Outcome sealed sum type + Disposition enum + ParkReason vocabulary enumeration
  C2  try.Attempt function shell (wraps existing executor; no behavior change)
  C3  Move review verdict handling into try.Attempt (180 lines from loop body)
  C4  Move conflict recovery into try.Attempt
  C5  Move no_changes adjudication, decomposition, push-failed, push-conflict (absorbs ddx-b24e9630, ddx-c6e3db02 behavior)
  C6  Single commitOutcome helper; deletes 18 store-error exit paths + handleOutcomeStoreError
  C7  Uniform Guard contract; delete attempted/hookFailed maps; pick() derives from drain.result
  C8  Routing preflight moves to Drain startup (runs once, not per iteration)
  C9  First-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation + downstream-consumer audit for execution_failed:max-cost grep before relocation. Includes queue snapshot plumbing and human-review blocker pressure from ddx-7dcbd40b.
  C10 ddx work once/drain/watch modes + transient idle substate. Watch mode owns idle interval sleeps, per-idle queue status, and next-ready transition messaging from ddx-f9acb86d and ddx-8b7ed960.
  C11 Heartbeat extracted to withHeartbeat helper
  C12 Single phase-event emission (replaces 4 sites)
  C13 File and identifier rename per package split + ADD ddx try CLI command + handle dc157075 file-path migration if it landed first
  C14 Delete ExecuteBead* shims; verify all callers ported off (grep audit)

title: REFACTOR: execute-bead surface → try / work packages aligned to FEAT-010

description:
PROBLEM
The landed zero-footprint refactor introduced `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)` and a pre-commit audit, but the audit only catches `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...)`. Production code still has direct `.ddx` state-root consumers that will keep reading or committing the project worktree instead of the XDG state repository in convention mode.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1791-1837` stats the tracker via `ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, "beads.jsonl")`, but then checks/stages/commits literal `.ddx/beads.jsonl` in `projectRoot`. In convention mode that never commits the XDG tracker repo.
`cli/internal/attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:19-101` defines `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` as a relative constant and joins it directly under `projectRoot`, bypassing `ddxroot`.
`cli/cmd/work_plan.go:82-87` and `cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go:46-47` open `projectRoot + "/.ddx"` directly, so convention-mode projects show empty or wrong queues.
The new audit command in `lefthook.yml` only searches for `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx")`, so these regressions are invisible to pre-commit.

PROPOSED FIX
Route the concrete residual state-root consumers above through `ddxroot.Path` / `ddxroot.JoinProject`. When committing tracker state in convention mode, run git in the actual DDx state root repository and stage `beads.jsonl` relative to that repo; preserve existing in-tree behavior and execute-bead merge-policy commits. Update the audit so it fails on production direct state-root patterns such as `projectRoot + "/.ddx"`, `WorkingDir + "/.ddx"`, and constants joined under projectRoot for DDx-managed state, while still allowing relative artifact path strings used inside worker prompts and git pathspec filters.

NON-SCOPE
Do not migrate this repository out of in-tree mode. Do not rewrite prompt text that intentionally tells agents where evidence appears in an execution worktree. Do not change the bead storage format or attachment externalization policy.
title: ddx home: route residual state-root consumers through ddxroot

description:
PROBLEM
ddx work's readiness check emits a misleading parse error during routine bead reads, blaming a specific line number that is NOT actually corrupt. Observed in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau today at 19:25:58 during pickup of fizeau-8ebac59d:

  19:25:58 readiness check: starting
  bead: read line 18: bead: unmarshal: unexpected end of JSON input
  23:26:03 readiness route claude/opus reason=policy=default; score=99.5

The route still succeeded — the error is non-fatal — but the operator-facing log line tells them line 18 of .ddx/beads.jsonl is corrupt when it is not. python3 json.loads on every line of the 210-line file returns no errors; line 18 specifically is a 10441-byte fully-valid JSON record.

ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis — verify in fix)
The "line 18" in the error message is almost certainly NOT a line in the on-disk JSONL file. Plausible causes:
1. A relative buffer offset inside a streaming json.Decoder that has been mistaken for a file line number when formatting the error.
2. A partial read race: the tracker is being read concurrently with a write (auto-doc-stamp from `ddx bead create`/`ddx bead update`), and the reader hit EOF mid-record. The "line 18" is the line index inside the partial buffer, not the file.
3. A bead's events array contains an embedded string with newlines that the reader's line counter is tokenizing on, throwing off the line index.

The error is non-fatal because the reader presumably skips the bad bead and continues — but it surfaces to the operator as if the file itself is corrupt, prompting them to inspect / fsck / restore a perfectly good tracker file.

REPRO
Observable any time `ddx work` (and likely `ddx bead list`, `ddx work plan`, etc.) reads the tracker while another `ddx bead create`/`update`/`dep add` is mid-write. Most reliably reproducible on a project with active auto-doc-stamp commits flying (i.e., any project running `ddx work` continuously). The 210-line fizeau beads.jsonl reproduces it under contention.

PROPOSED FIX
1. **Fix the error formatting.** The error should report either:
   - The actual on-disk line number where the parse failed (compute via byte offset / newline scan).
   - OR "(partial record at byte offset N)" if a streaming decoder hit EOF mid-record.
   - Never report a number that is not a real file line, especially when telling operators to "reconcile manually."
2. **Distinguish "transient partial read" from "persistent corruption."** A partial read during concurrent write should be retried (with a short backoff or via a read lock around the tracker file). A genuine corruption should fail the read and surface a louder error. Today both look identical to the operator.
3. **Add coverage.** Test that simulates concurrent write+read on a tracker file (write a long record byte-by-byte while the reader does a full scan) and asserts the reader either retries cleanly OR reports an accurate diagnostic. Failure: today's behavior of "line 18: unmarshal: unexpected end of JSON input" against a non-line-18 problem.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing JSONL with a different tracker format.
- Adding a file lock around all tracker writes (probably needed eventually but out of scope here — this bead is strictly about diagnostics + transient-read handling).
- The actual readiness-check race condition that caused the read in the first place — that's incidental; any concurrent reader would hit it.

PARENT AND DEPS
No parent. No deps.

EVIDENCE FILE
Worker log preserved at /tmp/fizeau-worker-1778973834.log (line containing "bead: read line 18: bead: unmarshal: unexpected end of JSON input"). Companion file: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/beads.jsonl, 210 lines, all of which parse cleanly via `python3 -c "import json, sys; [json.loads(l) for l in open('.ddx/beads.jsonl')]"`.
title: bead tracker: 'bead: read line N: bead: unmarshal: unexpected end of JSON input' fires when JSONL is well-formed

3. In-tree mode end-to-end test: run `ddx init` in a fresh git repo, verify .ddx/ created in worktree, verify subsequent commands route there.

description:
EPIC

Eliminate the .ddx/ footprint from arbitrary git repos using DDx, while keeping today's in-tree behavior available for projects that want it (DDx itself, HELIX, etc.). Make init effectively touchless: zero ceremony for the common case.

DESIGN PRINCIPLE

Single rule: presence of <project>/.ddx/ determines the DDx root.
- If <project>/.ddx/ exists → in-tree mode. Root = <project>/.ddx/. Operator commits or gitignores per their own .gitignore. DDx never manipulates .gitignore.
- If <project>/.ddx/ does NOT exist → convention mode. Root = ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/). The XDG directory is itself a git repo containing all DDx state for that project; the project's own git repo stays pristine.

PROJECT ID is the human-readable remote URL host/owner/repo triple, parsed from `git remote get-url origin`. SSH URLs (`git@github.com:easel/ddx.git`) and HTTPS URLs both resolve to the same path. Projects without a remote fall back to `local/<basename>-<abs-path-short-hash>/`. The slashes are filesystem-real, so the XDG path is browsable: `~/.local/share/ddx/projects/github.com/easel/ddx/`.

OVERRIDE PRECEDENCE at command time:
1. In-tree (<project>/.ddx/config.yaml) — top priority
2. Global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml) — installed by `ddx init --global`
3. Convention defaults — baked into the binary

Each layer is a delta on the one below. A project with no overrides commits zero new files and writes zero files into its worktree.

ENGINEERING IMPERATIVE

EVERY path in the codebase that today reads or writes under .ddx/ must route through a single DDxRoot(ctx) resolver. No filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) calls outside that resolver. A regression linter asserts this invariant. The audit is the hard part of this epic.

KNOWN-HARD QUESTION (worth solving but not blocking):

When multiple worktrees of the same project (same remote URL) exist on one machine — e.g. `~/Projects/ddx` and `~/work/ddx-experiment` — they share the same XDG state. SHAs in events are unambiguous, but operator UX for "where do I cd to inspect this" is. Solution direction: each XDG project keeps a worktrees.json registry. One entry is marked master (operator pin or first-seen). Cross-references in audit logs and bead show output cite the master path.

CHILD BEADS

- E1: config: DDxRoot resolver + audit every .ddx/ path writer
- E2: config: convention mode bootstrap (XDG git repo + worktree registry)
- E3: config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas
- E4: config: ddx init creates .ddx/ in project, switches to in-tree mode
- E5: config: optional .ddx/config.yaml overlay for per-project overrides

NON-SCOPE
- Migration of existing in-tree repos (today's DDx repo, HELIX) is a separate concern. They stay in-tree mode by virtue of having .ddx/ already.
- Server-backed state (federation) is orthogonal. XDG state can be a git repo with a remote; pushing that remote IS the team-share mechanism.
- Manifest-only mode is rejected — the convention/in-tree split makes a separate manifest file unnecessary.
title: config: XDG-rooted DDx state — convention mode + in-tree mode + touchless init

description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal a readiness_checks response when an entry's verdict is a JSON bool. The worker logs the warn-only rejection 'decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string' and skips the bead. When the faulty payload is produced for every candidate, the whole queue idles (observed Fizeau 2026-05-18, identical fingerprint 15155a1c... across distinct bead_ids).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:75-80 defines preClaimReadinessCheck with 'Verdict string `json:"verdict,omitempty"`' (line 77). preClaimReadinessChecksPayload.UnmarshalJSON (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:322-352) decodes each entry into preClaimReadinessCheck via json.Unmarshal at lines 331 (array case) and 334 (singleton-object case). When the producer emits verdict as a JSON bool (true/false) instead of a string, json.Unmarshal returns the type error, the decode aborts, and the payload is rejected as system_unready.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the verdict field tolerant: introduce a custom JSON type (e.g. a 'readinessVerdict' string newtype with UnmarshalJSON, or a custom UnmarshalJSON on preClaimReadinessCheck) that accepts a JSON bool, a JSON string, or an absent/null value and coerces to a documented canonical string form:
  - JSON bool true  -> "pass"
  - JSON bool false -> "fail"
  - JSON string     -> trimmed value, passed through unchanged (e.g. "pass", "fail", "ready", "not_ready")
  - absent / null   -> "" (empty, same as today)
The downstream fail-detection failedReadinessReasons (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:782-794) already lower/trim-compares Verdict to "fail", so false->"fail" keeps fail semantics intact; true->"pass" is a non-fail verdict. Document the bool->string mapping in a doc comment on the verdict type.

NON-SCOPE
Do not add the shared JSON schema / CI drift gate (separate child), the same-fingerprint escalation (separate child), the claim-success-rate signal (separate child), or README docs (separate child). Only the decoder tolerance + its unit test.
notes: Salvaged from wedged ddx try (ddx-8f2e0ebf repro). Fix=readinessVerdict newtype (bool true->pass/false->fail, string trimmed passthrough, absent->""); array+singleton cases. Agent used --no-verify; verified independently: TestPreClaimReadinessCheck_VerdictAcceptsBoolStringAbsent green incl. false->fail regression (AC4); full go test ./internal/agent/... green (AC5); go build ./... ok. Landed at merge 854ef1817 (fix commit 4d3f829c0).
title: pre-claim intake decoder: accept readiness_checks[].verdict as JSON bool, string, or absent

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx server install` installs a singleton per-user service, but until this fix its default `WorkingDirectory` and log path were derived from the DDx project root that happened to run the install. In practice, the host had a single `ddx-server.service` rooted at `/home/erik/Projects/axon`; reinstalling from `/home/erik/Projects/ddx` merely moved the singleton to another project, which is still wrong. A per-user multi-project server must not be bound by default to Axon, DDx, or any other project checkout.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/server_service.go:49-68` defaulted service config to `CommandFactory.WorkingDir`, and `cli/internal/service/systemd.go:21-27` wrote that into `WorkingDirectory` plus project-scoped log paths. The multi-project server contract in `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-002-server.md:21-33` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-019-multi-project-server-topology.md:349-373` says the long-lived service is per-user and stores registry state under XDG, but the service-manager section still preserved the older project-root default. Separately, `cli/internal/service/systemd.go:92-96` used `systemctl --user enable --now`, which rewrites the unit but does not restart an already-active service, so even a corrected unit can leave the old project-rooted process running.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make default service installs use a neutral user working directory (`$HOME`) and user-scoped logs (`$XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx/logs/ddx-server.log` or `~/.local/share/ddx/logs/ddx-server.log`).
- Keep `--workdir` only as an explicit compatibility/debug override, not as the default path.
- Update systemd install so active reinstalls restart after `daemon-reload`; inactive installs enable then start.
- Update FEAT-002 and SD-019 so service-manager docs align with the per-user multi-project topology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign project registry routing or worker scheduling in this bead.
- Do not change server API compatibility aliases.
- Do not migrate or mutate unrelated project trackers.

PARENT/DEPS
No parent. No deps.

Tests: targeted server/service tests pass; lefthook pre-commit pass before commit; broad cmd package still reports unrelated TempDir cleanup failure in TestWorkAttemptMetricsAutoCommitted.
title: server install: default singleton service to user scope and restart active unit

2. The readiness prompt/skill contract tells the model that newer operator notes, reopen notes, or unblocked notes supersede older blocker events unless current evidence revalidates the blocker.

description:
PROBLEM
The readiness hook can over-weight stale external-blocker history and under-weight explicit operator notes that say the blocker was cleared. This produces misleading `intake.warn` text and can steer an implementation attempt toward obsolete infrastructure conclusions even when the bead is intentionally unblocked for forward progress.

OBSERVED EVIDENCE
Niflheim bead `niflheim-439a49ae` has notes saying: `Unblocked 2026-05-18: the prior Redpanda/AIO blocker was stale after commit 90cd8348 bounded Redpanda test storage with an owned tmpfs mount... The container became ready and was cleaned up.` During `ddx work --once` on 2026-05-21, readiness still emitted `bead readiness blocked: redpanda environment can support 32-partition stress test... prior_attempts show a hard external blocker: Redpanda fails to start due to AIO slot exhaustion on WSL2`. The attempt continued because readiness was warn-only, but the warning contradicted current bead notes and polluted the agent context.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:60` includes bead notes in the triage/readiness payload model, and `cli/internal/agent/types.go:298` carries notes in the execute-bead payload, but the readiness prompt/contract does not require stale prior blockers to be discounted when a later note explicitly clears them. The current tests in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go` cover warn-only continuation and intake event recording, but not precedence between later unblocked notes and older `no_changes_blocked`/external-blocker events.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the readiness prompt/contract and pre-claim intake tests so later explicit operator notes or reopen/unblocked notes supersede older external-blocker events unless current evidence revalidates the blocker. Readiness may still mention stale blocker history as historical context, but it must not phrase the bead as currently blocked or require operator action when a newer note says the blocker was cleared and no fresh recheck failed.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing lifecycle status semantics for actual `status=blocked` beads.
- Suppressing all prior-attempt history.
- Removing warn-only readiness behavior.
- Niflheim benchmark implementation.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go`
- `cli/internal/agent/bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go`
- DDx bead-lifecycle skill text under `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` if the prompt contract lives there.
- Any pre-claim intake prompt assembly code that formats notes/events for readiness.

title: readiness: discount stale external blockers cleared by newer notes

description:
PROBLEM
In the recommended "let fizeau route" mode (no --harness/--model/--profile pin), the worker's liveness record keeps harness/model/route EMPTY for the whole attempt: liveness.SetAttempt is called once pre-dispatch with the REQUESTED (empty) pins and never updated with the route fizeau resolves mid-stream. The progress watchdog (cli/internal/agent/work/watchdog.go:50 phaseEmpty) treats harness==""&&model==""&&route=="" as a wedge; at the 30-min Running budget it cancels the HEALTHY attempt, which trips the consecutive-wedge guard (threshold 2) and parks the bead to operator-attention. This is the dominant false-wedge (16 beads carry needs-human-reason=consecutive_wedge). fizeau v0.14.41 already fixed the routing-side codex false-reject; this is the ddx-side half.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2440 calls liveness.SetAttempt(candidate.ID, provAttemptID, PhaseRunning, profile, harness, model, profile, 0) — the 4th arg (route) is "profile" (with no profile pin it is ""), harness/model are the empty requested pins. fizeau emits the resolved route in-stream as agentlib.ServiceRoutingDecisionData (Harness/Provider/Model), decoded in drainServiceEventsWithRenderer at cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:257 (no-watchdog fast path) AND :388 (drain-watchdog path), but nothing propagates it back to the loop's liveness reporter (set up at execute_bead_loop.go:1116).

PROPOSED FIX (the watchdog is already correct in spirit — it resets when a non-empty route field appears; only the route is never recorded)
1. Add to cli/internal/agent/work/liveness.go: func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) UpdateRoute(beadID, harness, model, route string), guarded on r.rec.CurrentBead==beadID, overwriting only non-empty fields, same mutex discipline as SetAttempt.
2. Add field to AgentRunRuntime (cli/internal/agent/types.go): OnRouteResolved func(harness, provider, model string).
3. In drainServiceEventsWithRenderer (agent_runner_service.go), add an onRouteResolved callback param, invoke it in BOTH "case decoded.RoutingDecision != nil" branches (:257 and :388) with decoded.RoutingDecision.Harness/Provider/Model. Update both callers: service_run.go:294 passes runtime.OnRouteResolved; agent_runner_service.go:150 (runAgentViaService / ddx run) passes nil.
4. Thread the callback from the loop to where AgentRunRuntime is built (execute_bead.go:1666 runRuntime): pass OnRouteResolved through agenttry.AttemptOpts -> Executor -> execute_bead. In the loop near execute_bead_loop.go:2440 set it to func(h,p,m string){ liveness.UpdateRoute(candidate.ID, h, m, p) }. Also fix the SetAttempt 4th arg so route is not "profile".
5. Do NOT change phaseEmpty or LastActivityAt logic: OnTick advances LastActivityAt unconditionally (wall-clock, liveness.go:87-97); resetting the watchdog on heartbeat-advance would DISABLE it (reintroduces the 80-min wedge). The fix is recording the route, nothing else.

NON-SCOPE: D1b/D1c/D1d/D2/D3 are separate beads. Do not weaken the watchdog or consecutive-wedge guard.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestSidecarLiveness_UpdateRoute (cli/internal/agent/work/liveness_test.go): after SetAttempt(bead,...,route="",harness="",model=""), UpdateRoute(bead,"fiz","sonnet-4.6","anthropic") makes Snapshot() report non-empty Harness/Model/Route; CurrentBead mismatch is a no-op; empty args do not blank existing values.
2. Test asserts OnRouteResolved fires once with the resolved triple in BOTH drainServiceEventsWithRenderer branches (no-watchdog + drain-watchdog) before Final — stub a ServiceEvent channel emitting RoutingDecision then Final.
3. Watchdog test: an attempt with populated route fields past the 30-min Running budget is NOT flagged wedged (progressWatchdog.observe returns fired=false); the existing all-empty frozen case still fires.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/work/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: execute-bead: record fizeau-resolved route in liveness so the watchdog stops false-killing no-pin attempts (D1)

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-f9ddaa68 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the same warn-only rejection, so the worker logs a warning and skips. With 23 ready beads in the fizeau queue and one fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue is idle indefinitely.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC; about an hour after the previous wedge bug ddx-8f2e0ebf)
- Worker: PID 1527216, ddx work --watch in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, etime 01:22:40 at observation.
- Log: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779077845655338391.jsonl
- Pattern every ~50s: resource.preflight (status ok) -> pre_claim_intake.start -> pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle.
- Sample pre_claim_intake.warn payload:
  {
    "bead_id":"fizeau-5b2a8950",
    "decision":"warn",
    "decision_source":"pre_claim_intake",

    "fingerprint":"15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f",
    "outcome":"intake_error",
    "policy_mode":"warn-only",
    "reason":"system_unready",
    "rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready",

    "system_reason":"unavailable"
  }

- Queue state at observation: open=44, ready=23, dependency-waiting=19. None of the 23 ready beads got claimed in the prior 30 minutes; commit log empty for the same window.
- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Either the producer or consumer changed without coordination.
2. warn-only policy_mode masks the failure: the worker keeps polling and looks alive (loop.idle every 50s) but never claims anything. Operator has no signal beyond tailing the agent-loop log.
3. The 'suggested_action' text ('check the
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- CI schema gate: the readiness_checks JSON schema is defined in a shared place; producer and consumer both consume it; CI fails on schema drift.
- Same-fingerprint escalation: after N (default 5) consecutive warn outcomes with the same fingerprint, the worker emits an operator-attention event and stops looping silently. Tested by injecting a faulty readiness payload and asserting the escalation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
The work applies bead-scoped cooldowns to infra-class failures, contradicting documented policy and violating the forward-progress invariant. Observed today: 14 beads all routed to a temporarily-unreachable LM Studio host received 15-min cooldowns in sequence, then the worker went idle with the entire queue gated for 15 minutes despite cloud providers being reachable. A separate worker also hit a 2nd-strike preclaim cooldown after .ddx/beads.jsonl was momentarily staged between two parallel workers' tracker commits — recoverable in milliseconds, but the policy parked the bead for minutes anyway. Both cases produced the failure the operator named: "cooling down every bead in the queue when we are failing to find a provider, how is that going to fix anything?"

ROOT CAUSE — code violates existing spec
The forward-progress invariant and the right cooldown policy are already documented; the code does not honor them.

- reliability-principles.md P6 (docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md:35): "Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race -> no cooldown, return to ready." Today's code violates this for `provider_connectivity` and `no_viable_provider`.
- ADR-024 §Infrastructure And Rate-Limit Fallback (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:96): "Infrastructure failures are not capability failures... do not consume escalation budget... apply a retry-after cooldown only when time passing could plausibly fix the same attempt class." Loose "or" wording lets the current implementation skirt the intent.
- TD-031 §3.1 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:75): "Continuous forward progress is the default. Before a bead can move to `status=proposed`, the drain loop MUST exhaust every applicable automatic path in priority order." Says MAY set retry-after for `no_viable_provider`; the code unconditionally sets 15 min regardless of whether alternative routes exist.

Implementation sites that violate the spec:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2283-2284 — provider_connectivity sets bead cooldown unconditionally after applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion already recorded the failed route. With ddx-71629938 + fizeau-8134d6db landed, the exclusion is the correct mechanism; the cooldown is now redundant deadweight.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2294-2306 — no_viable_provider sets bead cooldown unconditionally; should transition the WORKER to a paused state (like paused-quota), not penalize the bead.
- cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:96-107 — preclaim-hook-failed sets bead cooldown on 2nd strike regardless of whether the cause is bead-specific (real divergence) or systemic (transient tracker race / index lock). The existing isSystemicPreClaimError path correctly avoids cooldown for global failures; staged-tracker / index-lock races belong in the same class.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:~968 (NoReadyWork branch) — when every ready bead is in cooldown for the same infra reason, the worker goes idle instead of transitioning to a paused-infra state and resuming when the condition clears. Violates "forward progress in all possible scenarios."

PROPOSED FIX
1. Restore code-to-spec conformance for infra-class outcomes:
   - provider_connectivity: drop SetExecutionCooldown call; the work-failed-routes entry + RouteExclusionWindow TTL is the correct mechanism. Bead is immediately reclaimable; next attempt routes around the failed (provider, model).
   - no_viable_provider: drop SetExecutionCooldown; instead transition the WORKER to paused-infra (new state), poll every 2 min, leave every bead immediately reclaimable.
   - preclaim-hook-failed: classify staged-.ddx/beads.jsonl-only and index-lock-races as systemic-transient (existing isSystemicPreClaimError path); skip cycle without cooldown. Only bead-specific persistent failures (across distinct attempts) trigger cooldown.
2. Add paused-infra worker state to TD-031 §4 with entry/exit conditions mirroring paused-quota. Surface in workers panel (P5 operator-visible state) with resume-at timestamp.
3. Worker idle-check (execute_bead_loop.go:~968): before returning NoReadyWork, scan whether every active cooldown shares an infra-fault status. If yes, transition to paused-infra with the earliest cooldown.until as the resume target. Worker sleeps until then, then re-evaluates the full queue.
4. Tighten loose spec wording:
   - TD-031 §3.1 line 73 — "MAY set retry-after (15 min)" for no_viable_provider becomes "MAY set retry-after only when no alternative routing path exists and the condition is purely time-dependent; when alternate routes exist, leave bead immediately reclaimable."
   - ADR-024:96 — replace ambiguous "or" with a crisp rule: cooldown applies only when no alternate route exists AND the condition is purely time-dependent.
5. Operator escape hatch: `ddx bead clear-cooldown [--all] [--reason <class>]` clears work-retry-after on matching beads. Today this requires per-bead `ddx bead update <id> --unset work-retry-after` (14 invocations today).

NON-SCOPE
- Escalation ladder behavior (ddx-8a7a6843 already in progress).
- Fizeau-side route health (fizeau-8134d6db already shipped in v0.14.0).
- Cooldown for genuinely bead-faulting outcomes (no_progress, no_changes with rationale, repair_cycle_exhausted) stays unchanged — those are documented bead-fault cases where time passing may help.
title: work loop: rework cooldown policy — code violates documented forward-progress invariant

acceptance:
1. 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.
3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

acceptance:
1. DDx stores or forwards Fizeau ServiceEvents without mutating their payloads; TestServiceRun_ForwardsOpaqueFizeauEvents covers this with an unknown future event payload.
2. DDx final adapter decodes only final status, output, token/cost, actual route, and session artifact fields needed for DDx Result/indexes; TestServiceRun_FinalResultProjectionOnly covers the allowed fields.
3. Local DDx copies of Fizeau service-event names/partial schemas are removed or restricted to tests; production uses Fizeau-provided typed decoders when decoding is required.
4. DDx does not reconstruct tool-call transcripts from Fizeau events for policy decisions.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server -run 'Test.*Service|Test.*Run|Test.*Worker|Test.*ToolCall|Test.*Opaque' -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx service-run code still understands too much of Fizeau's inner event stream. It should forward/store Fizeau ServiceEvents opaquely and adapt only the final data needed for DDx Result bookkeeping.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:243-355 switches on raw service-event names and reconstructs transcript/progress/tool-call records.
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go also owns DDx-specific fallback policy for internal Fizeau events that should not drive DDx runtime outcomes.
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go still exposes service event handling surfaces that make it easy for DDx callers to couple to Fizeau internals.

PROPOSED FIX
Collapse DDx's Fizeau service consumption to two responsibilities: store/forward opaque ServiceEvents unchanged, and decode only final-result/routing/session artifact fields required to populate DDx Result and indexes. Prefer Fizeau typed decoders such as agentlib.DecodeServiceEvent where decoding is necessary; remove local stringly-typed event constants and custom partial structs that duplicate Fizeau schema.

NON-SCOPE
- Fizeau event schema changes.
- Deleting the legacy direct Claude parser; ddx-64c24626 handles that after opaque forwarding is in place.
- Deleting session-log tailing; ddx-f948b7a4 handles that after opaque forwarding is in place.
title: Forward Fizeau events opaquely and keep only final-result adapter

acceptance:
1. Production code has no DDx-owned Claude stream-json parser; rg -n 'runClaudeStreamingFn|parseClaudeStream|claudeStreamArgsUnsupported|DDX_USE_NEW_AGENT_PATH' cli/internal/agent cli/cmd returns no production hits.
2. Production code no longer synthesizes Fizeau/internal transcript events such as tool.call/tool.result from Claude stream-json.
3. Tests that covered the deleted parser are removed or rewritten around the Fizeau service adapter boundary.
4. TestAgentExecution_UsesFizeauServicePathOnly or equivalent proves transcript-producing agent work cannot select the deleted direct parser.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'Test.*Claude|Test.*Agent|Test.*Service|Test.*Fizeau' -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
DDx still contains a legacy direct Claude stream-json execution/transcript path. After DDx runs agent work through Fizeau, this alternate code path violates the boundary and can produce divergent transcript semantics from Fizeau.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go contains runClaudeStreamingFn, parseClaudeStream, claudeStreamArgsUnsupported, and Fizeau-style event synthesis from Claude stream-json.
- cli/internal/agent/runner.go and tests still allow DDX_USE_NEW_AGENT_PATH / legacy fallback routing to select DDx-owned parser behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete the DDx-owned Claude stream parser and any execution fallback that can route transcript-producing agent work through it. DDx should invoke Fizeau for agent execution and consume Fizeau events/results through the opaque/final-result adapter.

NON-SCOPE
- DDx final Result projection from Fizeau final events.
- Fizeau harness internals.
- Historical artifact readability for already-created logs.
title: Delete legacy direct agent execution transcript handling

acceptance:
1. `ddx init --global` creates `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/` and writes `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` — TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks.
2. `ddx init` (no flag, in-project) does NOT write to any `plugins/` tree; the plugin lazy-resolves from the global layer — TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins.
3. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
First-time per-machine setup has no entry point, and `ddx init` eagerly installs the default ddx plugin into every project. The intended model is: `ddx init --global` does one-time machine setup (global default plugin + agent-tier links + global config), while plain `ddx init` only creates `<project>/.ddx/` and lazily resolves plugins from the global layer on first use.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` always installs the default ddx plugin (init.go:271-284 via `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)`), producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx` unconditionally.
- There is no `--global` flag and no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` writer.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx init` (cli/cmd/init.go).
2. init --global: install the default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx` (via the install-global path from the sibling install bead), create agent-tier links `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into the global plugin dir, and write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.
3. init (no flag): create `<project>/.ddx/` and the manifest, but do NOT install any plugin — plugins lazy-resolve via `registry.ResolvePlugin` (sibling resolver bead), preferring project over global, on first use. Remove the eager install at init.go:271-284 from the no-flag path.

NON-SCOPE
- The resolver precedence itself (sibling bead: registry two-layer resolution).
- install/update `--global` wiring (sibling bead).
- `ddx doctor` reporting and spec/doc updates (sibling bead: doctor + specs).
- Migrating any `~/.config/ddx/` legacy paths.

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution); sibling registry-resolver bead; sibling install/update --global bead (reuse its agent-tier link helper).
title: init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` lazy-resolve plugins

acceptance:
1. `ddx doc stale --json` output is triaged into active-actionable vs historical/superseded/noise buckets, either by docs updates or CLI behavior.
2. At least one regression test or documented command proves active docs with missing dependency review hashes are reported in an actionable way.
3. Historical artifacts are either excluded from the active stale count or explicitly marked so their staleness does not obscure active work.
4. `ddx doc stale --json | jq length` is lower than the current 138 count, or a documented rationale explains why the count remains high but actionable.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx doc stale --json | jq length` reports 138 stale docs, mostly because dependencies lack review hashes. That scale makes the stale signal too noisy to guide spec-first work.

ROOT CAUSE
The doc graph review-hash process is not being maintained for many active HELIX artifacts, or `ddx doc stale` is over-reporting missing hashes without enough operational triage. Sample output includes core FEAT, ADR, API, and TD documents such as API-001 and FEAT-010.

PROPOSED FIX
Reconcile the staleness workflow: decide whether to stamp/review the active graph, suppress/scope historical artifacts, or improve `ddx doc stale` output so missing review hashes produce actionable buckets. The result should leave operators with a small, meaningful stale set or a documented recovery process.

NON-SCOPE
Do not mechanically stamp every doc without review. Do not rewrite unrelated HELIX content except to mark historical/superseded artifacts if that is the chosen policy.
title: docs: reconcile doc stale review-hash governance
work-last-detail: tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 2983171)

acceptance: See bead body — 3 verifiable AC items: unit tests covering the four classification scenarios; lefthook green; manual smoke confirming worker emits 'server unreachable' instead of flagging beads when server is down.
description:
**Problem**

When `ddx work --watch` runs against a `ddx server` that is partially
unavailable (e.g. TLS handshake failure, agent-service connection
refusal, persistent route-resolution failures with no candidates), the
worker treats every individual bead as the failure unit. Each attempt
gets flagged with `operator_attention` via the progress-watchdog or
`execution_failed` via the routing preflight, then the next bead is
tried, then the next.

Result: a single server-side problem (one bad TLS cert, an expired
provider credential, a stale model catalog) silently turns the entire
ready queue into "needs human" beads, even though the *beads* are fine
— the *server* is broken. The worker keeps idling, the operator sees
"0 closures in 24h" with no clear signal that this is environmental.

Observed concretely on niflheim on 2026-05-31:

- `ddx server` was serving a TLS cert from
  `$DDX_PROJECT_ROOT/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` (Apr 17 fingerprint)
  while the client `ddx work` pinned the cert in `~/.ddx/server/tls/`
  (May 18 fingerprint). The server logged
  `http: TLS handshake error from 127.0.0.1:XXXXX: remote error: tls:
  bad certificate` every ~5 min for ~36 hours.
- Every `niflheim-71365a9f` attempt during that window failed with
  `ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate: 16 candidates rejected,
  outcome_reason=no_viable_provider`.
- After 15+ attempts the worker flagged the bead for operator
  attention, then quietly idled with `execution-ready=0 blocked=47
  operator-attention=17 needs-human/investigation=10`. No clear
  signal to the operator that the *server* was the cause.

**Desired behavior**

The worker should detect server-level failures separately from
bead-level failures. Two-tier classification:

1. **Bead-level failure** (current behavior): the bead's tests/lint
   fail, the agent commits something invalid, etc. → mark the bead
   `execution_failed`, retry per policy, eventually
   `operator-attention`.
2. **Server-level failure** (new): TLS handshake error, agent-service
   connection refused, persistent `no_viable_provider` across ≥ K
   distinct beads in the last M minutes (default K=3, M=10) → mark
   the *server* unavailable, NOT the individual beads. Loop into a
   probe-and-resume mode: every 30 s ping
   `https://server/api/health`; the moment the server returns 200
   and a small `ddx run --text` smoke test succeeds, re-enter the
   queue. None of the beads attempted during the server-failure
   window get penalized.

The server-failure backoff prevents the per-bead operator-attention
flagging storm we observed. It also makes the actual root cause
visible — `ddx work` should emit a single distinctive log line
("server unreachable: holding queue until /api/health returns") and
expose that state via `ddx work status` so the operator notices it.

**In-scope files (likely; verify before edit)**

- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go` — wraps `ResolveRoute` →
  classification site for routing failures.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — the place where
  `no_viable_provider` is turned into `outcome_reason` for the bead.
- The watcher loop in `cli/internal/work/` (or wherever `ddx work
  --watch` lives) — needs the new server-unavailable state machine.
- `cli/internal/agent/` — health-probe helper.
- New unit tests covering: (a) K consecutive `no_viable_provider`
  across distinct bead IDs trips server-failure mode; (b)
  server-failure backoff doesn't penalize the beads it was about to
  try; (c) successful health probe transitions back to normal queue
  drain; (d) TLS handshake error from the server is classified as
  server-level regardless of bead.

**Acceptance**

1. `go test ./cli/internal/agent/... ./cli/internal/work/...` passes
   with the new tests above.
2. `lefthook run pre-commit` (or equivalent gates) green.
3. Manual smoke: stop `ddx server`, run `ddx work --watch`; observe
   the worker emits "server unreachable" within 30 s and does NOT
   flag any beads for operator attention while the server is down.
   Restart `ddx server`; observe queue drain resumes.

**Out of scope**

- Auto-repairing server-side issues (TLS cert refresh, credential
  rotation). Operator still has to fix the server. The worker just
  has to detect and not amplify the failure.
- Multi-server failover.
- Changing the existing per-bead `operator-attention` semantics —
  those stay for true bead-level failures.

**Severity**: P1. Prevents quiet queue-stall pattern that wastes
operator hours on triage when the actual cause is environmental.
title: ddx work: detect server-level failures separately from bead-level (don't flag beads when server is unavailable)
work-last-detail: tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 1993005)

acceptance: Either: (a) ddx work --watch survives bead-only commits to .ddx/beads.jsonl without exiting, OR (b) when ddx work decides to restart, it actually re-execs the new binary rather than just exiting. Reproducer: run ddx work --watch in any project, then run ddx bead create from another shell — the worker should not die.
description:
Reproducer captured 2026-05-20 in helix repo:

  ddx work: detected installed ddx binary changed (current commit 8bd0ae88e, installed commit ee65c467f); starting replacement worker and exiting

Symptoms: worker exits cleanly on every ddx repo HEAD advance. In a project where ddx bead create / ddx bead update auto-commits to .ddx/beads.jsonl (which they do — see chore(beads): create ddx-* doc-stamp commits), the ddx repo HEAD advances repeatedly. The worker detects this as "binary changed" and self-terminates. No replacement worker is spawned automatically. The user must restart manually.

Result: worker uptime is capped at ~one-or-two bead writes between externally-induced restarts. In a heavy-write session this means a restart every few minutes.

Two issues:
1. The "binary changed" check compares the installed ddx commit against something — what? If it is the running worker process commit hash, then any ddx repo HEAD advance (including bead commits that do not change Go code) triggers a false positive.
2. Even when the check is correct, "starting replacement worker" never actually starts one. The worker just exits.

Suggested fix: only restart on Go-code-affecting changes (track go.mod/internal/ paths in the diff), OR actually spawn the replacement, OR drop the auto-restart entirely and require operator restart on intentional binary upgrades.
title: work --watch self-terminates after every bead commit in same-repo workflows

description:
PROBLEM
The reliability claim — that 5+ concurrent ddx work --watch workers can run without blocking operator-issued bead create/update/close — must be backed by an automated test. Without it, regressions in lock scoping will recur.

ROOT CAUSE
No existing integration test exercises concurrent ddx work --watch instances against a shared repo with simultaneous operator-shell bead commands. Executing agent must add one under cli/internal/integration/ (or wherever the existing integration harness lives — grep for TestIntegration_*).

PROPOSED FIX
Add an integration test using the standard fixture-repo helper (testutils.NewFixtureRepo) that:
- Spawns 5 ddx work --watch processes against 5 independent ready beads (each with a stubbed harness that sleeps for a controlled duration to mimic LLM wait).
- Concurrently issues 20 ddx bead create/update/close commands from a sibling goroutine.
- Collects lock metrics emitted by [[lock-lifetime-metric]] and asserts p99 hold time for .git/index.lock < 10s and for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock < 30s.
- Asserts zero 'tracker lock timeout' errors from the operator-issued commands.

NON-SCOPE
- Performance benchmarking beyond the p99 assertion
- Stress test at higher worker counts (5 is the contractual minimum)
- Changing lock implementation
- The fix itself (handled by [[scope-locks-to-mutation-window]]).
title: multi-worker integration test asserting p99 lock hold time under cap with operator commands not timing out

acceptance: 1. Agent and Claude execute-bead prompt snapshots no longer instruct `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`; they instruct parent metadata plus `parent -> child` dependency semantics. Test: TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-f3c3b38a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM: Decomposition can still produce child beads whose deps include the parent when agent-facing instructions or structured decomposition payloads request `child -> parent` edges. That recreates the deadlock even if the direct `dep add` path is fixed only later or if another writer bypasses the CLI.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE: The execute-bead prompt snapshots instruct agents to run `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` at `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/agent.txt:18` and `cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/claude.txt:16`. The preclaim readiness payload model includes suggested child `Parent` and `Deps` fields at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:259`, but the decomposition application path at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4678` only creates children and wires parent deps, with no golden assertion that children exclude the parent from deps.

PROPOSED FIX: Update live prompt construction and checked-in prompt snapshots so decomposition instructions create children with `parent=<parent-id>` and wire only `parent -> child` dependencies. Add validation in the programmatic decomposition path that rejects or strips any suggested child dep equal to the parent or an ancestor before tracker mutation. Add a golden e2e test that decomposes a bead into N children and asserts every child has `Parent == parent.ID` and no dependency target equal to the parent.

NON-SCOPE: Do not implement the global `DepAdd` invariant here except where needed by decomposition writer tests. Do not add doctor repair. Do not change post-attempt remediation or operator-attention behavior. Do not relax AC-map completeness rules.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS: `docs/triage/decomposition.md:65` requires child beads to link back via `parent`; `docs/triage/decomposition.md:72` requires dependency edges in the parent-to-child direction; `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:193` requires executable child beads with dependency edges.

INHERITED DEPENDENCIES: none specified by the parent bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Agent and Claude execute-bead prompt snapshots no longer instruct `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`; they instruct parent metadata plus `parent -> child` dependency semantics. Test: TestExecuteBeadPromptSnapshotsUseParentToChildDecompositionEdges.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

1. TestInstallGlobal_WritesGlobalTierAndAgentLinks asserts `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. TestInstallProject_InTreeMode asserts `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>.
3. TestInstallProject_ConventionMode asserts `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

`ddx install` targets the project path unconditionally and has no `--global` surface. Operators who want one Claude Code install per machine must repeat the install per project, and project installs do not correctly distinguish in-tree vs convention mode.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go targets ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and writes agent-tier links only for the project. There is no `--global` flag and no branch for convention-mode project paths.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` to `ddx install`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx plugin show`. When `--global` is set: resolve target via the GlobalPluginDir resolver from the dependency bead (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/), write the plugin content there, and create symlinks (or copies where the OS requires) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read — ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name> (and ~/.codex/skills/<name> where the agent registry defines it). Skill discovery from a fresh shell must find the skill via the agent-tier path.
2. Without `--global`: write to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and create project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In in-tree mode the links point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>; in convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) they point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>.

NON-SCOPE
- The runtime lookup precedence itself (owned by the registry dependency bead).
- `ddx init` / `ddx update` changes (sibling beads).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir + precedence) — install consumes GlobalPluginDir.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.
- ddx-777f4a09 ddx-validate pre-commit hook: skill validation must still scope to staged skill files regardless of install location — do not regress that scoping.
title: install: add --global flag and honor convention vs in-tree project install paths

description:
Create tests/helix-loop-integration.sh (or equivalent under tests/) that drives real scripts/helix against a stubbed ddx emitting scripted execute-loop --json output. Coverage requirements:
(a) no_ready_work: helix run exits cleanly with no cycle counted
(b) single success: one results[] entry with status=success, HELIX applies post-cycle to that bead
(c) mixed success/failure in one drain
(d) retry-after cooldown: results[] entry with retry_after set; HELIX does not unclaim or blame
(e) preserve_ref present: HELIX surfaces it without attempting land
(f) tolerant parsing: unknown top-level or per-result fields MUST be ignored without error
(g) strict required fields: missing bead_id or status MUST cause a clear structured error, not a silent pass

CI exercise of the contract matrix produced by the audit bead: each DDx-owned item with a code-link resolution has a harness test asserting the linked behavior; each new-DDx-bead resolution is tracked as skipped-pending; each HELIX-backport resolution has an in-harness fake verifying the HELIX fallback.

1. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks (cli/cmd/init_test.go or init_functional_test.go) asserts `ddx init --global` makes ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx resolve into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml.

TestDoctor_ReportsInstallTopology (cli/cmd/doctor_plugins_test.go) asserts `ddx doctor` output contains a "Global Install (...) — ok|missing" line and a "Project Install (...) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global" line, and that the project line reads `lazy-resolves-to-global` when only the global install is present.

description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

First-time machine setup needs a single umbrella command (`ddx init --global`) that installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates the agent-tier links, and writes the global config. A plain `ddx init` must create the project workspace WITHOUT installing plugins (they lazy-resolve from the global/baked-in layers on first use). `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see whether a project install exists or is lazy-resolving to global.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx init --global` dispatches to `initGlobal()` (cli/cmd/init.go:75,318), which: creates ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:319-323), installs the bundled default plugin there (init.go:328), creates agent-tier links so every harness discovers the skill (init.go:314-316), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults via `writeGlobalConfig` (init.go:351-366).
- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create `<project>/.ddx/` and must NOT write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via ResolvePlugin (project→global→baked-in).
- `ddx doctor` topology reporting lives at cli/cmd/doctor.go:415 `reportSkillInstallTopology`, which prints `Global Install (<globalPath>) — ok|missing` (doctor.go:447) and `Project Install (<projectPath>) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global` (doctor.go:448), using `registry.ResolvePlugin` to detect lazy resolution (doctor.go:431-444).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx init --global` creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/ and writes the global config.
2. Verify plain `ddx init` creates the project workspace and writes nothing under plugins/.
3. Verify `ddx doctor` renders both the "Global Install" and "Project Install" lines, including the `lazy-resolves-to-global` project status when no project install exists but the global one does.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence implementation (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- The `--global` install/update surface and project install modes (sibling children).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — initGlobal and doctor topology both route through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
title: init/doctor: `ddx init --global` umbrella setup, `ddx init` lazy (no plugin install), doctor reports both layers

1. Child beads exist for installer mapping semantics, local symlink overlays, embedded default package installation, init/bootstrap replacement, skill tree relocation, mirror cleanup, and governing documentation updates.
2. `ddx bead list --label plan-2026-05-13` shows this epic and all child beads.
3. `ddx bead ready | rg 'ddx-71005284'` shows the first executable bead is the registry mapping bead.
4. `ddx bead show ddx-c34b9915`, `ddx bead show ddx-6cde5ffd`, `ddx bead show ddx-7275e16d`, `ddx bead show ddx-aa630f6d`, `ddx bead show ddx-11ef34e1`, and `ddx bead show ddx-73bb933a` show dependency edges matching the migration order.

description:
PROBLEM
  The DDx skill tree is still managed through parallel bootstrap copies instead of a single package-owned source. The governing plan at docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:16-23 requires the default `ddx` plugin package to own the DDx skill, requires `ddx init` and `ddx plugin install ddx` to share one package installer path, and keeps home-directory skill installs out of scope.

ROOT CAUSE
  Current state is split across several bootstrap paths: cli/cmd/init.go:256-287 copies embedded skills with `skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, ...)`, registers `.ddx/skills/ddx` separately, then installs the default plugin through `registry.InstallPackage`; cli/internal/registry/installer.go:110-132 handles package skills by calling `skills.Install` directly instead of applying manifest skill mappings; cli/cmd/install.go:254-372 has a separate local symlink implementation; cli/Makefile:65-84 keeps copied skill mirrors in sync. The plan requires these paths to collapse into package install semantics.

PROPOSED FIX
  Break the migration into child beads that first make package install semantics correct, then route init through the embedded package installer, then move the skill source into the default package layout, and finally update docs/specs/help.

NON-SCOPE
  - Do not execute the child implementation work in this epic bead.
  - Do not restore global or home-directory skill installs.
  - Do not change public plugin CLI names beyond the separate CLI rationalization work.

PARENT + DEPS
  Root epic. No deps.
title: skills package: migrate DDx skill ownership to the default plugin package

acceptance: `ddx install --force --local <path>` against a repo containing broken symlinks ONLY under gitignored paths completes successfully. The validation logic either consults .gitignore or skips well-known scratch/dotfile paths.
description:
## Summary

Observed against ddx v0.6.2-alpha103-12-ge6379ebc5 on 2026-05-18.

`ddx install <plugin> --force --local <repo>` validates ALL symlinks in the source tree, including those under gitignored paths. Result: any scratch/tmp file with a stale symlink (e.g. files written by a previous `ddx doctor` invocation under doctor/home/.codex/tmp/) breaks the install despite being gitignored and never intended to ship.

## Reproduction

1. In a repo with `doctor/home/.codex/tmp/` gitignored (matches `.codex`)
2. Run any operation that creates broken symlinks under that dir (e.g. an old codex CLI install no longer present)
3. Run `ddx install helix --force --local /path/to/helix`
4. Install fails: `validating package structure: ... broken symlink target ...`

Repro on HELIX:
```
ddx install helix --force --local /home/erik/Projects/helix
# Error: validating package structure:
#   doctor/home/.codex/tmp/arg0/codex-arg0zt0PZe/apply_patch: broken symlink target ...
```

Workaround: rm -rf the offending tmp paths and retry.

## Expected behavior

`ddx install --local` should skip files that are gitignored in the source repository (run via `git check-ignore -v <path>` or read `.gitignore` directly). At minimum, it should skip paths the user could not have intended to ship: well-known scratch/tmp directory names, dotfiles outside the plugin manifest's declared paths, etc.

## Related

- Related to (but distinct from) ddx-efadca32 (lifecycle worktree empty) filed 2026-05-16.
- Blocks HELIX's `ddx install helix --force` for any operator with tmp scratch state present.
title: ddx install --local walks gitignored paths and fails validation on broken symlinks in scratch/tmp dirs

description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4 --project /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau` still reports readiness decode failures and then fail-opens into execution. Two observed shapes are now known:
- `waivers_applied` may be emitted as a flat string list, causing `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.waivers_applied of type agent.preClaimReadinessWaiver`.
- `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance` may be emitted as a single string, causing `json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild.suggested_child_beads.acceptance of type []string`.

Both failures make the readiness model unavailable even though the model produced a semantically usable classification. This keeps readiness from contributing route/difficulty signals and produces noisy fail-open logs during normal `ddx work --watch` operation.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:60-72` decodes the readiness classification payload directly into strict Go structs. `preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.SuggestedChildren` is `[]preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild`, while `preClaimReadinessSuggestedChild.Acceptance` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:148-152` requires `[]string`. `WaiversApplied` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:71` requires structured objects. `decodeReadinessClassificationPayloadWithMode` at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:584-588` fails the whole readiness classification on any JSON shape mismatch. The prompt text at `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:424-431` asks for `suggested_child_beads` and `waivers_applied` but does not give enough defensive handling for common model scalar/list variants.

PROPOSED FIX
Add small JSON normalization types for readiness model-only fields so decoding accepts the observed variants without creating new execution paths. Specifically: normalize `waivers_applied` from omitted/null/empty array/flat string list/object list, and normalize `suggested_child_beads[].acceptance` from either a string or a string array. Keep the internal readiness result behavior unchanged unless existing code already consumes these fields. Tighten the readiness prompt so it documents the preferred shapes and explicitly says scalar fallbacks are tolerated only by the decoder.

NON-SCOPE
Do not persist readiness waiver or child-bead suggestion data to bead metadata. Do not add numeric difficulty/score metadata. Do not implement automatic decomposition behavior here. Do not change the easy/medium/hard routing contract.
title: readiness: accept model-output waiver and child-acceptance variants

description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, emits loop.idle, and either returns (--once / drain) or sleeps (--watch). It never diagnoses or auto-remediates. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation requires the loop to diagnose every non-ready bead, fire matching remediators, and re-scan without sleeping when at least one remediation succeeded.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnose+dispatch logic exists at the insertion point.

PROPOSED FIX
Inside execute_bead_loop.go, after ReadyExecutionBreakdown populates breakdown (~line 1265) and before applyStop/sleepOrWake (~line 1271), insert the idle-path auto-remediation block.

Pseudo-flow (translate to Go):
1. Build childIndex, openChildIndex, depIndex from the store (these may already be computed inside ReadyExecutionBreakdown; expose them or rebuild as needed).
2. Walk breakdown.Epics ∪ breakdown.DepWaiting ∪ breakdown.OperatorAttention. For each bead, call bead.Diagnose(...) (from cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go).
3. Switch on the BlockReasonCode:
   - superseded_pending_close: queue a no-op (the cascade happens automatically inside any subsequent Close(); to actively trigger it on a known X, call store.Close on the SUPERSEDER Y if it's somehow still open in this branch — typically not; if X is the bead and Y is already closed but the cascade never ran, call a new store.RunSupersededCascade(X, Y) helper that runs the same scope-checked cascade out of band).
   - closure_candidate_misclassified: this should disappear once the RC-1 classifier fix lands; if it still appears, log and surface (defensive).
   - dead_intermediate_all_children_closed: trigger the existing closure-evaluation path against the bead.
   - dead_intermediate_open_children_pending OR epic_of_epic: skip (recursion via children handles it).
   - genuinely_needs_decomposition: queue an AutoDecomposeEpic call. At most ONE auto-decompose dispatch per loop iteration (track a counter). Skip if --no-auto-epic-decompose set.
   - dep_blocked_by, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis: no idle-path action; will surface via ddx work focus.
   - stale_graph_index: trigger an index rebuild and re-diagnose next loop.
4. Execute queued remediations sequentially. Track count of successes.
5. If any remediation succeeded, emit loop.auto_remediated event with {remediation_counts: {...}, total_attempted, total_succeeded}, clearTransientCandidateSkips, and `continue` (re-scan without entering sleepOrWake).
6. If none succeeded, fall through to the existing emit("loop.idle", ...) + sleepOrWake path.

Add operator override flags to cli/cmd/work.go: --no-auto-supersede-close, --no-auto-epic-decompose, --no-auto-closure-reclassify. Plumb them through to the work loop via runtime config (mirror how existing --max-recovery-cost flows in).

Add config key work.autoRemediations: { autoSupersedeClose: bool, autoEpicDecompose: bool, autoClosureReclassify: bool } with defaults of true. Flags override config; per-project config overrides defaults.

Reuse existing cost budget threading: AutoDecomposeEpic counts against --max-recovery-cost just like the existing post-TooLarge decomposer does. Honor SmartRetryCooldown / SetExecutionCooldown precedent for per-bead cooldown after failed auto-decompose (already implemented in the auto_decompose_epic bead).

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the diagnoser, RC-1 fix, RC-2 cascade, RC-3 walk-up, RC-6 handler — those are separate beads this one depends on.
- Operator surface changes (ddx work focus, ddx work plan) — separate beads.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. Depends on ddx-79e66725 (diagnose), ddx-e9f3adaf (RC-1 lifecycle), ddx-04e89823 (RC-2 cascade), ddx-f500c75b (RC-3 walk-up), ddx-7b88e3a7 (RC-6 auto-decompose handler).

1. TestLockMetrics_EmitsAcquireAndReleaseEvents asserts that acquiring and releasing the wrapped lock produces two JSON events with required fields (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation).
2. TestLockMetrics_DurationMatchesElapsed asserts duration_ms equals released_at minus acquired_at within 5ms tolerance.

description:
PROBLEM
DDx has no visibility into how long workers hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock. Operators diagnosing tracker-lock-timeout errors must infer from filesystem mtimes and ps output instead of structured logs. Lock-lifetime cap enforcement (separate child) depends on having this signal first.

ROOT CAUSE
Lock acquisition sites in cli/cmd/work.go, cli/cmd/run.go, and cli/internal/bead/store.go log nothing structured around acquire/release. Executing agent must locate the wrappers introduced in [[scope-locks-to-mutation-window]] (or the existing call sites if that child has not landed yet) and wrap them in an instrumented helper.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small instrumented-lock helper in cli/internal/lockmetrics/ (or extend an existing util package) that wraps acquire/release and emits one JSON-line event per acquire and release containing: lock_name, acquired_at (RFC3339Nano), released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation (e.g. 'execute-bead.stage-commit', 'bead.update'). Events go through the existing ddx logs/event stream (consistent with .ddx/attachments/ event conventions).

NON-SCOPE
- Enforcing a hold-time cap (separate child)
- Refactoring lock acquisition scope itself (separate child)
- Metrics for non-git locks (worktree locks, etc.)
- Dashboards or aggregation tooling.
title: emit structured lock acquire/release events with duration for index.lock and tracker.lock
