description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

Operators who run one Claude Code/Codex install per machine need a `--global` surface so skills/personas install once into the machine-wide DDx tree and are discoverable from every project without per-project setup. Today the global install and update paths must write to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and link into the home-tier agent skill dirs, using a separate global state file so they never touch a project's install.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx install` carries the `--global` flag at cli/cmd/install.go:49 and routes through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184) and `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202). For global=true, resolveInstallDirs returns installRoot=`ddxroot.GlobalDir()`, agentSkillsDir=`~/.agents/skills`, claudeSkillsDir=`~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-193); selectInstallState returns `registry.LoadGlobalState`/`SaveGlobalState` (install.go:206-208). Targets are remapped by `adjustInstallTargets`/`remapSkillTargets` (install.go:215,235). Global installs skip the project auto-commit (install.go:174).
- `ddx update --global` routes through `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300), which operates exclusively on `registry.LoadGlobalState()`/`ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (update.go:301,337,345) and never touches the project tree; the flag is parsed at update.go:395 and dispatched at update.go:66.
- The `--global` flag must also be present on `ddx plugin list`/`ddx plugin show` so query surfaces can target the global tier.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Verify `ddx install <name> --global --silent` writes the plugin under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>, recording the result in the global state file (not the project state).
2. Verify `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves any project install untouched.
3. Add/confirm `--global` on `ddx plugin list` and `ddx plugin show`.
4. Document the global install location in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works".
5. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of install location.

NON-SCOPE
- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).
- Project (non-global) install behavior and init/doctor wiring (sibling children).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global install routes through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
title: install/update: --global surface installs to XDG global tree + agent-tier skill links

2. docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-git-awareness.md no longer contradict the no-history-rewrite merge policy for execute-bead branches.

description:
PROBLEM
The governing docs still disagree on parts of multi-worker execution. SD-013 describes concurrent claims as serialized with "last writer wins", while TP-021 requires one claim to win and losing concurrent `ddx try` attempts to exit without terminal-looking evidence. API-001 and FEAT-012 still describe rebase+fast-forward landing, while repository merge policy forbids history rewriting on execute-bead branches.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-013-multi-agent-coordination.md:49 uses stale claim-race language.
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:223-239 describes rebase+fast-forward and push behavior that conflicts with current no-history-rewrite policy.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-git-awareness.md:224-239 and :410-416 need to align with the bounded mutation-window contract introduced by TP-021.

PROPOSED FIX
- Amend SD-013 to say concurrent claim attempts are atomic and at most one runner owns the bead.
- Amend API-001/FEAT-012 landing language to distinguish local merge/preserve semantics from any future multi-machine coordination, and remove stale rebase-as-normal wording where it conflicts with no-history-rewrite policy.
- Update reliability-principle references that still say there are 7 principles when the doc now defines P1-P10.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change code behavior in this docs-only bead.
- Do not redesign multi-machine coordination; file separate beads if SD-020 needs implementation changes.
title: docs: align multi-worker claim and landing contracts

acceptance:
1. A diagnostic or health check marks an unreachable Vidar endpoint unavailable before DDx/Fizeau selects it for work.
2. A provider_connectivity failure records actionable route facts including provider, model, endpoint, and timeout class.
3. A retry/fallback path avoids repeatedly selecting the same unreachable Vidar route for the same bead attempt.
4. Focused tests for provider connectivity failure handling pass.
5. Repository pre-commit or equivalent local gate passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A Niflheim ddx work attempt for bead niflheim-27416d39 reached execution and then failed after DDx/Fizeau routed the continuation to provider vidar model Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit. The route failed with provider_connectivity: openai POST http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions dial tcp 100.108.162.80:1235 i/o timeout.

IMPACT
The project queue remains worker-ready, but attempts can fail after useful work if the default/cheap route selects an unreachable Vidar endpoint. This is infrastructure, not a Niflheim acceptance failure.

EVIDENCE
Niflheim attempt id: 20260518T190003-d738a271. Worker output ended with: provider_connectivity, provider=vidar, model=Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit, endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions, i/o timeout.

PROPOSED FIX
Make provider health or route selection avoid unreachable Vidar endpoints, or mark Vidar unhealthy before dispatch so DDx falls back to a reachable provider. Add regression coverage for provider-connectivity failures so a failed route is recorded and retried/falls back without consuming the useful attempt.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change Niflheim beads. Do not mask genuine task failures as provider failures.
notes: 2026-05-21 repro: Niflheim bead niflheim-439a49ae failed attempt 20260521T080548-f4200852 after auto-routing selected `policy=cheap` / `provider=vidar` / `model=Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit` for a medium validation task. Route failed with `connect: connection refused` to `http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions`. Bead event recorded `work-failed-routes` for vidar, but the once-worker exited after one failed route instead of retrying/falling back to a reachable route. This confirms the bug remains live and should block further Niflheim burn-down until route failure exclusion/fallback is fixed or Vidar health is marked unavailable before selection.
title: Avoid unreachable Vidar route after provider_connectivity failure

description:
PROBLEM
The docker-clone attempt backend is unusable on a remote, rootless, or de-nested Docker daemon (e.g. OrbStack on this machine, docker info Name=orbstack). Every attempt fast-fails in 17-39ms before any agent work with: docker: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type "bind": bind source path does not exist: <host path>. Outcome is classified execution_failed / transport.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:612 dockerRunArgs() constructs the container with five classes of HOST bind-mount (--mount type=bind,src=<hostpath>):
  - :622 src=ws.WorkDir            dst=/work               (cloned repo)
  - :623 src=exe                   dst=/usr/local/bin/ddx  (ddx binary, os.Executable())
  - :636 src=ws.DockerRun          dst=/ddx-runtime        (HOME/cache/tmp + injected creds)
  - :641-642 gocache, work-tmp                              (Go build/test cache)
  - :644-649 dockerToolMounts() -> claude/codex/gemini      (agent CLIs via exec.LookPath)
A de-nested/remote daemon's filesystem namespace does not include this machine's /home/erik/... , so every src path 'does not exist' from the daemon's view. Confirmed: 4 attempts on ddx-b5816493 failed this way; failing paths included /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx and /home/erik/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.145. A short -v probe 'succeeded' only because docker silently auto-created an empty dir (the classic -v footgun), proving the source was not visible.

PROPOSED FIX (decomposed into child tasks)
Adopt the standard create -> cp -> start -> cp-out -> rm pattern used for remote/rootless daemons, plus bake static tooling into the image and use daemon-managed named volumes for caches. Only the daemon's own storage (image layers + named volumes) is reliably reachable; everything dynamic is copied in/out.
  1. Bake agent CLIs into the attempt-runner image; drop their bind-mounts.
  2. Keystone: move workspace + ddx binary in via docker create/cp instead of bind.
  3. Convert Go caches + runtime dir to named volumes.
  4. Inject credentials via env/docker cp instead of the runtime-home bind.

NON-SCOPE
- The worktree and local-clone backends (unaffected; worktree is the working default).
- Routing/provider selection.

2. A unit test asserts withMainGitLock with section="durable_audit" yields a TrackerLockSample with Section=="durable_audit".
3. A unit test asserts the worker-installed sink emits both a loop.tracker_lock event and a plain log line per acquire/release with all four fields (section, wait, hold, retries).
4. A grep `grep -n 'withMainGitLock\|withTrackerLock' cli/internal/agent/*.go cli/cmd/*.go` shows every call site passes a non-empty section string (no positional-only call remains).

description:
PROBLEM: `cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:151-156` defines
`TrackerLockSample{LockDir, Wait, Hold, Retries}` and a global
`TrackerLockMetricsSink func(TrackerLockSample)` (line 161). The hook fires
on every acquire+release cycle at line 232. **No production code wires the
sink to anything operator-visible** — the sample is computed and dropped on
the floor. Similarly, `lockmetrics.Instrument("tracker.lock",
"tracker.commit", fn)` at line 231 always passes the literal label
"tracker.commit" regardless of which of the five call sites
(`durable_audit.go:50`, `execute_bead.go:694`, `execute_bead.go:1493`,
`execute_bead_land.go:839`, `execute_bead_land.go:857`,
`execute_bead_land.go:1210`) is holding the lock — so the metric cannot
distinguish a slow durable-audit commit from a slow Land or a slow
pre-dispatch worktree creation.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28: a niflheim drain with two concurrent `ddx work`
processes saw worker B (pid 681801) time out on the tracker lock with
`tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 424293)` after
the 30s `MaxElapsed` budget defined in `DefaultLockRetryPolicy`. We have
no log of how long A held the lock or which call site was holding —
diagnosis required reading the source to enumerate the call sites and
infer plausible candidates (pre-dispatch worktree creation, Land merge).
The metric to answer "was that a 31s hold or a 2.9s hold blocked by a
30s queue" is computed but never logged.

ROOT CAUSE: Two coupled gaps —
  1. `withTrackerLock` / `withMainGitLock` (line 145 and 128) take no
     section/caller identifier, so the sink receives only `LockDir`
     plus timings. There is no way to attribute a long hold to a
     specific call site from the metric alone.
  2. No worker startup code installs a `TrackerLockMetricsSink`. The
     hook is a dead extension point in production.

PROPOSED FIX:

A) Plumb a section identifier through the lock API:
   1. Extend `withMainGitLock` and `withTrackerLock` to accept a
      `section string` parameter (e.g. "durable_audit", "pre_dispatch",
      "land", "preserve_dirt", "ancestry_local").
   2. Extend `TrackerLockSample` with `Section string`.
   3. Pass `section` to `lockmetrics.Instrument` (replacing the hardcoded
      "tracker.commit") so cap- and metrics-level views also distinguish
      sites.
   4. Update all six call sites listed above to pass a stable section
      string.

B) Wire the sink to the worker log in `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`:
   1. In `execute_bead_loop.go` near where the runtime / log is
      constructed (search for `runtime.Log` usage near `emit(...)`
      callsites), install a sink that, on every release, emits a
      structured line. Use the existing `emit` event stream when
      available (`emit("loop.tracker_lock", map[string]any{...})`) so
      the data shows up in the same channel as `loop.idle`,
      `loop.operator_attention`, etc. Always also write a plain-text
      log line for terminal monitors:
        `tracker_lock section=<x> wait=<dur> hold=<dur> retries=<n>`
   2. Make the install per-worker and idempotent (the sink is a package
      global today; if multiple workers run in the same process the
      sink must serialize or use sync/atomic). For per-process worker
      isolation as it stands, idempotent install at worker start is
      sufficient.

C) Surface lock samples in `ddx work status`:
   - Out of scope for this bead. Track separately if useful — the log
     wiring is the immediate need.

NON-SCOPE:
- Narrowing the lock-held region itself — tracked separately in the
  sibling bead about pre-dispatch and Land lock-surface narrowing.
- Changing the retry policy (30s MaxElapsed, 200 MaxRetries, 1s ceiling).
- Adding a new metric backend or storage; emit through existing
  `loop.*` event stream and `runtime.Log`.
- Adding telemetry to non-execute-bead lock sites (e.g.
  `cmd/bead.go:167`).

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go (add Section field, plumb param)
- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go (pass "durable_audit" section)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (pass "preserve_dirt" at 694,
  "pre_dispatch" at 1493)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go (pass "ancestry_fetch" at 839,
  "ancestry_local" at 857, "land" at 1210)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (install sink at worker start)
- cli/cmd/bead.go (pass a section identifier at line 167 —
  "cmd_bead_write" or similar)
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go (extend tests for Section
  field and sink behavior)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- gitlock package
- cli/internal/lockmetrics — already accepts the label parameter; only
  change is in callers
- ddx work status command (separate bead if surfaced there later)
title: execute-bead: wire TrackerLockMetricsSink to worker log with section identifier (current sink is unread, lock hold/wait times invisible)

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-937de9fc is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 5 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.
- Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and: part 5

PROPOSED FIX

  - B2 (code): align reconcile.go with amended TD-031 and add comment at

    duplicates.
  - B6 (refactor): consoli
...[truncated]

description:
PROBLEM
The unattended `ddx work` drain halts with "worker exited: operator attention required" when a tracked skill SYMLINK is materialized into a real directory by an external skill-install/sync, leaving the project worktree dirty with a non-tracker path. Observed 2026-05-27 06:26: a continuous ddx worker closed 3 beads, then on the next pre-claim found `.crush/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter` dirty — git tracks it as a symlink (mode 120000) but the working tree had it as a directory (SKILL.md/assets/scripts) — and exited via the uncommitted-implementation-changes guard rather than continue. Operator had to restore the symlink (rm + git checkout) and restart the worker. This breaks the unattended-drain goal: a stray tool-managed skill dir stops the whole queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2480 emits "operator attention: project worktree %s has uncommitted implementation changes; released %s" and exits the drain when the landing worktree has any non-allowlisted dirty path (message built at execute_bead_loop.go:955-957).
- cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:38-48 trackerMetadataPaths only allowlists DDx-managed files (.ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, …) — added by ddx-df77e668. Tool-managed skill symlinks under .crush/skills/ (and likely .claude/skills, .agents/skills) are NOT covered, so their symlink->dir materialization reads as implementation dirt and halts the drain.

PROPOSED FIX (pick one; implementer/operator decides)
A. Pre-claim: treat a tracked symlink that has been materialized into a directory whose content matches the symlink target as benign (optionally auto-restore the symlink) instead of implementation dirt.
B. Pre-claim: allowlist tool-managed skill-dir roots (.crush/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills) in the cleanliness check, analogous to trackerMetadataPaths.
C. Repo hygiene: gitignore + untrack these tool-materialized skill paths so they never read as dirty (note: they are currently committed as symlinks, so this is a deliberate structural change).
Recommend A (most precise; only neutralizes the spurious symlink->dir case, preserves the guard for genuine edits).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken the guard for genuine uncommitted source/test/doc edits.
- Do not change what materializes the symlink (external skill tooling) — make ddx resilient to it.

CROSS-REF: extends the tracker-file allowlist from ddx-df77e668; same unattended-drain-resilience theme.

title: execute-bead: unattended drain halts on tool-managed skill-symlink churn (.crush/skills/*); make pre-claim resilient

description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

`ddx update` targets the project path unconditionally with no `--global` surface, `ddx doctor` does not report the global vs project install layers, and the install-topology decision is undocumented.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/update.go targets ddxroot.Path() unconditionally and has no `--global` flag and no in-tree-vs-convention branch.
- ddx doctor has no lines reporting the global tier (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) or the project tier (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) install status.
- No ADR or CLAUDE.md text records the install-to-agent vs install-in-project topology and precedence.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add `--global` to `ddx update`. When set, update only the global plugin tree under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>; do not touch the project tree.
2. Without `--global`, `ddx update` updates the project install in whichever mode is active (in-tree <project>/.ddx/plugins or convention-mode XDG project plugins).
3. `ddx doctor` reports both layers: a "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" line and a "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global" line, using the tier-resolution info exposed by the registry bead.
4. Land the spec/docs updates: file-or-update the skill-install-topology ADR (ADR-025 or the equivalent skill-topology record), document install locations in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works", document agent-tier paths in library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md if that file exists, and document the install precedence in the CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section.

NON-SCOPE
- install / init `--global` flags (sibling beads).
- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).
- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).
- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir + tier reporting) — update and doctor consume it.
- install sibling bead — doctor's project-tier reporting assumes the install layout it produces.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.
- ddx-06cbaa90 spec updates (this bead's spec edits are in addition to those).
- ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology).
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" precedence note.
title: update + doctor + specs: --global update, mode-respecting update, two-layer doctor report, and spec/docs updates

PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

PROPOSED FIX

- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.

NON-SCOPE

- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

description:
# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 6 archive/interchange methods (`Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile`). Per TD-027 §6 archive belongs on `BeadArchive` interface; interchange on `BeadInterchangeReader`/`BeadInterchangeWriter`. All take ctx per AC #1. These are signature-only changes — no helper migration needed; the methods stay as direct storage primitives.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go`, `cli/internal/bead/archive.go`, `cli/internal/bead/import.go` define these 6 methods without ctx. Callers: `cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go` (export/import subcommands).

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update `*Store.Archive`, `ArchiveWithEvents`, `Migrate`, `Import`, `ExportTo`, `ExportToFile` to take `ctx context.Context` first.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadArchive = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadInterchangeWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers (`cli/cmd/bead_archive.go`, `cli/cmd/bead.go`).

# NON-SCOPE
- Archive policy (TD-027 §16) — not modified.
- Attachment storage layout (TD-027 §17) — not modified.
- bd/br interchange shape (TD-027 §20) — wire format unchanged.
- Migration tooling (`MigrateLifecycle`/`MigrateFromHelix`/etc.) — separate concern, handled in MIG bead.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. The 6 methods take ctx as first parameter. Compile-time assertions for `BeadArchive`, `BeadInterchangeReader`, `BeadInterchangeWriter` compile.
2. Callers thread ctx. Existing archive/import/export tests pass.

description:
PROBLEM: Two issues mask the intended design system (`.stitch/DESIGN.md`: warm-paper brutalist). (1) `cli/internal/server/frontend/design-tokens.json` is the STALE generic Stitch export (`tertiary:#8e35a3` purple, `surface:#f9fafb`, Inter for all roles) — its warm palette only reaches the app because `cli/internal/server/frontend/tailwind.config.js:8-37` hardcodes `semanticColors` that override the JSON. So `bun run design:export` regenerates GENERIC values; a reader cannot tell which file is authoritative. (2) The typeface (`Inter`, tailwind.config.js:39-46) is SPECIFIED but never LOADED (no @font-face/@fontsource/Google Fonts anywhere) -> renders the OS system-sans fallback.

ROOT CAUSE: the design:export pipeline emits the generic default token DATA (not DESIGN.md's warm palette); and no font asset is imported. Per codex: `semanticColors` is the load-bearing alias layer (KEEP it) — only the generated token DATA is stale. Operator decision: load Inter (do NOT introduce a new display font — that would be a spec change).

PROPOSED FIX: Regenerate `design-tokens.json` from `.stitch/DESIGN.md` (warm palette: #3B5B7A lever-steel, #F4EFE6 cream, etc.) so the export pipeline is the source of truth and carries the correct values; KEEP the `semanticColors` alias layer in tailwind.config.js (do not delete it; ensure it reads the regenerated tokens rather than shadowing stale ones). Add Inter via `@fontsource-variable/inter` (`bun add`), import in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/app.css`, so all non-mono roles render Inter instead of system fallback.

NON-SCOPE: introducing a distinctive display font (operator chose Inter-only); off-palette utility migration (separate bead); deleting the semanticColors layer.
title: web-ui: canonicalize design tokens from DESIGN.md and load the Inter typeface

description:
PROBLEM
During Niflheim ddx work monitoring, attempt 20260518T021035-da282d6e for bead niflheim-bc47ed66 violated the execute-bead contract while still landing as success. The prompt told the implementer to run gates before committing, rerun lefthook after staging when staged files are required, commit exactly once, and stop after the commit.

Observed behavior:
- The agent ran lefthook before staging, so every hook skipped with no matching staged files.
- The agent committed 1551674d, then a later post-commit lefthook/clippy run found compile errors.
- The agent edited after the commit and amended to e7ae0424.
- A later cargo check found more missing shard_id initializers; the agent edited after the second final summary and amended again to 66beb39b.
- After those amendments, lefthook evidence still included a no-staged-files run, not a meaningful staged pre-commit gate.
- DDx accepted the attempt as success and landed it with result.json status=success/outcome=merged.

This makes the execution audit trail less trustworthy: a successful attempt can contain post-commit mutation, multiple amended implementation commits, and gate output that did not actually test the staged commit set.

PROPOSED FIX
- Teach execute-bead result validation to detect when the implementation commit was amended or replaced after the first successful commit event, and either reject the attempt or record a blocking validation error.
- Treat lefthook runs that report no matching staged files/no staged files as non-evidence for pre-commit acceptance when the prompt requires staged-file gates.
- Add a structured post-agent validation step that compares the first implementation commit event, final implementation_rev, dirty state, and evidence commands before marking success.
- Preserve successful retry behavior by allowing the orchestrator to rerun the bead in a fresh attempt instead of letting an in-attempt agent rewrite its own commit history.

EVIDENCE
Niflheim repo attempt: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim/.ddx/executions/20260518T021035-da282d6e
Final DDx result: implementation_rev=66beb39ba598dfe12301dec195f0673e750e1c98, status=success, outcome=merged.
Agent log shows commits 1551674d, e7ae0424, and 66beb39b in the same attempt.
title: execute-bead: reject post-commit mutation and empty gate evidence

description:
PROBLEM
The service-drain watchdog cancels a healthy run when it sees no "meaningful" events for a window, but a long quiet tool call (build, test, or a slow/stalled model turn) emits no events it classifies as meaningful — fabricating a failure on work that is actually progressing. See cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go (the drainWatchdog idle path; re-pin line against HEAD — the meaningful-event classification + idle timer reset).

ROOT CAUSE
The idle timer resets only on events classified as "meaningful" (final, certain progress). Tool-call lifecycle events and token deltas during a long subprocess are not counted, so a genuinely-working attempt trips the idle cancel.

PROPOSED FIX
Count tool-call lifecycle events (tool_call start, tool_result) and token/progress deltas as meaningful activity that resets the idle timer. Bound the cancel only on PROVEN silence — no events of ANY kind for the window — not on "no events we classified as meaningful". Keep ToolCallTimeout as the per-tool hung-subprocess cap (separate concern).

NON-SCOPE: D1 route recording (merged), D1d tool-timeout config seam (separate bead).

ACCEPTANCE
1. New test in agent_runner_service_wedge_test.go: a stream that emits only tool_call/tool_result/progress events (no "final") for longer than the idle window does NOT cancel (idle timer keeps resetting on those events).
2. A stream that emits NOTHING for the window still cancels (true silence).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
notes: ELEVATED to P0 2026-06-02: D1 (route recording) fixed the false phaseEmpty wedge but exposed this gap — a routed-but-hung attempt (model stalling at 0.0 tok/s, e.g. ddx-06f820f4 hung silent ~1h42m) is caught by NEITHER the phaseEmpty watchdog (route is now non-empty) NOR ToolCallTimeout (not in a tool call). The idle/drain watchdog must catch sustained no-real-progress (repeated 0-token model responses / event silence) and cancel + reroute (ideally failover to a different harness). Critical: a single stuck bead blocked the whole queue for ~2h.
title: agent drain: idle watchdog counts tool-call/token activity as progress (D1b)

2. Any API-001 text that still lists only five statuses is removed or explicitly scoped as a historical minimal v1 subset.

description:
PROBLEM
API-001 documents only five supervisor-visible execute-bead statuses, but the implementation exposes a larger stable status contract used by `ddx try`, `ddx work`, retry logic, review outcomes, land conflict handling, and resource exhaustion. A worker or server consumer that follows API-001 literally will miss valid statuses.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:177 lists only `structural_validation_failed`, `execution_failed`, `post_run_check_failed`, `land_conflict`, and `success`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:330 defines additional stable statuses including `ratchet_failed`, `push_failed`, `push_conflict`, `preserved_needs_review`, `no_evidence_produced`, `no_changes`, `already_satisfied`, `resource_exhausted`, review statuses, conflict-resolution statuses, and repair-cycle statuses.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md already describes richer candidate-cycle and drain lifecycle semantics, so API-001 is stale rather than the implementation being obviously wrong.

PROPOSED FIX
Update API-001 to define the full supervisor-visible status taxonomy or explicitly delegate the canonical list to FEAT-010 plus the implementation enum. Add a compact status table with close/unclaim/operator-attention behavior categories and note which statuses are legacy/future-only if any are not emitted today.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change status strings or worker behavior. Do not redo landing semantics; ddx-693d504f covers that separately.
title: docs: align API-001 execute-bead status contract

description:
PROBLEM
  The governing specs still describe the old copied-bootstrap skill layout and need to be aligned with the package-owned DDx skill model so future workers do not reintroduce copied mirrors or home skill installs.

ROOT CAUSE
  FEAT-011 documents the five-copy model at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:214-244 and acceptance checks across five paths at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:295-317. FEAT-015 still says `ddx init` copies bootstrap skills to `.ddx/skills`, `.agents/skills`, and `.claude/skills` at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md:128-166, even though its 2026-05-12 amendment at lines 16-34 already requires no home skill installs and local symlink overlays. SD-011 still says skill source lives in `cli/internal/skills/ddx` and `ddx init` writes `.ddx/skills/ddx` at docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-011-agent-skills.md:91-112.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Update FEAT-011 to name `library/skills/ddx` as the canonical package-owned skill source and describe generated/install outputs instead of five checked-in mirrors.
  - Update FEAT-015 init and plugin-install sections so `ddx init` uses the embedded default package installer and local overlays link package skill children from manifest mappings.
  - Update SD-011 to match the package-owned source, embedded package copy, and project-local output paths.
  - Update generated AGENTS/CLAUDE guidance or command help snippets that still describe `.ddx/skills/ddx` as a committed bootstrap mirror.

NON-SCOPE

  - Reopening legacy compatibility guidance for old skill names.
  - Adding home-directory skill installation instructions.

PARENT + DEPS

description:
# PROBLEM
After Phase 1 (Foundation `ddx-c6317784`) and per-concern beads (L/C/X/Q/D/A/MIG) land, callers no longer use the removed workflow methods on `*Store` — but they still hold `*Store` as a struct field type for foundational reads/writes/queries/etc. The `Backend` interface composite isn't yet asserted on `*Store`. The concrete types (`*Store`, `RawBackend`, `JSONLBackend`, `ExternalBackend`, `AxonStore`, `*WatcherHub`) live at `cli/internal/bead/` — publicly importable from any other package.

The goal of TD-027 §21 is **physical impossibility** of bypass: not lint-prevented, not review-prevented, but a Go compiler error if any code outside `cli/internal/bead/` tries to import the concrete storage implementations. This bead enforces that via Go's `internal/` rule.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go` — `JSONLBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go` — `ExternalBackend` public.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go` (or `AxonStore` post `ddx-9c5bca8f`) — public.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` — `*WatcherHub` public.
- All supporting concrete implementation files (`claim_liveness.go`, `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc.) — public.

Plus the final assembly step (formerly "K" in earlier breakdowns): `Backend` composite interface declared, `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite, compile-time assertion `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`. These were deferred from Foundation because the composite couldn't be asserted until all per-concern beads removed their methods.

# PROPOSED FIX

This bead does three load-bearing things in one atomic change:

## 1. Narrow caller field types from `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (or narrower)

Every caller file in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that holds `*bead.Store` as a struct field or local variable is updated:

- Most callers: change `*bead.Store` to `bead.Backend` (full interface). Mechanical rename.
- Narrow candidates (identified during per-concern beads): use narrower sub-interface where appropriate (e.g., `cli/cmd/work_focus.go` → `bead.BeadQueries`; `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go` → `bead.BeadReader`).
- Documented exceptions remain on concrete `*Store` access OR on direct file I/O (allowlisted):
  - `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` — file-level repair; reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.
  - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (git auto-commit + conflict-resolution code paths only) — operates on the file as a file.
  - `cli/cmd/sync.go` — `ddx sync` operates on the file.

## 2. Add `Backend` composite + compile-time assertion

- Declare `Backend` as the union of all 11 sub-interfaces:
  `BeadInitializer + BeadReader + BeadLifecycle + BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter + BeadQueries + BeadDependencyReader + BeadDependencyWriter + BeadArchive + BeadInterchangeReader + BeadInterchangeWriter`.
- Declare `ReadOnlyBackend` convenience composite (5 reader sub-interfaces) per TD-027 §6.1.
- Add compile-time assertion: `var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)`.

## 3. Move concretes to internal/

- Create `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/`.
- Move (with package rename):
  - `store.go` → `internal/storage/store.go` (package `storage`)
  - `backend_jsonl.go` → `internal/storage/backend_jsonl.go`
  - `backend_external.go` → `internal/storage/backend_external.go`
  - `axon_backend.go` / `axon_store.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `claim_liveness.go` → `internal/storage/`
  - `attachments.go`, `archive.go`, `migrate.go`, etc. → `internal/storage/`
  - `watcher.go` → `internal/lifecycle/watcher_hub.go` (package `lifecycle`)
  - `RawBackend` and `OperationApplier` interfaces also move into `internal/storage/`
- Create `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` as the SOLE public construction path:
  ```go
  package bead
  import "cli/internal/bead/internal/storage"
  func NewStore(opts StoreOptions) (Backend, error) { return storage.New(opts) }
  ```
- The public `cli/internal/bead/` package surface is now: `Bead` struct, `Backend` + sub-interfaces, `Operation` + named ops, errors, `IDGenerator` + `ValidateID`, ctx accessors, `LifecycleSubscriber` interface, `Migrator` factory (from MIG bead), and the `ops/` helper sub-packages.

## 4. Lint cleanup

After lockdown, two analyzers in the lint suite (`ddx-e91a45c0`) are redundant because Go's `internal/` rule enforces the same property at the compiler level:
- `concrete-store-methods` — removed.
- `no-internal-store-construction` — removed.

Retained analyzers (defense-in-depth inside `cli/internal/bead/`):
- `direct-bead-jsonl-io` (callers still shouldn't bypass the abstraction)
- `no-new-rawbackend-impls` (new backends use Backend directly)
- `typed-context-accessors-only` (ctx.Value discipline)

# NON-SCOPE
- Per-concern bead work — must complete first.
- AxonStore implementation — `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- IMP (JSONL→Postgres importer) — separate downstream bead.
- WIRE (real-wire integration tests) — separate downstream bead.

# Acceptance criteria

1. Every caller file in scope (per PROPOSED FIX §1) has its `*bead.Store` field/variable types narrowed to `bead.Backend` or a sub-interface, with the allowlist exceptions (`bead_doctor.go`, file-level paths in `bead.go`, `sync.go`).
2. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` declares `Backend` as the union of 11 sub-interfaces. `ReadOnlyBackend` declared per TD-027 §6.1.
3. `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage/` and `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle/` exist; all concrete implementation files relocated with appropriate package renames.
4. `cli/internal/bead/factory.go` is the sole public construction path. Exports `NewStore`, `NewLifecycleSubscriber`, `NewMigrator` (from MIG). No other public function constructs a concrete storage type.
5. **Build must succeed**: `cd cli && go build ./...`. **Failure indicates incomplete per-concern bead migration** — diagnose the failing caller and complete its migration before retrying. Do NOT bypass via build tags or temporary public re-exports.
6. Boundary verification test: `TestModuleBoundary_NoInternalImportsOutsideBead` — static analysis scans every Go file under `cli/` outside `cli/internal/bead/` and asserts none of them import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` or `cli/internal/bead/internal/lifecycle`. AST-based.
7. Compile-time `var _ Backend = (*storage.Store)(nil)` exists inside `internal/storage/` (verifying Backend satisfaction now that the concrete type is private to that package).
8. Lint cleanup: `concrete-store-methods` and `no-internal-store-construction` analyzers removed from the bead-lints binary. Documented in `cli/tools/lints/README.md`. The retained 3 analyzers stay.
9. Existing tests pass without modification. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
10. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027, reliability

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049
Deps: all per-concern beads must complete before this bead can execute:
- ddx-bca628fa (L)
- ddx-e1c743d3 (C)
- ddx-f39b41b3 (E)
- ddx-aed7c7ab (X)
- ddx-bc07270f (Q)
- ddx-3af1c1a6 (D)
- ddx-71d3a2de (A)
- ddx-0ab16765 (MIG)

Plus required infrastructure:
- ddx-9c5bca8f (AxonStore — its concrete type is also relocated)
- ddx-29f02cf4 (config factory — factory.go takes over construction)
- ddx-900a8d38 (WatcherHub factory — must be migrated to BeadReader factory before lockdown)

# What this earns

**Physically impossible bypass enforced by the Go compiler.** Any future caller in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, etc. that tries to import `cli/internal/bead/internal/storage` fails to build. The contract becomes: callers see only the public `Backend` interface (and sub-interfaces, Operations, sentinels, IDGenerator, factory); they cannot reach the concrete type.

acceptance:
1. TestInstall_InTreeMode_WritesProjectPluginsAndLinks (cli/cmd/install_test.go) asserts `ddx install <name>` with a `<project>/.ddx/` present installs to `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/` and creates links under `<project>/.claude/skills/<name>` and `<project>/.agents/skills/<name>`.
2. TestInstall_ConventionMode_WritesXDGProjectPluginsAndLinks (cli/cmd/install_test.go) asserts `ddx install <name>` with no `<project>/.ddx/` installs under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<identity>/plugins/<name>/` and the project-tier links resolve into that XDG plugins/ path.
3. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode (cli/cmd/update_test.go) asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install for whichever mode is active and leaves the global tree untouched.

description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2)

Without `--global`, `ddx install`/`ddx update` must target the active project install location, which differs by mode: in-tree mode (a `<project>/.ddx/` exists) writes under `<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`; convention mode (no `.ddx/`) writes under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/`. In both modes the project-scoped agent skill dirs (`<project>/.claude/skills/` and `<project>/.agents/skills/`) must link into whichever plugins/ path the mode resolves to.

ROOT CAUSE
- For global=false, `resolveInstallDirs` (cli/cmd/install.go:184) returns installRoot=`ddxroot.Path(context.Background(), f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs under `f.WorkingDir/.agents/skills` and `f.WorkingDir/.claude/skills` (install.go:195-197). `ddxroot.Path()` is what selects the in-tree vs convention plugins root, so the install root automatically follows the mode.
- `ddx update` (no flag) must update the project install in whichever mode is active; the non-global path in update.go (dispatched when `opts.Global` is false, update.go:66) operates on the project working dir's resolved root.
- Project installs auto-commit trackable project changes (install.go:174-176) — global installs do not.

PROPOSED FIX

description:
PROBLEM
The pre-claim intake contract and its failure-class behavior are undocumented for operators. When a worker silently idles on a full queue (the ddx-f9ddaa68 symptom), an operator has no written guidance on what pre-claim intake is, how warn vs operator-attention vs hard-error outcomes differ, or how to diagnose silent-idle. The 'suggested_action' text in warn events ('check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry') is itself misleading when the fault is in DDx's own decoder.

CONTEXT / WHERE
The operator-facing README is /Users/erik/Projects/.../README.md (repo root; there is no cli/README.md). Pre-claim intake mechanics live in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go and readiness_decision.go.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a README section documenting:
  1. The pre-claim intake contract: what it evaluates before claim and the readiness_checks payload shape (point at the shared schema from ddx-c5d485c9).
  2. The failure-class mapping: warn-only (skip + keep polling) vs operator-attention (escalate + stop) vs hard error — including the consecutive-fingerprint escalation threshold (ddx-d67f124e) and the rolling claim-success-rate warning (ddx-977e1a7f).
  3. How operators diagnose silent-idle: where the agent-loop JSONL lives, what the pre_claim_intake.warn fingerprint means, and how to query worker status.

DEPENDENCY
Depends on ddx-606fe854, ddx-d67f124e, ddx-977e1a7f, ddx-c5d485c9 — document the behavior those children implement.

NON-SCOPE
No code changes; README (and, if needed, a linked docs/ page) only.

acceptance:
1. Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with `Path(ctx, projectRoot) string`.
2. TestDDxRoot_InTreeMode asserts Path returns <projectRoot>/.ddx when that dir exists.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-06cbaa90 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/serv
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Package cli/internal/ddxroot exists with `Path(ctx, projectRoot) string`.
- TestDDxRoot_InTreeMode asserts Path returns <projectRoot>/.ddx when that dir exists.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: ddx home: presence-based root resolution + per-project worktree registry: part 1

2. TP-021 Fast Chaos list names the regression test shipped by B2.
3. FEAT-010 states transient durable-audit-commit contention is not an operator-attention condition while non-transient git failure still is.

description:
PROBLEM: TP-021:44-47 frames the .ddx/.git-tracker.lock 30s only as a PERFORMANCE contract and TP-021:38 names the durable-audit window without a collision-safety contract; FEAT-010's operator-attention discussion (around FEAT-010:294, :322, :797) never carves out transient git contention. The B1 (ddx-83361480) / B2 (ddx-e8bf0695) behavior must be pinned in the specs so it does not regress.

ROOT CAUSE: missing contract text — TP-021:38, TP-021:44-47, FEAT-010:322.

PROPOSED FIX: (1) Extend TP-021's durable-audit window bullet (TP-021:38) and append a "Durable-audit commit collision-safety contract" paragraph after TP-021:47 stating: lock-acquire timeout AND a git-subprocess SIGKILL/context-deadline ("signal: killed") on any status/add/commit step MUST be classified transient and retried (never operator_attention); the commit MUST be idempotent/resumable (re-run completes with no manual git reset); concurrent workers serialize on the durable-audit critical section with a bounded wait that BACKS OFF and returns a transient error rather than killing the git child; genuine non-transient failure (permissions, ENOSPC, corruption) still surfaces operator attention. (2) Add the regression test name shipped by B2 to TP-021 Fast Chaos list (after TP-021:78). (3) Add the one-sentence transient-contention carve-out to FEAT-010 near :322.

NON-SCOPE: code changes (B1/B2); editing the gitlock package contract (unchanged — gitlock.IsIndexLockError is correct; the gap is the agent-layer classifier + deadline policy).
IN-SCOPE FILES: docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-021*.md, docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md.
title: spec: add durable-audit commit collision-safety + idempotency contract to TP-021 and FEAT-010

description:
PROBLEM
ddx ships both `ddx install <name>` and `ddx plugin install <name>` for the same operation. FEAT-001-cli.md:102 (item 38) names `ddx plugin install <name>` as the canonical surface. The top-level `ddx install` is a vestige that survived the move to project-local plugins (CLAUDE.md:30-33 and engineering-principles.md:18,21 still mention it). Users get duplicate help paths, output messages point at the wrong surface, and the help text itself shows examples that mix both forms.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/command_factory.go:586 registers `rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstallCommand())` (top-level). cli/cmd/install.go:153-163 newPluginCommand reuses the SAME command object via `installCmd := f.newInstallCommand(); installCmd.Use = "install <name>"; cmd.AddCommand(installCmd)`. Both surfaces resolve to the same code; only the help text differs. Output messages and docs that should point users at `ddx plugin install` still point at `ddx install`:
- cli/cmd/list.go:245 — "(none — run 'ddx install <name>' to install)"
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:464 — "Run 'ddx install %s' to update."
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:839 — "MISSING (%s not found, run: ddx install %s)"
- cli/cmd/init.go:111 — "ddx install helix   - Install HELIX workflow (optional)"
- cli/cmd/install.go:31-34 — Long help body for the cmd (examples list)
- cli/cmd/install.go:974 — error string "run 'ddx install <name> --force' to repair"
- CLAUDE.md:30-33 — mentions `ddx install <name>` as a current surface
- docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:18,21 — same
- docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md:194 — same

Tests that shell `ddx install ...` directly:
- cli/cmd/installation_acceptance_test.go:361,760,870 — exercise `ddx install --offline ...` (verify behavior; rewrite to `ddx plugin install --offline` if --offline is preserved)
- cli/cmd/install_manifest_test.go:668 — asserts the removed `--global` flag still errors (rewrite to call `ddx plugin install --global` and assert the same error)

PROPOSED FIX
Single-PR change:
1. Remove `rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newInstallCommand())` at cli/cmd/command_factory.go:586. Keep newInstallCommand() the constructor since cli/cmd/install.go:159 newPluginCommand still calls it. Optionally rename to newPluginInstallCommand for clarity.
2. Update newPluginCommand at cli/cmd/install.go:153-163 to also override the Long help body so it no longer references "ddx install" examples (it currently inherits the parent Long with mixed examples).
3. Update the 4 user-facing output messages in cli/cmd/{list.go:245, command_factory.go:464, doctor.go:839, init.go:111, install.go:974} to say `ddx plugin install ...`.
4. Update docs that name `ddx install` as a current surface: CLAUDE.md:30-33, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:18,21, docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md:194. Replace with `ddx plugin install`.
5. Update the two tests cited above to invoke `ddx plugin install ...`. Add a new TestDDxInstall_TopLevelCommandRetired that runs `ddx install whatever` and asserts an exit code != 0 with an error message pointing to `ddx plugin install`.
6. Run `make build && ddx --help` and verify `install` is no longer listed under top-level commands. Verify `ddx install foo` produces the typical cobra "unknown command" error.

NON-SCOPE
- `ddx installed` and `ddx uninstall` (cli/cmd/command_factory.go:587-588) are also vestiges per FEAT-001-cli.md:107 (item 42 says they were removed but they are still registered). File a separate follow-up bead for those; this bead is scoped strictly to `ddx install`.
- The --offline flag semantics, the --force flag, or any other install behavior. Only the command surface moves.
- Library-registry or plugin manifest changes.

PARENT AND DEPS
No parent. No deps.
title: cli: retire top-level ddx install in favor of ddx plugin install

description:
PROBLEM
  TD-031 §3.1 defines ClosureGate (store.go:1245) as the gate for evidence-bearing 
  execute-bead closes. However, applyReconcilePlan at reconcile.go:208-254 with 
  CloseSatisfied=true intentionally bypasses ClosureGate for meta-closes where 
  all transitive dependencies are closed. This distinction is not explicitly 
  documented in the spec.

PROPOSED FIX
  Amend TD-031 §3.1 to add a normative paragraph explaining:
  - ClosureGate applies only to evidence-bearing execute-bead closes via CloseWithEvidence
  - reconcile-close (applyReconcilePlan with CloseSatisfied=true) is a distinct 
    meta-close that intentionally bypasses ClosureGate
  - The bypass is safe because every transitive dependency is already closed 
    (each having individually passed ClosureGate or been a prior meta-close)
  
  Cross-reference the existing language at reconcile.go:208-254.

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the ClosureGate logic itself
  - Changing reconcile-close behavior
  - Adding new persisted statuses

3. TestDiagnose covers the priority-match behavior: a bead matching both superseded_pending_close and dep_blocked_by reports superseded_pending_close (first match in FEAT-010 order).
4. The function is pure: no calls to store.Get, no file I/O, no global state mutation. Verified by code reading.

description:
PROBLEM
ddx work's idle path (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315) reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail and then sleeps or exits. There is no per-bead diagnosis of WHY each non-ready bead is non-ready, so the loop cannot fire targeted auto-remediations. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires a closed reason taxonomy with one code per non-ready bead.

ROOT CAUSE
No diagnoser exists. ReadyExecutionBreakdown returns bucket lists (Epics, EpicClosureCandidates, DepWaiting, OperatorAttention) but does not annotate each entry with why-it-is-blocked detail at a granularity the work loop can switch on.

PROPOSED FIX
Add cli/internal/bead/diagnose.go with a pure function Diagnose(b Bead, childIndex map[string][]Bead, openChildIndex map[string][]Bead, depIndex map[string][]Dependency) BlockReason. BlockReason is a typed struct: { Code BlockReasonCode; Detail string; CitedIDs []string }. BlockReasonCode is a typed-string enum with exactly the codes listed in FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation (superseded_pending_close, closure_candidate_misclassified, dead_intermediate_all_children_closed, dead_intermediate_open_children_pending, epic_of_epic, dep_blocked_by, genuinely_needs_decomposition, parent_child_state_conflict, claimed_in_progress, provider_route_unavailable, gated_by_budget_or_cooldown, malformed_parent_or_dep_ref, dependency_cycle, closed_or_missing_parent, stale_graph_index, auto_remediation_exhausted, no_diagnosis). The function is pure — no I/O — and takes pre-built indexes so it can be called from both the loop and the CLI.

Detection rules per code are specified in the FEAT-010 table. For superseded_pending_close, read the bead's superseded-by field OR a superseded-by:<Y> label, then verify Y is closed in the index. For closure_candidate_misclassified, check IssueType==epic AND len(openChildIndex[b.ID])==0 AND len(childIndex[b.ID])>0. For genuinely_needs_decomposition, check IssueType==epic AND len(childIndex[b.ID])==0 AND no manual-hold/no-auto-decompose/container label. For dep_blocked_by, cite every open dep's ID and status. Match codes in the order they appear in the FEAT-010 table; first match wins.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring Diagnose into the work loop (separate bead).
- Inventing new reason codes beyond the FEAT-010 taxonomy.
- Mutations of any kind.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. No deps.
title: diagnose: per-bead idle-scan reason classifier

acceptance:
1. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject (cli/cmd/update_test.go): with both a global and a project plugin tree seeded, `ddx update --global` modifies only `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/` and leaves the project plugins/ dir byte-identical.
2. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode (cli/cmd/update_test.go): `ddx update` (no flag) updates the in-tree `<project>/.ddx/plugins/` when an in-tree root exists, and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/` dir when it does not, without touching the global tree in either case.
3. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)
`ddx update` must mirror the install topology: `--global` updates only the machine-wide plugin tree under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/`, while the no-flag form updates the active project install in whichever mode (in-tree or convention) is currently in effect, leaving the other tier untouched.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/update.go:58 `runUpdate` branches on `--global` (comment at update.go:65) to cli/cmd/update.go:300 `performGlobalUpdate`, otherwise to cli/cmd/update.go:204 `performUpdate(workingDir, opts)` which operates on `ddxroot.Path`-resolved targets and `enumerateSkillUpdateTargets` (update.go:122).
- Without locked-in tests, a `--global` update could touch the project tree (or a project update could touch global), violating tier isolation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Ensure `ddx update --global` writes only under `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/` and never mutates any project plugins/ dir.
- Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install resolved by `ddxroot.Path()` — `<project>/.ddx/plugins/` in in-tree mode, the XDG `projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/` dir in convention mode — and does not mutate the global tree.
- Tests sandbox `XDG_DATA_HOME`/`HOME`, seed both tiers, run the command, and assert mtimes/content changed in exactly one tier.

NON-SCOPE
- Install/init surfaces — sibling beads.
- doctor reporting and spec/docs updates — sibling bead.
- Removing the per-project install (both modes coexist).
- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) for `ddxroot.Path()` / `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.
- Sibling install beads (global + project install must exist to seed update targets).
title: update: `ddx update --global` updates global tree only; `ddx update` is mode-aware

description:
PROBLEM
While validating the landed `ddx-7eb97e4e` side-worktree reporting fix on 2026-05-15, the broad agent package gate failed once in `TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded`. The failure closed only 4 of 5 seeded beads and `git show HEAD:bead-0005.txt` failed with `fatal: path 'bead-0005.txt' does not exist in 'HEAD'`. A direct rerun of the same test immediately passed, so this is either a real concurrent worker race or a nondeterministic integration-test assumption. Either way, this test currently cannot serve as a trustworthy gate for concurrent bead landing safety.

Observed failing command:
`cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1`

Observed focused rerun:
`cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestIntegration_ScriptHarness_FiveConcurrentBeads_AllLanded -count=1 -v` passed in 0.483s.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go:408-423` starts 5 workers concurrently with `Once:false` against one store and shared project root.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_integration_test.go:432-451` asserts all 5 beads are closed and all bead files are reachable from HEAD, but the failure showed one bead missing from both the tracker terminal state and landed git state.
- The current test does not dump per-worker `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, claimed bead IDs, terminal statuses, or preserved refs on failure, which makes it impossible to distinguish an actual claim/land race from a test harness timing assumption using only CI output.

PROPOSED FIX
Reproduce under stress, then either fix the underlying concurrent claim/land behavior or make the test harness deterministic without weakening the invariant. Add failure diagnostics that print each worker result, bead lifecycle summary, and git refs when the invariant fails. The invariant must remain: five concurrent workers over five ready beads eventually close and land all five distinct beads exactly once.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not skip, delete, or loosen the concurrent landing invariant.
- Do not hide the test behind short mode.
- Do not change production queue ordering or retry policy except where a real race is proven and fixed.

DEPS
None.
title: agent: stabilize concurrent script-harness landing test

TestAutoDecomposeEpic_PreconditionGates: one sub-test per precondition (non-epic, has children, in closure-candidate bucket, dep-blocked, manual-hold/no-auto-decompose/container label, missing description sections, missing spec:/area: label, attempt count at cap, active cooldown). Each returns the matching typed error and does NOT dispatch the agent.

8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Genuinely undecomposed epics (IssueType==epic, totalChildCount==0, no closure-pending state) need automated decomposition into child task beads so ddx work can drain them. Today the only decomposition path is operator-invoked (bead-breakdown skill) or post-TooLarge recovery (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go). FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires the work loop to auto-dispatch decomposition for this specific case (RC-6) with strict preconditions to avoid the v1-plan duplicate-child bug.

ROOT CAUSE
No handler exists that bridges the diagnoser's genuinely_needs_decomposition reason code to runPreClaimDecomposer (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:48). The existing post-TooLarge hook (NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook at recovery_decompose.go:107) gates only on TooLarge failure class, not on idle-scan diagnosis.

PROPOSED FIX
Add cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go exporting AutoDecomposeEpic(ctx, store, runner, rcfg, projectRoot, beadID) (DecomposeResult, error). It calls runPreClaimDecomposer (reused unchanged) after asserting every precondition. Preconditions (each must hold; first failing precondition produces a typed error AND increments the per-bead auto_decompose_attempted event count):
- store.Get(beadID).IssueType == "epic"
- totalChildCount(beadID) == 0 (use the same index ReadyExecutionBreakdown uses)
- bead is not currently in the EpicClosureCandidate bucket
- bead has no open dep_blocked_by or closed_or_missing_parent diagnoses (call the new Diagnose function on the bead; if it returns either, return ErrDepsBlockDecompose)
- bead carries no label in {manual-hold, no-auto-decompose, container}
- bead description is non-empty AND contains PROBLEM AND PROPOSED FIX sections AND has at least one acceptance criterion AND has at least one label matching ^(spec|area):
- per-bead event count of auto_decompose_attempted < 3
- bead has no active execution cooldown
- (operator override --no-auto-epic-decompose checked at the caller, not here)

On success: parent epic's execution_eligible is set to false (mirrors recovery_decompose.go behavior), child beads are created via store.Create, the auto_decompose_attempted event is emitted with body {child_ids: [...], cost_usd: N}.

On failure: SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, now+15m, "auto_decompose_failed", reason, baseRev), emit auto_decompose_failed event. On the 3rd cumulative attempt failure also emit auto_remediation_exhausted so the diagnoser surfaces it.

NON-SCOPE
- Wiring this into the work loop (separate bead).
- Changing the runPreClaimDecomposer prompt (reused as-is).
- Surfacing the operator override flag (work-loop bead handles flag plumbing).

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. Depends on ddx-79e66725 (diagnose function) and ddx-e9f3adaf (RC-1 lifecycle fix — the closure-bucket check needs the correct classifier).
title: agent: auto_decompose_epic — wrap runPreClaimDecomposer for genuinely-undecomposed epics

description:
PROBLEM (child of ddx-937de9fc)
Non-global ddx install / ddx update assume an in-tree .ddx/. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the plugin must land under the XDG project dir (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/) with project-tier skill links pointing into it. Today install writes to the literal relative .ddx/plugins path.

ROOT CAUSE
- registry.InstallPackage(pkg, f.WorkingDir) and the default target defaultPackageRootTarget (installer.go:128) build a project-relative '.ddx/plugins/<name>' string; they do not route through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) which already resolves in-tree vs convention (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75).
- update.go performUpdate (update.go:194) and commitPluginChanges (install.go:210) likewise assume .ddx/ in-tree.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Resolve the project plugin root through ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/<name> for the non-global path so convention mode lands under the XDG project dir and in-tree mode stays under <project>/.ddx/plugins/.
2. Create project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name> pointing into whichever plugins root resolved.
3. ddx update (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.

NON-SCOPE: --global (ddx-aedb009d); init behavior; runtime precedence (ddx-18e55cd2).

DEPS: ddx-18e55cd2.
title: install/update: project install honors in-tree vs convention mode via ddxroot.Path()

acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_ClaimSuccessRateWarnsBelowThreshold` simulates a full rolling window of non-successful claim attempts and asserts exactly one `loop.operator_attention` event containing `claim_success_rate_below_threshold`, window size, successes, non-successes, success rate, and threshold.
2. The same test, or a companion subtest, proves a later successful claim clears the warned state so a later below-threshold window can warn again.
3. `ddx work --help` exposes configurable `--claim-rate-window` and `--claim-rate-threshold` flags with defaults 10 and 0.0.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestExecuteBeadLoop_ClaimSuccessRateWarnsBelowThreshold` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestWork.*Claim|TestWork_PreservesJSONResultSchema_SameAsExecuteLoop'` passes if CLI flags or result schema are touched.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
`ddx work --watch` can appear healthy while repeatedly skipping claims on a ready queue. A top-level operator-visible signal is needed when the worker's recent claim success rate drops to zero or below a configured threshold over a rolling window.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The work loop owns claim-attempt telemetry and operator-visible events. Runtime configuration for claim throughput belongs with `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime` in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:86-92`, and operator-visible loop events are emitted from the loop. Without a rolling claim-success-rate guard, repeated non-successes only appear as low-level intake warnings or idle events instead of a clear stuck-worker signal.

PROPOSED FIX
Track claim-attempt outcomes in a rolling window, default window=10 and threshold=0.0, and emit a single `loop.operator_attention` event plus stderr/log text when the full window is at or below threshold. Reset the warned state after a successful claim so a later regression can signal again. Expose CLI knobs on `ddx work --watch` for window and threshold.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change verdict decoding, same-fingerprint escalation, shared schema files, README documentation beyond CLI help if needed, Fizeau route/harness configuration, lock handling from ddx-57c40485, or route-resolution wedge handling from ddx-8f2e0ebf. Do not make low claim rate close, park, or mutate individual beads by itself. Parent lifecycle, child dependency wiring, and tracker mutation are orchestrator-owned.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT / CROSS-REF
AR-2026-05-17 follow-up context; ADR-023 readiness warn-only policy; adjacent reliability bugs ddx-57c40485 and ddx-8f2e0ebf are cross-references only.

DEPENDENCIES
None.
title: Surface low claim-success-rate warnings from ddx work watch

description:
PROBLEM
Once work supports initial and repair cycles, operators need a durable append-only trace for every implementation and repair cycle. Today the loop emits bead.result and review events, but there is no single cycle trace schema that connects cycle_index, attempt_id, implementer route facts, review group slots, review verdicts, and final decision.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-67 defines the try.Report shape without cycle_index, review_group_id, reviewer slot, or final cycle decision fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:9-46 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_try_adapter.go:49-86 copy report fields between legacy and try shapes, so any new trace fields must be wired through both directions while the compatibility layer exists.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1339-1349 emits bead.result without cycle trace fields.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_group.go:56-94 builds review_group_id and reviewer_index slot data, but that data remains review-local rather than being attached to a durable cycle trace.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:200-227 records the reduced review verdict and closes on APPROVE without appending a unified initial/repair cycle record.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an append-only execution cycle trace data shape for initial implementation cycles and review repair cycles.
- Required fields: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, review result, and final decision.
- Persist the trace in execution evidence and/or bead events using an append-only format that can survive retries and process restarts.
- Wire the trace through the try.Report/legacy ExecuteBeadReport adapter while both shapes exist.
- Cover both the first implementation cycle and the bounded repair cycle introduced by ddx-ece97605.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement repair-cycle scheduling; this bead consumes the repair behavior from ddx-ece97605.
- Do not build frontend UI for cycle traces.
- Do not prune, compact, or rewrite older execution evidence.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-ece97605 so the trace test can cover both initial and repair cycles.
title: evidence: record append-only implementation and repair cycle trace

acceptance:
1. `TestWorkStartupReapsProjectScopedOrphanedExecuteBeadHarness` plants a fake execute-bead harness process associated with the current project/workspace whose recorded owner PID is gone or whose parent is PID 1, runs the startup reaper, and asserts the fake orphan process group is terminated within a bounded grace.
2. `TestWorkStartupReaperDoesNotKillLiveOwnedHarness` plants a fake execute-bead harness process associated with a live worker owner and asserts the reaper leaves it running.
3. `TestWorkStartupReaperDoesNotKillOtherWorkspaceHarness` plants a fake execute-bead-like harness associated with a different workspace/project and asserts it is not killed.
4. `TestWorkStartupReaperRecordsOperatorAttentionEvidence` verifies the cleanup records durable operator-visible evidence including bead or attempt identity when available, PID/process-group information, workspace scope, and diagnosis.
5. `TestExecutorGracefulSignalStillTerminatesHarnessTree` or the existing graceful-shutdown regression guard remains green, proving the startup reaper did not replace or break normal executor cleanup.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
If a previous `ddx work` worker died abnormally before executor cleanup ran, execute-bead harness children can remain running under PID 1 and continue consuming harness sessions or anchoring leaked subprocess trees. Kernel parent-death signaling reduces future leaks on Linux, but prior or non-Linux orphans still need a bounded startup cleanup path.

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 only kills the harness process group from in-process cleanup paths.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 handles graceful os.Interrupt and SIGTERM but cannot handle SIGKILL/crashes, so no cleanup runs after abnormal parent death.
- There is no startup reaper in the `ddx work` registration/claim path that detects execute-bead harness processes whose owner PID is gone or whose parent is PID 1.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a `ddx work` startup reaper, invoked before claiming/registering work, that identifies stale execute-bead harness children from prior dead workers scoped to the current project/workspace and kills their process groups. The scope must prevent killing unrelated harness processes or harness children owned by live workers. Tie the event to existing lease-release/operator-attention style evidence so operators can inspect what was reaped, cross-referencing ddx-8f2e0ebf.

NON-SCOPE
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child bead is only the DDx orchestrator-to-harness-child boundary.
- Linux `Pdeathsig` executor hardening is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.
- docker-clone backend cleanup is covered by a separate child bead from this decomposition.

CROSS-REF
- fizeau-50b6f055: fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup.
- ddx-8f2e0ebf: lease release / operator-attention.
title: fix workers: reap stale execute-bead harness orphans on ddx work startup

acceptance:
1. Add `TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesWaiversAppliedObjectScalarCriteria` proving classification-shaped readiness JSON with `"waivers_applied":[{"reason":"docs-only","criteria":"docs-only","evidence":"fixture"}]` decodes without an unavailable-readiness error, normalizes criteria to `[]string{"docs-only"}`, and still applies the classification result.

description:
PROBLEM
After `ddx-c9b6fb51` landed, the next live readiness check still produced a warn-only decode failure on 2026-05-15 while evaluating `ddx-5cc92777`: `decode readiness classification result: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.waivers_applied.criteria of type []string`. The previous fix tolerated scalar `waivers_applied` and flat string arrays, but not a waiver object whose `criteria` field is a scalar string.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:223-229` defines `preClaimReadinessWaiver.Criteria` as `[]string`. `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:272-279` unmarshals object entries directly into that strict struct before `normalizeReadinessStringList` can run, so legacy/model output like `{"waivers_applied":[{"reason":"docs-only","criteria":"docs-only","evidence":"..."}]}` fails the entire readiness check. Existing coverage at `cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go:291-304` only covers object waivers with `criteria` already encoded as an array.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small custom unmarshal path for waiver object entries so `criteria` accepts either a JSON string or a JSON string array and normalizes to `[]string`. Keep `criteria` requested as an array in prompts and skill text; this is compatibility-only tolerance for legacy/model output. Do not loosen decisive readiness fields.

NON-SCOPE
Do not use waiver metadata for routing, power inference, ordering, or bead metadata. Do not add another readiness schema path. Do not change warn-only readiness policy, timeout behavior, or canonical readiness classifications.
title: readiness: tolerate scalar waiver criteria
