The tests prove a successful side-worktree `ddx try` output includes `implementation_rev`, `landed_rev`, `landed_branch`, `project_root`, and a `git merge --no-ff <branch>` rescue command.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestLandResult_CarriesResolvedTargetBranch|TestApplyLandResult_PropagatesTargetBranchToReport' -count=1` passes and proves the resolved branch from `Land()` reaches `ExecuteBeadReport` / `agent/try.Report`.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestLandConflictUnresolvableEventIncludesRescueCommand -count=1` passes and proves the parked-conflict event body includes `preserve_ref` plus a no-rewrite rescue command using `git merge --no-ff`.
4. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
During the 2026-05-15 Fizeau grendel-off routing incident, the operator had to run a targeted `ddx try fizeau-a54e0299` from a clean side worktree because the active fizeau checkout was dirty. The worker succeeded and printed `✓ fizeau-a54e0299 → merged (0d70176c)`, but that success was branch-local to the side worktree branch `ddx/a54e0299-burndown-232516`. The active fizeau `master` checkout later advanced independently and still did not contain the route-time health fix. The operator had to inspect git history manually, notice that `master` lacked the landed branch, create a rescue branch, and run `git merge --no-ff ddx/a54e0299-burndown-232516` to make the successful bead reachable from current project history.

This is not a model/routing bug. It is DDx operator UX and audit-safety around side-worktree landings. A successful `ddx try` must make the landing coordinates and recovery command unambiguous when the code lands in a worktree branch that is not the operator's active target branch.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/cmd/try.go:503-514` writes the terminal try result but only prints `bead`, `status`, `detail`, `result_rev`, and `preserve_ref`. It omits existing `ImplementationRev` / `LandedRev` fields and has no branch/worktree/rescue summary, so `merged` looks complete even when it landed on a side branch.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1056-1068` resolves the target branch internally, and `LandRequest.TargetBranch` documents the branch contract at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:88-90`, but `LandResult` at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:111-130` does not carry the resolved target branch back to reports.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:397-430` already has `ImplementationRev` and `LandedRev`, and `cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:31-47` already mirrors them. The data exists but the CLI result surface does not expose enough to distinguish worker commit, landed branch tip, and branch-local landing.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:212-220` records unresolvable land conflicts with `preserve_ref`, but it does not attach an operator-ready rescue command such as creating a clean worktree and merging the preserve ref with `--no-ff`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add resolved landing metadata to the success path: `LandResult.TargetBranch`, and propagate it through `ExecuteBeadResult`, `ExecuteBeadReport`, `agent/try.Report`, and the try adapter.
- Update `ddx try` output to always print `implementation_rev` and `landed_rev` when they differ, and to print `landed_branch` / `project_root` for landed attempts.
- When `ddx try` is run from a side worktree or non-default branch, print a clear follow-up block naming the branch-local landing and the no-rewrite rescue command: `git merge --no-ff <landed-branch>` from the desired target worktree, or a `git worktree add ... && git merge --no-ff ...` variant when the active checkout is dirty.
- For `land_conflict_unresolvable` / `land_conflict_operator_required`, include a rescue command in the bead event body and terminal detail using the preserve ref, not just the ref name.
- Keep merge-policy intact: do not squash, rebase, or rewrite execute-bead histories. The rescue path must preserve the worker commits and tracker/evidence commits.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau routing, provider health, or grendel-specific behavior.
- Do not auto-merge side branches into another checked-out dirty worktree.
- Do not change `ddx work` queue ordering or bead priority policy.
- Do not rewrite existing execute-bead branch history.

INTERSECTIONS
- `ddx-06cbaa90` will eventually add a per-project worktree registry. This bead is narrower: expose/rescue the landing coordinates for today's in-tree mode.
- `ddx-c191e91a` covers stale worktree cleanup. This bead is not cleanup; it is successful-result discoverability and manual rescue guidance.
notes: Additional rescue observation: after creating rescue branch rescue/a54e0299-route-health-235101, the committed file /tmp/fizeau-a54e0299-rescue.235101/.ddx/beads.jsonl contains fizeau-a54e0299 with status=closed and closing_commit_sha=0d70176c, but running ddx bead show fizeau-a54e0299 from that rescue worktree reported status=open because the installed ddx command resolved state from the active/canonical fizeau checkout instead of the command worktree's committed .ddx file. The fix should print the state root/worktree used by try/bead commands, or otherwise make side-worktree state resolution explicit; this reinforces the need for branch/worktree landing coordinates in the terminal result.
title: try: surface side-worktree landings with rescue commands

2. New test `TestWork_NetworkFreeDrain_NoFetchInLoop` (in `cli/cmd/work_test.go`) constructs a fixture repo with an unreachable origin (file:// URL pointing at a deleted path) and asserts that `ddx work --once` claims and drains a ready bead without error.

4. Existing ancestry-divergence behavior preserved: a fixture where local main has diverged from `refs/remotes/origin/main` (with origin still pointing at file://) still returns the same skip-with-reason result it does today.

description:
PROBLEM
Reliability principle P9 (docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md:71-87) states "`git fetch` and `git push` are never called from `Land()` or from the `ddx work` queue loop." The CLI pre-claim hook violates this every iteration: it shells `git fetch origin <branch>` before each claim from the drain loop, which couples the queue's forward progress to network reachability and origin availability. This is one of the recurring blockers operators see (`pre-claim hook blocked queue: ...`) when origin is unreachable or the local branch has diverged.

ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go:51-69` (`buildCLIPreClaimHook`) calls `gitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck(projectRoot, branch)` for every iteration of the drain loop.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:806` is the actual `git fetch origin <branch>` invocation inside `fetchOriginAncestryCheckLocked` (called from `RealLandingGitOps.FetchOriginAncestryCheck` at line 782).
- The hook is wired into `ddx work` at `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:544` (`PreClaimHook: buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot, cliLandingOps)`) and into `ddx try` at `cli/cmd/try.go:480`.

PROPOSED FIX
Remove the `git fetch` from the per-claim hot path. Two acceptable shapes:
  (a) Drop the fetch entirely from `buildCLIPreClaimHook` and rely on local ancestry against `refs/remotes/origin/<branch>` as last-observed; FEAT-023 `ddx sync` is the operator-driven refresh path.
  (b) Move the fetch to a one-time pre-drain preflight that runs at the start of `runWork`/`runTry` before the loop begins (still violates P9 for `try`, but `try` is a single-bead path so it's bounded).

Recommend (a). The ancestry check itself (comparing local SHA vs `refs/remotes/origin/<branch>`) can stay; only the fetch is forbidden.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/cmd/shared_helpers.go` (buildCLIPreClaimHook)
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` (FetchOriginAncestryCheck, fetchOriginAncestryCheckLocked)
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go:1677` if the server-side preclaim has the same defect — audit and apply parallel fix.
- `cli/cmd/work_test.go` (new TestWork_NetworkFreeDrain_NoFetchInLoop)
- `cli/cmd/try.go:480` (callsite parity)

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change `ddx sync` semantics.
- Do not remove the ancestry comparison itself — only the fetch.
- Do not touch the `Land()` post-claim git push path (P9 also forbids it but that's separate, file a sibling bead if it's still present).

REFERENCES
- Helix align audit run-id: helix-align-20260518T074735Z (R2)
- Reliability principles: docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md (P9)
title: agent: stop calling git fetch from buildCLIPreClaimHook; restore P9 network-free drain

description:
PROBLEM
Lock-cap enforcement may remove the wrong tracker-lock path from linked worktrees. The real main-git lock is resolved through `sharedMainGitLockRoot(projectRoot)`, but `lockmetrics.lockPathFor("tracker.lock")` currently uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, ".git-tracker.lock")`, which can point at the linked checkout-local `.ddx` path instead of the shared primary workspace lock.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:101-116 resolves `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` through `sharedMainGitLockRoot(projectRoot)` so linked worktrees converge on the same primary lock.
- cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go:216-221 resolves the cap-enforcement path directly through `ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, ".git-tracker.lock")`.
- cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap_test.go:142-154 only asserts that a tracker path contains `.git-tracker.lock`; it does not prove linked-worktree parity with the real lock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Move or expose a shared tracker-lock path resolver so cap enforcement and `withMainGitLock` use the same path for `tracker.lock`.
- Add a linked-worktree fixture test that enables cap enforcement from a linked worktree and asserts the resolved cap path equals the actual main-git lock path.
- Preserve existing `index.lock` cap behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change cap durations or violation JSON schema in this bead.
- Do not redesign lockmetrics event storage.
title: lockmetrics: resolve tracker cap path through shared main-git root

description:
PROBLEM
Operators have no documented contract for the pre-claim intake, no mapping of failure classes (warn-only vs operator-attention escalation vs hard error), and no playbook for diagnosing silent-idle on a full queue. During the Fizeau idle, the warn payload's suggested_action ("check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry") actively misdirected the operator: the real fault was DDx's own decoder, not the route or harness config.

ROOT CAUSE
The README lacks a pre-claim intake section, so the readiness_checks verdict forms, the failure-class mapping, the warn-fingerprint escalation threshold, the claim-rate window flags, and the silent-idle diagnosis steps are undocumented.

PROPOSED FIX (parent AC5)
Add a "Pre-claim Intake and Silent-Idle Diagnosis" section to README.md documenting: (a) the readiness_checks contract including the canonical verdict forms (bool/string/null); (b) the failure-class mapping warn-only -> operator-attention -> hard error; (c) the consecutive-warn-fingerprint escalation threshold and the --claim-rate-window / --claim-rate-threshold flags; (d) a step-by-step silent-idle diagnosis playbook (how to tell a stuck full queue from a legitimately empty one via the agent-loop log and status surfaces).

DEPENDS ON
All four code children (decoder coercion, schema lock, warn-fingerprint escalation, claim-rate window) must land first so the documented contract, flags, and thresholds match shipped behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Any code changes to the decoder, schema, loop, or CLI (covered by the four sibling code children this depends on).

Governing artifact: AR-2026-05-17 follow-up beads. Adjacent silent-failure bugs documented alongside: ddx-57c40485, ddx-8f2e0ebf.

description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:636-642 bind-mounts ws.DockerRun->/ddx-runtime, the shared/per-attempt gocache->/work/.gocache, and work-tmp->/work/.tmp from host paths. These are invisible to a de-nested/remote daemon. They exist for HOME/tmp and cross-attempt Go cache reuse, not for host visibility.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace these host binds in dockerRunArgs with daemon-managed named volumes (--mount type=volume,src=<stable-name>,dst=...). Use a deterministic volume name keyed by project + cache role so cross-attempt cache warmth is preserved without any host path. Keep ws.DockerSharedGoCache semantics by mapping the shared cache to a shared named volume.

IN-SCOPE

- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_test.go

OUT-OF-SCOPE

- credentials (separate bead)

acceptance:
1. `grep -nE 'Worktree resource expectations|resource expectations|build cache|CARGO_TARGET_DIR' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` returns matches for the new subsection.
2. `grep -nE 'environment passthrough|CARGO_TARGET_DIR|GRADLE_USER_HOME|toolchain cache' docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md` returns matches for the new subsection.
3. `git diff --stat HEAD~1 HEAD -- docs/` shows changes only under `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`, optionally `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md`, and optionally `CLAUDE.md`.
4. `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD` does not show any file under `cli/`.

description:
PROBLEM: `ddx try` creates a fresh git worktree under
`~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<attempt>/` per attempt
(FEAT-010 layer 2). For projects with native build toolchains (Rust + cargo,
JVM + gradle, Go + module cache, etc.), the fresh worktree starts with an
empty toolchain build cache, so the agent's first build inside is a cold
build from scratch. FEAT-010 §Layer 2 and TD-010 §Storage Model are silent
on this — they specify worktree creation and evidence capture but make no
statement about toolchain build-cache sharing or per-worktree I/O budgets.

OBSERVED COST (2026-05-28, niflheim project):
- A single in-flight `ddx try` attempt accumulated `target/` = 14 GB in the
  execute-bead worktree.
- Two concurrent workers + the project's own niflheim recovery run
  collectively wrote ~23 GB to the same SSD over ~30 minutes.
- The project's WAL recovery (read-bound, ~2 MB written) was visibly
  starved by worker cargo build I/O.
- Each fresh attempt repeats this cost — the worktree is destroyed at
  attempt end (or preserved with `--no-merge`), so the cache is lost.

ROOT CAUSE (spec-level): FEAT-010 treats worktree resource cost as a
non-concern. The feature explicitly lists "workspace creation, bead
attempt, merge or preserve, evidence bundle" but lists no resource-budget
or cache-reuse hook surface. There is no ADR governing per-worktree I/O,
no documented hook for projects to opt into shared toolchain caches, and
no operator-visible counter that would surface this contention in
`ddx work status` or `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED EVOLUTION (the executing agent should choose how to slot these
into the artifact stack; below is the recommended shape):

1. **Amend FEAT-010 §Layer 2** (`docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md`):
   - Add a "Worktree resource expectations" subsection acknowledging that
     execute-bead worktrees do not, by default, share native toolchain
     build caches with the parent checkout or with sibling attempts.
   - State the operator's responsibility (set per-project env like
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `GOPATH/pkg/mod`, etc., or
     project-level config) versus DDx's responsibility (pass through
     environment unchanged, do not invent toolchain-specific knobs).
   - Document that `ddx work` makes no concurrency guarantee for native
     build steps inside attempts; concurrent workers may contend.

2. **Amend TD-010** (`docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md`):
   - Add a §Worktree environment passthrough subsection naming
     `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, `GRADLE_USER_HOME`, `MAVEN_OPTS`, `GOPATH`,
     `GOMODCACHE`, `npm_config_cache`, `PIP_CACHE_DIR`, etc. as
     environment variables that flow unmodified from the `ddx try`
     caller into the agent process inside the worktree.
   - Specify the invariant: DDx never sets, overrides, or unsets any
     toolchain cache env var. Operator/project owns the policy.

3. **Optionally add an ADR** (e.g., `ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md`)
   recording the decision to keep build-cache policy outside DDx, with the
   rationale (cross-language, single-responsibility, prevents lock-in to a
   specific toolchain). If the executing agent decides one ADR is overkill,
   adding the same paragraph as a §Rationale block at the end of TD-010 is
   acceptable.

4. **Update CLAUDE.md "Execution Evidence Convention" section** if needed
   to cross-reference the new TD-010 environment-passthrough subsection
   so agents working in execute-bead worktrees know they may set
   `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or equivalent) per project policy.

NON-SCOPE:
- Implementing build-cache mounting, sccache integration, or any
  toolchain-specific knob inside ddx itself. This bead is documentation
  only — it locks down the boundary so future implementation work has
  a clear policy to refer to.
- Changing `ddx try` worktree creation semantics or evidence capture.
- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.
- Adding `ddx doctor` or `ddx work status` instrumentation for disk
  contention (potential follow-up bead, not this one).
- Project-side fixes (niflheim has its own bead niflheim-e5cd7c69 for
  the cargo-target-dir workflow documentation).

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-010-executions.md
- Optionally: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-worktree-resource-isolation.md
  (new) — only if the agent chooses the ADR path over TD-010 §Rationale.
- Optionally: CLAUDE.md (cross-reference paragraph only, no behavior change).

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- Any cli/ code — this is a docs-only evolution.
- Other FEAT/TD/ADR documents — touch only the listed artifacts unless
  the agent discovers a direct conflicting claim in another doc, in
  which case file a follow-up bead rather than expanding scope here.
title: specs: worktree resource-isolation expectations (build-cache reuse, I/O budgets) for ddx try/work

description:
PROBLEM: Epic auto-close exists but the autonomous factory bypasses it, so epics linger open forever during real drains. The walk-up-and-close logic (walkUpClosureCandidate, cli/internal/bead/store.go:1639-1697; entered via cascadeAndWalkUp at store.go:1588) is wired ONLY into the manual Store.Close path (store.go:1554). The path EVERY worker close uses — CloseWithEvidence (store.go:1939-1953), called from internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3059,3148, execute_bead_post_review.go:101,318, try/conflict_recovery.go:87,112 — does NOT call cascadeAndWalkUp. So when ddx work/ddx try closes the last child of an epic, the epic stays open. This violates FEAT-004 §Queue Semantics For Epics (docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:122-163), which mandates the closure cascade.

DECISION (operator-ratified): a CANCELLED child counts as terminal/done for epic rollup (cancelled = "work that will not run", types.go:72-73 — a deliberate terminal, never retried), matching the existing walkUpClosureCandidate non-terminal test (store.go:1651: status != closed && != cancelled). Refinement: if ALL children are cancelled (zero closed), the epic is CANCELLED, not closed-as-done. The dependency-DAG "does not satisfy dependents" rule is a separate axis and is unaffected.

ROOT CAUSE: the walk-up is hooked only into Store.Close, not CloseWithEvidence (store.go:1939).

PROPOSED FIX: In Store.CloseWithEvidence, after the bead confirms it transitioned to closed (existing b.Status == StatusClosed check ~store.go:1945), run the same single-hop walk-up Store.Close runs: visited := map[string]bool{id:true}; s.cascadeAndWalkUp(id, visited). Guard so a REJECTED close (gate refusal, bead still open) does NOT trigger walk-up. Add the all-cancelled-children -> cancel-the-epic refinement to walkUpClosureCandidate. Update FEAT-004:134-136 to state cancelled-counts-as-terminal explicitly.

NON-SCOPE: idle-loop cascade (separate bead); reconcile pass (separate bead); multi-hop recursion (separate dependent bead).
title: epic-autoclose: wire walk-up closure into CloseWithEvidence (factory close path) + ratify semantics

description:
PROBLEM: On 2026-05-27 the installed ddx-server systemd user unit was found in a crash-loop with NRestarts/StartCounter at 4382. Its ExecStart was failing persistently (a stale singleton lock at ~/.local/share/ddx/server.lock held dead pid 724, and the legacy unit also had a nonexistent WorkingDirectory). Because the unit sets Restart=on-failure + RestartSec=5 with NO start-limit, systemd restarted it forever (every 5s), spamming the journal and burning CPU, never giving up.

ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/service/systemd.go:15-31 — the `unitTemplate` constant defines Restart=on-failure (line 23) and RestartSec=5 (line 24) but declares no StartLimitIntervalSec/StartLimitBurst in the [Unit] section. The systemd --user manager's default start-limit is not effective here, so a persistently-failing ExecStart loops indefinitely instead of entering `failed` state.

PROPOSED FIX: Add StartLimitIntervalSec= and StartLimitBurst= to the [Unit] section of `unitTemplate` (suggested: StartLimitIntervalSec=300, StartLimitBurst=5). Keep Restart=on-failure and RestartSec=5. After N failures within the interval, systemd enters `failed` (operator can `systemctl --user reset-failed ddx-server` after fixing the cause) instead of crash-looping.

NON-SCOPE: Do NOT modify singleton stale-lock detection in cli/internal/server/singleton.go (separate concern). Do NOT touch the launchd/macOS backend. Do NOT change WorkingDirectory or the XDG paths set by buildServerServiceConfig.
title: server systemd unit: add StartLimitIntervalSec/StartLimitBurst so persistent startup failure stops crash-looping

acceptance:
1. TestWalkUpClosure_RecursesToGrandparentEpic: epic A -> epic B -> leaf child; closing the leaf auto-closes B then A in one close operation, each with its own epic_auto_close event.
2. TestWalkUpClosure_StopsAtOpenAncestor: a three-level chain where the grandparent has another OPEN child stops recursion at the grandparent (it stays open).
3. TestWalkUpClosure_CycleGuardTerminates: a malformed parent cycle does not infinite-loop (visited set bounds it).
4. FEAT-004 states the nested-epic convergence guarantee.

6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM: walkUpClosureCandidate (cli/internal/bead/store.go:1639-1697) is single-hop by explicit design (comment store.go:1638: "Single-hop only; does not chase the grandparent"). An epic-of-epics (Diagnose has a dedicated ReasonEpicOfEpic, diagnose.go:106-116) whose last leaf closes will auto-close the immediate parent epic but leave the GRANDPARENT epic open until an unrelated second close happens to fire. Operator decision: closure must RECURSE the full ancestor chain so one leaf-close settles the entire nested-epic chain in a single operation.

ROOT CAUSE: the deliberate single-hop design in walkUpClosureCandidate (store.go:1683 closes the parent but never recurses to parent.Parent).

PROPOSED FIX: When walkUpClosureCandidate closes a parent epic (store.go:1683), RECURSE to parent.Parent using the existing visited set (store.go:1682) to bound recursion and prevent cycles. Apply the same cancelled-as-terminal + all-cancelled-cancels semantics at each level. Update FEAT-004 to state the nested-epic convergence guarantee (one child-close settles the full ancestor chain). Depends on the CloseWithEvidence walk-up bead so both the manual and factory close paths inherit recursion.

NON-SCOPE: idle-loop/reconcile beads (they converge via re-scan anyway, but the recursion makes them prompt).
title: epic-autoclose: recurse closure up the full parent chain for nested epics

description:
PROBLEM (captured live): Two `ddx work --watch` workers on the same checkout /Users/erik/Projects/ddx collided. Worker 1 ran ddx-69a9ecd3 to completion (closed, 1h11m) but its durable-audit commit was SIGKILLed and the worker exited "operator attention required", leaving .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, .ddx/attachments dirty. Memory was healthy (31Gi free) — NOT OOM. Error: `commit durable audit outputs: committing durable audit outputs: : signal: killed`.

ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:88 wraps the WHOLE commit body (status/add/diff/commit) in context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second); that ctx flows to exec.CommandContext (cli/internal/git/exec.go:38). While the git commit was blocked behind the concurrent worker's hold on the shared main-git lock (.ddx/.git-tracker.lock via withTrackerLock, tracker_lock.go:133-152) / .git/index.lock, the 30s deadline fired and Go sent SIGKILL to git -> CombinedOutput() returns empty output + *exec.ExitError("signal: killed") (durable_audit.go:125-127). The classifier isTransientGitContention (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1026-1041) matches index.lock / "unable to write new index file" / "cannot lock ref" / "unable to update the ref" but NOT "signal: killed"/context-deadline, so finalizeDurableAuditOrStop (execute_bead_loop.go:1311-1336) sets OperatorAttention and exits. The commit is ALREADY idempotent on re-entry (durable_audit.go:98-122), so a retry would succeed — only the misclassification-driven EXIT blocks the idempotent re-run.

PROPOSED FIX: Make isTransientGitContention (execute_bead_loop.go:1026) a SUPERSET that returns true for: (a) errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) and substrings "signal: killed" and "context deadline exceeded" (the SIGKILL/deadline class — defense-in-depth since the exec.ExitError from a ctx kill may not retrievably wrap context.DeadlineExceeded), AND (b) the "tracker lock timeout (...)" string from tracker_lock.go:283 (the lock-acquire-wait-exhausted class). This SUPERSEDES ddx-2189a07f (which adds only the tracker-lock-timeout string and is currently wedging on a routing issue) — folding both transient forms into one coherent classifier change avoids two beads racing to edit the same function. Keep all existing matches.

NON-SCOPE: changing the 30s deadline value or decoupling it from the lock wait (that is the sibling bead B2); worktree-leak cleanup (ddx-274943fc); classifier call sites other than the durable-audit branch.
IN-SCOPE FILES: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (classifier), cli/internal/agent/durable_audit_test.go (extend the transient table ~lines 175-185 via runAuditWithFinalizeErr).
title: execute-bead: classify SIGKILL/context-deadline durable-audit commit (signal: killed) + tracker-lock-timeout as transient, not operator-attention

description:
Once REACH-PROTO + REACH-GO have landed and ddx self-installs the check, run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current cli/ tree and resolve every violation. This is the systematic application phase the user mandated: "go back through the codebase systematically applying it."

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE
- The protocol or check implementation itself (separate beads).
- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).
- Auto-run as recurring sweep (could be a future /schedule offer; not built into this bead).

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-215b08a2 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 2 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` int
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Sentinel errors declared as distinct `error` values. Test: `TestSentinelErrors_AreErrorValues`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 1

description:
PROBLEM: Beads closed OUTSIDE the worker (ddx bead import, manual JSONL edits, federation sync) never trigger any walk-up, so their parent epics linger open even after the CloseWithEvidence fix. planLifecycleReconcile (cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go:159-208) marks all-terminal containers execution-ineligible (reconcile.go:194-205) but NEVER closes an epic whose children are all terminal.

ROOT CAUSE: reconcile has no closure-candidate rule; only childCount is threaded in (reconcile.go:159), not the non-terminal child count needed to decide closure.

PROPOSED FIX: In planLifecycleReconcile (reconcile.go:159), add a rule: when isEpicBead(b) (diagnose.go:269), childCount>0, and ALL children are terminal (closed or cancelled, per the ratified semantics), emit a CloseSatisfied-style plan (mirror the existing CloseSatisfied field at reconcile.go:184) that closes the epic with an epic_auto_close event — or cancels it if all children are cancelled. Thread non-terminal child count into the reconcile inputs.

NON-SCOPE: the live-worker close paths (separate beads); recursion (separate bead, though reconcile re-scanning converges nested epics over passes).

description:
PROBLEM
The ddx skill is the gateway most interactive agents read before deciding what to do. Without explicit mode guidance there, the agent can still interpret broad queue questions as implementation requests even after AGENTS.md and specs are updated.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/reference/status.md:18-23 maps "What should I work on next?" directly to ddx bead ready, which is the worker-ready queue. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:1-16 defines doing work as draining the ready queue and recommends ddx work, but it does not say broad interactive sessions should manage intervention/planning instead of manually executing ready beads. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:43-49 tells agents to pick the next ready bead by default without distinguishing direct worker mode from broad interactive queue management.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the canonical ddx skill references and shipped copies so broad interactive DDx prompts enter queue_steward mode. The skill should tell agents to inspect intervention/focus status, summarize blockers, propose bead/spec changes, and suggest a worker when ready depth warrants it; explicit ddx work/try/execute-bead prompts remain bead_execution and may implement bead AC. Keep review prompts read-only unless the user asks to fix findings.

NON-SCOPE
- Editing SKILL.md frontmatter unless required by existing schema.
- Changing skill install/discovery mechanics.
- Implementing ddx work focus itself; this bead consumes that command surface.

PARENT AND DEPS

2. The comment at lines 438-440 explains the 1h cap, references the wedge-detection rationale, and notes that idle timer (2h) and wall-clock (1h) are separate triggers.
3. TestExecutor_WallClockCapsAtConfiguredBudget in cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go asserts ExecuteInDir cancels at the configured wall-clock budget (use a much shorter budget for the test, e.g., 200ms, to keep it fast).

description:
PROBLEM
  cli/internal/agent/types.go:441 sets DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000
  (3 hours). This is the per-attempt absolute wall-clock cap on a
  harness invocation. Two drain wedges observed in the 2026-05-09
  drain consumed the full 3 hours before timing out:

    ddx-c113a116: silent from 16:21 to 19:13:54 (3h0m0.018s),
                  then "do response complete 8472 tok in 3h0m0.018s,
                  0.8 tok/s" → timeout: context deadline exceeded.

    ddx-94a368b6: silent from 19:36 onward (drain killed before
                  natural timeout fired).

  3 hours is too long: a wedged worker burns operator time and budget
  before the timeout fires. 1 hour catches the same wedge class
  before they accumulate substantial cost.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441
        DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
  - Comment at line 438: "Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout
    — so normal long tasks still complete while genuinely hung workers
    free themselves."
  - The 1.5x ratio against the 2h idle timeout (DefaultTimeoutMS at
    line 434) is preserved by halving both proportionally OR by
    halving wall-clock alone (idle timer protects against true silence
    independently).

PROPOSED FIX
  - cli/internal/agent/types.go:441: change DefaultWallClockMS to
    3600000 (1 hour).
  - Update the comment at line 438 to reflect the 1h cap and explain
    the wedge-detection rationale (citing this bead).
  - DO NOT change DefaultTimeoutMS (idle timer); it remains at 2h,
    which now exceeds wall-clock — but that's fine, idle and
    wall-clock are separate triggers (whichever fires first wins).
    Document this in the comment.
  - Verify nothing in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:45-46 or
    cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:158-159 hardcodes the 3h value
    elsewhere; they should pull from DefaultWallClockMS.
  - Add a regression test: TestExecutor_WallClockCapsAtConfiguredBudget
    in cli/internal/agent/executor_test.go (or extend an existing
    test) that asserts ExecuteInDir cancels at the configured budget.

NON-SCOPE
  - Per-tool-call timeout (sister bead handles that as a structural
    prevention; this bead is the quick symptom guard).
  - Loop detection via stream-json watcher (separate concern).
  - Removing the underlying source of long Bash calls (sister beads
    ddx-1fda33d2, ddx-150758e7, ddx-f1c476c3 reduce per-push test
    cost; ddx-1f774edf removes per-bead pushes entirely).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent: per-tool-call wall-clock for
    Bash..." — structural prevention; this bead is the immediate
    symptom mitigation.
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf — once landed, drain stops pushing per
    bead, eliminating the 168s test tax that fuels wedges.
notes: Cancelled per user direction 2026-05-09: wall-clock cap is wrong policy. Reducing 3h to 1h would kill legitimately-hard-but-still-active work along with wedges. Right policy: detect 'no real progress' (loops + true idle) rather than capping absolute wall-clock. See sister bead ddx-1c3cad6f (reshaped to cover loop detection + meaningful-event idle reset).
title: fix(agent): reduce DefaultWallClockMS from 3h to 1h (faster wedge detection)

3. The assertion got[0].Retries == 0 -> Fatal stays in place (NOT relaxed to >= 0).
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount' -count=50 green.

description:
PROBLEM
TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount (cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go:553) is flaky under parallel-package test load. Failure: 'expected Retries > 0 for contended lock, got 0'. Observed reproduction rate before fix: ~2/20 (10%) when run with -count=20.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go:576-579 releases the held lock via a time-based goroutine:
    go func() {
        time.Sleep(30 * time.Millisecond)
        _ = os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
    }()
Under scheduler load the releaser goroutine can run BEFORE withTrackerLockPolicy (line 588) reaches its os.Mkdir call. When that happens Mkdir succeeds on the first attempt, retries stays at 0, and the assertion at tracker_lock_test.go:598 fails. The contention is not deterministic — it depends on the releaser losing the schedule race against the contender's Mkdir.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a test-only hook trackerLockContendedAttemptHook in cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go fired inside the retry loop after a real (directory-classified, not stale) failed Mkdir, just before time.Sleep(policy.step(attempt)).
2. In the test, replace the time.Sleep+RemoveAll goroutine with a hook installation that calls os.RemoveAll(lockDir) inside sync.Once on first contended attempt. This guarantees retries >= 1 before release, independent of scheduling.

NON-SCOPE
- No change to TrackerLockMetricsSink contract.
- No change to LockRetryPolicy or DefaultLockRetryPolicy.
- Other TestTrackerLock_* subtests untouched (their contention sources differ).
title: fix(agent): make TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount contention deterministic

acceptance:

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-937de9fc is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.
- TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
- ddx doctor's two new lines ("Global Install" and "Project Install") render correctly.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and: part 4

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c191e91a is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
DDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:
- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.
- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-<nano-id>/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.
- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.
- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.

ROOT CAUSE
- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-<bead>-<timestamp>-<hash>/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.
- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-<nano-id>/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.
- .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.
- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).
2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity > 30 min stale, remove the directory.
3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorkerDirs asserts that .ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/ directories whose status.json shows pid=dead or last_activity_at > 30 min stale are removed at startup.
- TestExecutionsRetention_ArchivesOldEvidence asserts that .ddx/executions/<timestamp>/ entries older than DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS are moved under .ddx/executions-archive/<year>/<month>/ (or deleted if DDX_EXECUTION_RETAIN_DAYS=0).
- `ddx doctor` reports the count of stale worktrees, worker dirs, and execution dirs without making changes. `ddx doctor --apply` runs the reaper paths.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: housekeeping: reap orphan ddx worktrees and stale worker/execution state: part 2

description:
PROBLEM
Worker persistence and runtime execution still use a server-local ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, so fields present at cobra or wire boundaries can be lost before spec.json or runWorker consumes them.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec without MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, or SpecVersion. cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-845 builds CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime from that incomplete spec, so runWorker cannot honor those fields on server-spawned workers.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec, or make ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec a temporary type alias to executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec if needed for a mechanical migration.
- Persist the fully resolved unified spec to .ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json after ApplyDefaults has already run.
- Update lifecycleStartDetail, StartExecuteLoop, buildRoutePreflight, runWorker, and test literals to consume the unified spec.
- Ensure runWorker passes MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, and FromRev into config/loop/runtime paths, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD used only as the zero-value fallback.
- Keep disk reads tolerant of unknown legacy fields such as min_tier and max_tier.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change queue picking, route preflight policy, landing behavior, or watchdog behavior.
- Do not implement REST/GraphQL decode changes here except compile fixes.
- Do not change LabelFilter semantics.
title: server: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with unified spec in workers

description:
PROBLEM
cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:700 dockerToolMounts() resolves claude/codex/gemini via exec.LookPath on the HOST and :644-649 bind-mounts each into /usr/local/bin/<tool>,readonly. On a de-nested/remote daemon these host paths (e.g. /home/erik/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.145) are not visible, so the attempt fast-fails with 'bind source path does not exist'.

PROPOSED FIX
Install the agent CLIs into the attempt-runner image at build time so no host bind is needed. In docker/attempt-runner/Dockerfile add a pinned install (e.g. npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@<pin>, and codex/gemini equivalents) onto PATH. Then remove the dockerToolMounts() bind-mount loop from dockerRunArgs (attempt_backend.go:644-649) and delete/retire dockerToolMounts().

IN-SCOPE
- docker/attempt-runner/Dockerfile
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go (remove tool-mount loop + dockerToolMounts)
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend_test.go

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- workspace/ddx-binary mounts (child keystone bead)
- credential injection (separate child)

ACCEPTANCE
1. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestDockerRunArgs green; add TestDockerRunArgs_OmitsToolBindMounts asserting the returned args contain no '--mount' entry with dst=/usr/local/bin/claude (nor codex/gemini).
2. Dockerfile builds with the CLIs on PATH (docker build docker/attempt-runner succeeds; claude --version resolves inside the image).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: docker backend: bake agent CLIs into attempt-runner image and drop their host bind-mounts

acceptance:
1. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerTimeoutKillsProcessGroup` proves a timed-out nested shell command (`sh -lc 'sleep ...'` or equivalent fixture) leaves no child/grandchild process alive after the runner returns.
2. `TestDefaultVerificationCommandRunnerAllowsConfiguredLongGate` or equivalent proves the verification timeout is configurable/worker-derived and can run a command longer than 60s without forcing `no_changes_unverified`.
3. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerifiedLongCommandCloses` proves a `verification_command` that runs longer than the old 60s limit but exits 0 closes the bead as `already_satisfied` and emits `no_changes_verified`.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesVerificationTimeoutKeepsOpenAndReaps` proves timeout keeps the bead open with `triage:no-changes-unverified`, records timeout evidence, and leaves no verification process tree behind.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(DefaultVerificationCommandRunner|ExecuteBeadWorkerNoChanges.*Verification)' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
A `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command:` can time out, leave its child process tree running, and let `ddx work --watch` move on to another bead while the old verification is still consuming the project. Live reproduction on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: `fizeau-bc9586d7` returned `verification_command: sh -lc "go test ... && lefthook run pre-commit"`; the watcher logged `• fizeau-bc9586d7 → no_changes` and started `fizeau-7af3f548` at 13:07:21Z, while `ps` still showed orphaned PID 3510889 (`sh -lc go test ...`) and child PID 3512539 (`go test ./... -count=1`) under PPID 1.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:117-128` hard-codes `DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second` and runs the command with `exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)`. `exec.CommandContext` kills only the direct shell on timeout; because workers commonly emit nested shell commands such as `sh -lc "go test ..."`, the actual test process survives as an orphan. The 60s timeout is also shorter than normal bead acceptance gates like `go test ./...` plus `lefthook run pre-commit`, so legitimate already-satisfied checks are classified as unverified/no-progress instead of closing the bead.

PROPOSED FIX
Run no_changes verification commands with the same process-tree discipline used by agent executors: start the shell in its own process group on Unix, terminate the group on timeout/cancel, and wait/reap before returning. Replace the hard-coded 60s ceiling with a worker/config-derived timeout that is long enough for normal acceptance gates, or make it configurable with a conservative default. Preserve the contract: exit 0 closes as `already_satisfied`; non-zero/timeout keeps the bead open with clear `no_changes_unverified` evidence.

NON-SCOPE
Do not weaken no_changes verification semantics. Do not let verification run asynchronously. Do not close a bead without a passing command. Do not change readiness classification or route selection.
title: work: reap and correctly budget no_changes verification commands

description:
PROBLEM: With 2 live `ddx work --watch` workers per project running, the server Workers pane shows MANY stale workers, reports none active, and the queue view shows "no beads in progress" while attempts are actively running.

ROOT CAUSE (three compounding gaps in cli/internal/server):
1. NO REAPING: the in-memory worker ingest registry (worker_ingest.go) classifies via freshnessState() (worker_ingest.go:349-364) but NEVER removes entries; reportedWorkers (reported_workers_adapter.go:30-59) grows unbounded with dead workers.
2. TWO NON-RECONCILING SOURCES: `reportedWorkers` reads the in-memory ingest registry (POST /api/workers/register + /event); `workers` reads on-disk .ddx/workers/<id>/status.json (state_graphql.go:270-316). Inline `ddx work` (ADR-022) feeds neither with a live active signal, so the pane shows stale history and nothing active.
3. IN-PROGRESS FROM BEAD STATUS: QueueSummary (resolver_feat008.go:299-314 -> bead store.Status() store.go:2603-2677) counts Status==in_progress beads; during a live attempt the bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned and NOT flipped to in_progress (live state lives in .ddx/run-state.json), so in-progress is structurally ~0 while busy.

PROPOSED FIX: (1) evict ingest entries past a disconnected TTL; (2) surface active workers + in-flight attempts from .ddx/run-state.json (future expires_at); (3) reconcile/document which source the pane uses.
NON-SCOPE: changing the orchestrator-owned bead-lifecycle convention; fizeau project auto-registration (separate bead).

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-130cf9ab is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- Programmatic decomposition path (the action that fans a too-large bead into children) MUST NOT add the parent as a dep. Existing action templates / system prompts are audited and amended; one golden e2e test (decompose_does_not_self_dep) creates a bead with N children and asserts every child's deps set excludes the parent.
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable.
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead doctor' (existing subcommand) gains a check 'parent_ancestor_in_deps' that scans the tracker, lists violating beads, and (with --fix) removes the offending edges. Output is stable / scriptable. Test: ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge.
- ddx work no-progress remediation: when an attempted bead's outcome is 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale' AND any freshly-created child carries the attempted bead in its deps, ddx auto-emits an 'operator_attention' event with reason 'parent_back_edge' and stops re-claiming that bead. (Today it loops indefinitely.) Test: ddx_work_halts_on_parent_back_edge.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part: part 2

description:
PROBLEM
The Axon backend contract requires a generated GraphQL client plus a WebSocket subscription client, but the current implementation is an in-process JSONL emulation instead of a typed Axon package. The gap is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-26, which says the backend is a local emulation and not the wire-path implementation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 explicitly keeps the backend as a JSONL-shaped emulation, so there is nowhere for genqlient-generated bindings, schema snapshots, query documents, or a subscription transport to live. The repo has no Axon client package to satisfy TD-030's GraphQL-only requirement.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql with the pinned introspection snapshot.
- Add query/mutation/subscription documents under cli/internal/bead/axon/queries/.
- Add genqlient config and generated Go bindings under cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Add a small subscription transport in the same package for GraphQL WS changeEvents.

NON-SCOPE
- Replacing backend_axon.go itself; that is the follow-up implementation bead.
- Config routing and package-level test matrix changes.
- Axon server-side schema work.

Exactly one middle->max escalation, only on typed genuine failure; no_changes/dirty/transport/quota/auth do NOT escalate. 3. Per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap enforced (test simulates concurrent genuine failures, asserts cap). 4. quota-exhausted -> global pool cooldown, not N per-bead mutations (test). 5. cd /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green. 6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description: Implement the ddx side of the FEAT-004 failure-classification table: ddx work maps each attempt outcome to exactly the action in the addendum table (success/already_satisfied=close; no_changes=close/unclaim NO tier-escalation; genuine impl/capability failure=escalate middle->max ONCE; dirty=clean+same-tier retry; transport=same-tier reroute; quota-exhausted=global pool cooldown; auth/setup=operator-attention; timeout=clean+retry then operator-attention; merge-conflict=unclaim+retry; max genuine failure=operator-attention). ddx owns the ONE middle->max tier escalation (per the escalation-owner decision bead fizeau-994604ec); a per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede. Depends on the escalation-owner contract + the shared classifier (fizeau-44e98499).
notes:
SELF-CONTAINED SPEC (the ddx-side FEAT-004 here is Beads; the ROUTING policy lives in the fizeau repo, NOT accessible to a cold ddx agent — so the normative failure-classification table is inlined below; implement against THIS, do not look for a ddx FEAT-004 routing addendum):

Map each ddx-work attempt outcome to exactly one action:
- success / already_satisfied -> close bead
- no_changes (agent produced nothing + rationale) -> close or unclaim per rationale; do NOT escalate tier
- genuine implementation/capability failure (tests fail after real work; AC unmet) -> escalate middle->max ONCE
- dirty worktree / partial write -> clean/reset worktree, same-tier retry (not escalation)
- provider transport failure (i/o timeout, conn refused, 5xx) -> same-tier reroute to another dispatchable candidate; no tier change
- quota exhausted (retry_after) -> GLOBAL pool cooldown/sleep until reset; NOT per-bead mutation, not escalation
- auth/config missing, toolchain/setup failure -> operator-attention (typed bead state); do not escalate
- timeout / no-progress watchdog -> clean + same-tier retry once, then operator-attention
- merge/land conflict -> unclaim + retry later; not escalation
- max-tier genuine failure -> operator-attention (stop escalating)

operator-attention = explicit bead state transition (not a notification/indefinite wait). The single middle->max escalation owner is ddx work; a per-pool in-flight max-attempt cap prevents multi-worker stampede. Scope tests to avoid hangs (the prior attempt hit a 3m test timeout — bound any test that spawns/loops).
title: routing P1: ddx applies shared failure-classification table; single-owner one-hop middle->max escalation

acceptance: 1. backend_axon.go lands. 2. genqlient-generated client at cli/internal/bead/axon/. 3. Selectable via .ddx/config.yaml. 4. Default unchanged (jsonl). 5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes against both backends (parameterized test). 6. chaos_test.go suite passes against axon backend. 7. WebSocket subscription client present and exercised by an integration test.
description:
Per the TD from B_TD: implement the axon backend in cli/internal/bead/backend_axon.go (or per the existing backend.go shape). Use genqlient to generate a typed Go client from Axon's GraphQL introspection schema — no protoc/protobuf toolchain. Wire as a selectable backend (config flag .ddx/config.yaml backend: axon|jsonl|bd). Default stays jsonl Day 1. Existing tests must pass against both backends.

Subscription support: implement a subscription client (genqlient supports subscriptions via separate library, or use a simple WebSocket GraphQL subscriptions client) so that DDx server can subscribe to Axon ChangeEvents on ddx_beads / ddx_bead_events for live UI updates.

1. resolver_artifact_types.go guards a nil index returned by artifactTypeLoader.Load and returns an error instead of dereferencing index.Types.
2. New test in cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifact_types_test.go (e.g. TestAttachArtifactTypeDefinitions_NilIndexReturnsError) asserts a nil-index path returns an error and does NOT panic.

description:
PROBLEM: Browsing the Artifacts page in the ddx-server web UI returns HTTP 500 (observed 2026-05-27). A 500 (rather than a GraphQL error inside a 200 body) means something in the artifacts resolver chain PANICS and the HTTP layer converts it to 500.

ROOT CAUSE (identified candidate): cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifact_types.go:44-48 — attachArtifactTypeDefinitionsToArtifacts() loops project roots calling artifactTypeLoader.Load(root); it checks the returned err but does NOT check whether `index` is nil, then dereferences `index.Types` at line 48. A nil index (now, or after a future loader change) panics with a nil-pointer dereference. attachArtifactTypeDefinitionsToArtifacts is invoked from the Artifacts() query resolver at cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go:184 (resolver entry at resolver_artifacts.go:92). More generally, any panic anywhere in the Artifacts() chain becomes a 500 instead of a structured GraphQL error.

PROPOSED FIX:
1. Reproduce the actual 500: issue the `artifacts(projectID: ...)` GraphQL query (see frontend query in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.ts) against a registered project and capture the panic/stack from the server.
2. Add a nil-index guard after Load() in resolver_artifact_types.go (return a wrapped error, never dereference a nil index).
3. Fix the specific panic the repro surfaces, and confirm the gqlgen panic-recovery path returns a structured error (HTTP 200 + errors[]) rather than 500.

NON-SCOPE: frontend changes; the Documents resolver; artifact content rendering.
title: server: Artifacts GraphQL resolver returns HTTP 500 — guard nil artifact-type index and stop resolver panics surfacing as 500

description:
PROBLEM
TD-030 requires a live GraphQL subscription path so DDx can subscribe to Axon changeEvents for bead and bead-event updates. The current test suite in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go covers CRUD, deps, events, and import/export, but there is no subscription smoke test yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go:131-179 verifies the split event collection, but nothing in the package opens a WebSocket subscription and asserts that changeEvents are delivered in order. That leaves the live-update path unexercised.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an integration test in cli/internal/bead/axon/ that opens the subscription endpoint, performs a small bead mutation sequence, and asserts the expected changeEvents arrive.
- Reuse the generated Axon client and subscription transport from the client-scaffolding bead.
- Keep the test isolated and deterministic so it can run in the normal package suite.

NON-SCOPE
- Core backend CRUD logic.
- Config selection.
- Package-wide backend matrix work.

notes: REFERENCE UPDATE 2026-05-11: design + integration plan consolidated into TD-027 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md). TD-027 is now the canonical acceptance criteria source — its §28 (Acceptance Criteria) supersedes any AC enumerated in the prior plan doc. TD-031 also folded into TD-027. The bead's design hold (cooldown until 2027-01-01 for ddx-c6317784) remains in effect until the consolidated spec package is reviewed and committed.
title: bead/axon: add WebSocket subscription integration smoke test

acceptance:
1. Legacy format handling: beads with `schema_version` absent in JSONL read as `schema_version=1` per TD-027 §11.4. Test: `TestBead_LegacyFormat_ReadsAsV1`.
2. Every caller that uses Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID passes ctx through. No `*Store.Init()` (no-ctx) calls remain in production code outside test fixtures. Event callers are intentionally excluded and handled by E.
3. `RawBackend` docstring updated with the warning text from TD-027 §6.2 directing new backends to implement `Backend` directly.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 4 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:

NON-SCOPE

acceptance:
1. ddx work --watch enforces a configurable timeout on route resolution (default 60s); on timeout the bead lease is released, attempt status is failed, and an operator-attention event is emitted. Verified by an integration test that injects a hanging route-resolution stub and asserts release within timeout+10s.
2. A progress watchdog fires when phase-empty heartbeats (harness="", model="", route="") continue beyond a phase-specific budget; the worker releases the lease and emits operator-attention. Verified by a test that drives heartbeats with empty fields for budget+epsilon and asserts release.
3. Claiming a bead atomically sets status=in_progress in the same write; releasing on failure atomically clears the claim. ddx bead status reflects the lease state. Verified by a multi-process test that inspects status mid-claim.
4. ddx bead operator-attention surfaces every lease release caused by timeout or wedge detection, with bead-id, attempt-id, last_activity_at, and a short diagnosis string.
5. Same-bead-twice wedge guard: if the worker observes the same bead wedge on consecutive claims, it stops claiming that bead and surfaces it for operator review; tested by injection.
6. Documentation: ddx README or AGENTS.md names the timeout defaults, the wedge-detection contract, and how to inspect ddx bead operator-attention.
description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch can claim a bead, fail to resolve a route, and hang in the route-resolution step indefinitely. Heartbeats keep emitting (phase=running, last_activity_at advancing every 30s) but harness/model/route fields stay empty and no LLM call ever happens. The bead's lease is held but no work progresses, and the worker cannot pick up any other bead because it is single-threaded.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 02:05–03:27 EDT)
- Worker: PID 560430 (ddx work --watch, no flags, cwd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau).
- Claimed bead: fizeau-86f2bac0 (bench-pr-A3b) at attempt_id 20260518T020552-18b086b2.
- 80+ minutes of heartbeats logged in .ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779069470069950671.jsonl, every 30s, all with phase=running, harness="", model="", route="".
- ddx bead status shows In progress: 0 — the state machine never marked it in_progress despite the lease.
- ddx bead ready returned 30 dispatchable beads — none would be picked up because the worker is single-threaded and wedged.
- Related earlier event on fizeau-856b7957: routing-intent event with routing_intent_degraded=true, routing_intent_note='actual route facts unavailable'. Strongly suggests the same wedge: route resolution returns 'no route' but the worker treats it as 'wait forever' instead of 'release bead with error'.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Lease without progress: holding a lease for 80+ minutes with no LLM call, no commit, no resolved route is indistinguishable from a crashed worker, but heartbeats keep ticking so the staleness reaper (if any) never fires.
2. Single-worker starvation: 30 ready beads, one stuck worker, queue stops advancing.
3. State inconsistency: bead has a claim but bead status reports 0 in_progress, suggesting the claim and the lifecycle status are not atomically coupled.
4. No actionable error surface: the operator only learns about the wedge by tailing agent-logs and noticing the empty harness/route fields. The watch loop's stdout shows nothing because heartbeats are JSONL-only.
5. The harness for execute-bead inherits zero feedback when route resolution silently fails — even when a route eventually resolves, the wedge has stolen 80 minutes of throughput.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Route-resolution timeout: the resolution step must be bounded by a configurable timeout (default 60s). On timeout: release the lease with a structured error, mark the attempt as failed, do not retry without operator attention.
B. Progress timeout independent of heartbeats: a watchdog that fires when last_activity_at has not advanced beyond a phase-specific budget (e.g., 5 minutes in 'resolving', 30 minutes in 'running' with a harness/model set, etc.) — phase-empty heartbeats with no real work are the failure signature.
C. Lease+lifecycle atomicity: claiming a bead must atomically set status to in_progress; releasing on failure must atomically clear the claim. ddx bead status counts must reflect claimed beads.
D. Operator-visible error surface: when a worker releases a lease due to timeout or wedge detection, emit a top-level operator-attention event with bead-id, attempt-id, last-activity, and a short diagnosis. ddx bead operator-attention should surface it.
E. Worker exit on repeated wedge: if the same bead wedges twice in a row, the worker should exit (or stop claiming that bead) rather than keep eating the queue.

CROSS-REF
Filed from Fizeau session 2026-05-17/18 during the claude-tui chain rollout. Adjacent bug: ddx-57c40485 (workers hold git index.lock for minutes). Both bugs share root cause: long-running operations inside the worker without bounded timeouts or operator-visible failure modes.
notes: All 5 decomposed children landed + verified: 449baa1d (atomic Store.Release), d8970a7b (route-resolution timeout+release), dc23f001 (progress watchdog+external-close watcher), 9714eaac (consecutive-wedge claim guard), 58a69bb0 (operator-attention surface+docs). Wedge-fix behavior complete.
title: worker wedges on route resolution without timeout; no progress and no release for hours
work-last-detail: ddx-8f2e0ebf is feature-sized (6 acceptance criteria plus a notes-added 7th requirement) spanning unrelated subsystems — routing/preflight, the 5538-line execute-bead worker loop, heartbeat/liveness, the bead store, the `ddx bead operator-attention` command, and docs — and mixes implementation, integration tests, and documentation. It exceeds the single-pass size budget (Step 0), so it was decomposed (no implementation in this pass). Decomposed into 5 children (created with --parent ddx-8f2e0ebf; inter-child dependency edges record landing order): - ddx-449baa1d  bead: add atomic Store.Release so failure-release clears the claim and status reflects the lease   [AC3; foundation, no deps, currently ready] - ddx-d8970a7b  worker: bound route resolution and routing preflight with a configurable timeout and release on expiry   [AC1; depends on ddx-449baa1d] - ddx-dc23f001  worker: add progress watchdog for phase-empty heartbeats and terminate when the bead is closed externally   [AC2 + notes; depends on ddx-d8970a7b] - ddx-9714eaac  worker: stop claiming a bead that wedges on consecutive claims and surface it for operator review   [AC5; depends on ddx-d8970a7b] - ddx-58a69bb0  bead: surface every wedge/timeout lease release in ddx bead operator-attention and document the contract   [AC4 + AC6; depends on ddx-d8970a7b, ddx-dc23f001, ddx-9714eaac] Dependency direction note: this repo's own decomposer (cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:184-210) records the parent/child edge via Parent=<parentID> and marks the parent execution-ineligible; it does NOT add a `dep add <child> <parent>` blocking edge. Following Step 0's literal `dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` (child depends-on parent) would make every child blocked by the open parent and never become ready, stalling drain. So the parent/child edge is recorded via --parent (children stay ready because the parent is open), and only correct-direction inter-child ordering edges were added with `ddx bead dep add`. The parent now carries a `decomposed into ...` note; the orchestrator owns the parent's lifecycle from here.

So the parent/child edge is recorded via --parent (children stay ready because the parent is open), and only correct-direction inter-child ordering edges were added with `ddx bead dep add`. description:
PROBLEM
The frontend accessibility suite is failing on the dashboard and agent views because the primary accent buttons use contrast combinations that do not meet WCAG AA. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/a11y.spec.ts` reports `color-contrast` violations for `.bg-accent-fulcrum` on light mode and `.bg-accent-fulcrum` plus `.dark\:bg-dark-accent-lever` on dark mode. The visible button styles on the project dashboard and worker detail pages are the source of the failures.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte:50-72` defines the project dashboard action buttons, including the `bg-accent-lever` and `bg-accent-fulcrum` variants.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/[workerId]/+page.svelte:440-490` renders the live response controls that reuse the same low-contrast accent colors in dark and light modes.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/a11y.spec.ts:55-70` is the failing coverage that catches the contrast regression.

PROPOSED FIX
Adjust the button color tokens or local component classes so the dashboard and worker pages meet WCAG AA contrast in both themes without changing the interaction model. Keep the fix localized to the shared accent button styles or the two affected page components.

NON-SCOPE
- Copy or layout changes unrelated to contrast.
- Other page families that are not currently failing axe.
- Backend data or routing changes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/a11y.spec.ts` passes for the dashboard and agent pages in both light and dark modes.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e e2e/a11y.spec.ts --grep "dashboard|agent"` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
title: restore dashboard and agent contrast to WCAG AA
