Artifacts (route .../artifacts/, resolver cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go:92-249) is a SUPERSET: same doc-graph docs (collectDocArtifacts, 'doc:' prefix) PLUS sidecar .ddx.yaml artifacts, with mediaType/phase/prefix/search/staleness filters + generatedBy provenance. So Documents == Artifacts filtered to doc-graph markdown, minus features. Specs: FEAT-005/007/008 under docs/helix/.

description:
# PROBLEM
`*bead.WatcherHub` (`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:36-66`) constructs its own `*Store` internally via `NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. This bypasses any factory-level capability validation or backend selection. Today everything is JSONL so this is invisible; once AxonStore ships and `bead.backend: axon` is configured for production, the WatcherHub still polls a local JSONL file while the authoritative bead state lives in Lakebase Postgres. Result: silent stale-data bug — `beadLifecycle` GraphQL subscriptions return empty or stale events.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:54-66` — `SubscribeLifecycle` instantiates a `*Store` directly: `store := NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. No injection point for the configured backend.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:60-65` — `newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)` ties the watcher to that locally-constructed Store.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go` — constructs `*WatcherHub` and passes it to subscription resolvers. The hub is constructed once at server startup; the project Stores it creates internally are detached from any per-project factory.

# PROPOSED FIX
Change `WatcherHub` to take a `bead.BeadReader` (or `Backend`) factory function at construction:

```go
type StoreFactory func(projectID string) (bead.BeadReader, error)

type WatcherHub struct {
    factory  StoreFactory
    watchers map[string]*projectWatcher
    interval time.Duration
    ...
}

func NewWatcherHub(factory StoreFactory, interval time.Duration) *WatcherHub { ... }

func (h *WatcherHub) SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error) {
    // ...
    store, err := h.factory(projectID)
    if err != nil { return nil, nil, err }
    pw = newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)
    // ...
}
```

`cli/internal/server/server.go` is updated to pass a factory that uses the project's configured backend (selected via the same config pathway that the rest of ddx-server uses).

# NON-SCOPE
- Native subscription transport on AxonStore (separate work — would replace the polling-based `projectWatcher` with native push when available).
- Changing `LifecycleEvent` shape or semantics.
- Multi-tenant identity propagation (handled separately via the `bead.WithIdentity` ctx accessor pattern).
- Modifying callers other than `cli/internal/server/server.go` and the subscription resolver.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `WatcherHub` constructor signature changes to accept a `StoreFactory` parameter. Compile-time assertion `var _ LifecycleSubscriber = (*WatcherHub)(nil)` still holds.
2. `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go` no longer contains a direct `NewStore(...)` call.
3. `cli/internal/server/server.go` constructs `WatcherHub` with a factory that delegates to the same backend-selection path the rest of the server uses.
4. Test `TestWatcherHub_UsesProvidedFactory` injects a fake factory, asserts the factory is invoked when SubscribeLifecycle is called, and asserts the watcher polls the injected `bead.BeadReader` rather than the filesystem.
5. Test `TestWatcherHub_PropagatesFactoryErrors` injects a factory that returns an error and asserts SubscribeLifecycle returns the error.
6. Existing `TestWatcherHub_*` tests in `cli/internal/bead/watcher_test.go` are updated to use the factory pattern; they continue to pass.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:server, kind:refactor, spec:FEAT-002, spec:FEAT-004, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps
Parent: ddx-8d747049 (axon backend prototype umbrella).
Deps: ddx-c6317784 (interface refinement, which adds `BeadReader` and the LifecycleSubscriber ctx-aware signature).
notes: REFERENCE UPDATE 2026-05-11: design + integration plan consolidated into TD-027 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md). TD-027 is now the canonical acceptance criteria source — its §28 (Acceptance Criteria) supersedes any AC enumerated in the prior plan doc. TD-031 also folded into TD-027. The bead's design hold (cooldown until 2027-01-01 for ddx-c6317784) remains in effect until the consolidated spec package is reviewed and committed.
title: WatcherHub takes a BeadReader factory at construction (interface-refactor S5 follow-up)

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-70a432b5 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- FEAT-008 spec updated with Run-detail-page subsection + trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist.
- FEAT-010 spec updated with extended Run resolver contract + drain-time persistence note + ToolCallEntry definition.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.

description:
PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0: bead-lifecycle quality framework needs an ADR documenting the policy + amendments to existing FEATs to formally adopt the lint/triage hooks and the sub-skill packaging change.

ROOT CAUSE
Today no policy artifact exists for bead-quality enforcement. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 documents P7 as a principle but does not specify the enforcement mechanism, waiver model, staged rollout, or recovery UX. Existing FEAT docs are stale on relevant points:
- FEAT-011 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:180 says DDx ships exactly one skill, but .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md already demonstrates nested sub-skill packaging.
- FEAT-004 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:150 owns bead validation hooks but doesn't describe authoring-quality lint or post-attempt triage.
- FEAT-010 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:21 documents the layer-1/2/3 invocation model but doesn't spec hook insertion points for ddx try / ddx work.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Author ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md covering:
   - Policy: lint pre-dispatch + triage post-attempt; both invoke the same bead-lifecycle sub-skill
   - Staged rollout: WARN-ONLY default → BLOCK opt-in after baseline confirms
   - Waiver model: rubric-level by bead-type (doc/epic/deletion skip certain criteria, per docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md:219); per-bead lint-waiver:<criterion> label for rare exceptions; --force --reason records an event (does not silently bypass)
   - Fail-open hook behavior (P1 from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES): infrastructure failure proceeds; only valid low lint score blocks (in BLOCK mode)
   - Operator recovery UX: blocked dispatch prints missing fields + suggested ddx bead update commands

2. Amend FEAT-004 (beads): add §"Authoring quality lint" describing the rubric, sub-skill location, waiver storage (label-based), and tracker metadata (no new schema fields; lint output ephemeral in evidence dir).

3. Amend FEAT-010 (executions): add §"Quality hooks" describing PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook insertion points in ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime; OutcomeReason field beside existing Disrupted in ExecuteBeadReport.

4. Amend FEAT-011 (skills): replace "ships exactly one skill" with the nested workflow-skill model demonstrated by bead-breakdown/, replay-bead/, etc. Document the 5 shipped-skill paths per codex review (skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/).

5. Cross-link: ADR-023 references RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652) + evidence (ddx-f339c399) + bead-authoring-template.md as the canonical template.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT (codex: not warranted; existing FEATs cover the surface)
- ADR for the in_progress-eligibility bug (separate concern)
- Cross-project skill packaging (deferred per FEAT-015)
- Schema changes to bead JSON (waivers via labels; no new fields)
title: ADR-023 + FEAT-004/010/011 amendments: bead-lifecycle quality enforcement policy

acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defines StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked, and drain.stop(state) is the only production budget/terminal stop classifier.
2. Cost-cap checks are removed from executor closure call sites and reviewer group cost is charged to the same budget accumulator before close eligibility is evaluated.
3. No-progress retry budget is incremented only for valid implementation attempts; TestStopCondition_NoProgress_IgnoresIntakeRoutingReviewAndOperatorStates covers intake block, decomposition, claim race, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review-error, and needs_human cases.
4. Review REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK no longer reopens a closed bead. A review_fixable_gap prevents close, records review-fixable-gap, and schedules one bounded repair cycle with repair_context_from_review_group. TestPreCloseReviewFixableGap_PreventsCloseAndSchedulesRepair covers this.
5. review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error transition to blocked with needs_human and do not consume no-progress budget. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress covers this.
6. MinPower retry/escalation behavior from ddx-cfedee8e is wired in the attempt/retry path without mutating explicit harness/model/profile pins. TestRetryPolicy_ReviewFixableGapRaisesMinPowerAndPreservesPins covers the path.
7. Execution records include append-only cycle trace data for initial and repair cycles: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer_index values, review verdicts, and final decision. TestExecutionTrace_RecordsInitialAndRepairCycles covers both cycles.
8. Downstream-consumer audit for cost-cap and stop-condition event names is committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The execution loop now needs a single durable stop/retry policy for the reliable factory workflow. Current code still exposes older post-merge review/reopen semantics and scattered stop logic: ExecuteBeadReport.ReviewVerdict is documented as post-merge in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226, Reopen is documented for REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300, no-progress suppression only excludes a narrow transient set at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576, and C9 previously depended on ddx-cfedee8e even though C9 absorbs that scope.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818 contain historical cost-cap stop sites outside a first-class StopCondition gate.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:226 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:300 still describe post-merge reviewer close/reopen behavior, which conflicts with ADR-024 default adversarial pre-close review.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1576 counts no-progress by base/result equality but only excludes transport/quota/routing/timeout/merge_conflict outcome reasons; intake blocks, decomposition, review errors, and operator-required states must not consume no-progress retry budget.
- ddx-9228a484 previously listed ddx-cfedee8e as a dependency while also absorbing it, creating a queue deadlock.

PROPOSED FIX
- Implement cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go with StopCondition values Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked.
- Move all budget/cost-cap decisions, including implementer plus adversarial reviewer group cost, into drain.stop(state).
- Relocate MinPower retry behavior from ddx-cfedee8e into the attempt/retry path without mutating requested harness/model/profile pins. A review_fixable_gap may raise MinPower for the repair attempt; spec gaps, missing acceptance, too-large, unsafe/out-of-scope, review errors beyond cap, intake ambiguity, and decomposition overflow block for the operator instead.
- Make no-progress budget count only valid implementation attempts where checkout/context existed and the implementer had an opportunity to modify or verify code. Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota/transport/auth/tool setup, review errors, and operator-action states.
- Keep append-only cycle trace fields for every implementation cycle and repair cycle: cycle_index, attempt_id, result_rev, implementer route facts, review_group_id, reviewer slots, review result, final decision.

NON-SCOPE
- Implementing the adversarial review gate itself; that is tracked separately.
- Frontend display of stop reasons.
- Poll-interval defaults and idle workers; already handled by ddx-dc157075 / C10.

description:
PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0 + ADR-023 (filed as ddx-XXXX): a sub-skill is needed at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ (and 4 other shipped paths) carrying the rubric, examples, and judge prompts for lint + triage + refine modes. ddx try will compose this sub-skill via the runner library.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md (existing) is the precedent for nested workflow sub-skills under the parent ddx skill. No equivalent exists for bead authoring quality. The bead-authoring-template.md at docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md is the canonical template (per codex review) but only has 1 strong example + 1 weak example (line 98 of template).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Create sub-skill directory bead-lifecycle/ under each of 5 shipped paths (codex amendment): skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/. SKILL.md + supporting files identical across all 5 (or symlinked if the build process supports it; check current bead-breakdown/ packaging).

2. SKILL.md frontmatter: name=bead-lifecycle, description="Score, classify, and refine ddx beads. Used by ddx try hooks pre-dispatch (lint mode) and post-attempt (triage mode); operator-invocable for refine mode."

3. SKILL.md body sections:
   - LINT MODE: input=bead JSON; output=JSON {score 0-8, rationale, suggested_fixes, waivers_applied}. References the 8-criterion rubric from bead-authoring-template.md.
   - TRIAGE MODE: input=bead + outcome event + session log excerpt; output=JSON {classification ∈ {already_satisfied, ambiguous, investigated_no_path, decomposed, operator_input_needed, routing, quota, transport, tests_red, merge_conflict, review_block, timeout}, recommended_action ∈ {refine_bead_and_retry, retry_with_more_context, file_children_and_supersede, escalate_to_operator, close_as_already_done, wait_and_retry, give_up}, rationale, suggested_amendments, suggested_followup_beads}.
   - REFINE MODE: input=bead + (optional) prior triage output; output=YAML diff suggesting amendments to bead description + AC.
   - WAIVER TABLE: per-bead-type criterion skips (doc-only skips named-tests + test-name; epic skips concrete-implementation; deletion skips wired-in-assertion). Codex: rubric-first, per-bead label override second.
   - 5-10 CURATED EXAMPLES under bead-lifecycle/examples/: code bug, feature, doc-only, epic, deletion/rename, no-op investigation, upstream/external work. Operator (epic owner ddx-a9d130d0) curates from existing closed beads; LLM does not pick.

4. Mode selection: invocation prompt MUST include "MODE: lint|triage|refine" as first line; SKILL.md instructs the LLM to read mode and apply only that mode's contract.

NON-SCOPE
- LLM judgment baked into Go (this bead is data + markdown only; no code)
- ddx try hook integration (separate child bead)
- Tooling to validate skill output JSON (deferred; LLMs are usually well-behaved with structured-output prompts)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first per FEAT-015)
title: skill: bead-lifecycle sub-skill (lint + triage + refine modes) shipped to all 5 paths + 5-10 curated examples

description:
PROBLEM
The pre-claim readiness payload shape lived only implicitly in the Go decoder structs and the producer prompt text, so the two drifted: the producer emitted `verdict` as a bool while the consumer parsed a string, idling a full fizeau queue (2026-05-18). Nothing in CI fails when producer and consumer disagree on the payload shape.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go` — the producer prompt text (:616-617) and the Go decoder structs (:68-119, :356-395) independently describe the same payload with no single source of truth, allowing silent drift.

PROPOSED FIX
Define the canonical payload in a shared schema file `cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json` that both the producer prompt and `decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult` reference, typing `readiness_checks[].verdict` as `oneOf [boolean, string, null]` with `additionalProperties:true` for forward-compat. Add a test that validates representative payloads (bool, string, null verdict, and a malformed kind) against the schema so producer/consumer drift fails CI.

NON-SCOPE
Does NOT implement the decoder coercion itself (decoder child). Does NOT touch the worker loop escalation or claim-rate signal, or README docs. DEPENDS ON the decoder child — the schema's verdict oneOf must match the kinds the decoder accepts. Preserves the FEAT-010 / ADR-023 readiness vocabulary.
title: Lock readiness_checks JSON schema in a shared file with a CI drift gate

acceptance:
1. `ddx install <name> --global --silent` installs the plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ and creates a symlink or copy under ~/.claude/skills/<name> and ~/.agents/skills/<name>.
2. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, in-tree mode) installs to <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links under <project>/.claude/skills/<name> and <project>/.agents/skills/<name>.
3. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
4. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
5. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.
6. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
7. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.
8. TestUpdateGlobal_UpdatesGlobalNotProject asserts `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree.
9. TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.
10. ddx doctor's two new lines ("Global Install" and "Project Install") render correctly.
11. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.
12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
13. Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit.
description:
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella command for first-time setup: installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates agent-tier links, and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.

4. `ddx init` (no flag) creates <project>/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins — plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project install over global.

5. Plugin lookup precedence at runtime:
   - <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/ (or ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ in convention mode) if present.
   - ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ otherwise.
   - Hardcoded baked-in fallback (the default ddx plugin only).

6. The "ddx-validate" pre-commit hook (per ddx-777f4a09 already landed) must continue to scope its checks correctly: skill-related validation runs only when skill files are staged, regardless of install location.

7. `ddx doctor` reports both install layers: "Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing" and "Project Install (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/ddx) — ok | missing | lazy-resolves-to-global".

SPEC IMPACT (in addition to ddx-06cbaa90's spec updates):
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-025-or-equivalent (skill install topology) — file or update.
- library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md "How this skill works" section — document install locations.
- library/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md (if exists) — document agent-tier paths.
- CLAUDE.md "Persona System" section — document install precedence.

NON-SCOPE
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths if any exist (likely none today).
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex (add others as those harnesses define their skill paths).

DEPS
ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — install paths route through ddxroot.Path() AND the new ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ path. Both must be agreed-on before this lands.

acceptance:
1. A harness child spawned by the execute-bead executor does NOT survive abnormal death of its parent worker. Verified by a test that starts the executor against a fake harness binary which forks a long-lived grandchild, then SIGKILLs the parent worker process (not graceful SIGTERM) and asserts the harness child + grandchild are gone (not PID-1 orphans) within a bounded grace. On Linux this is satisfied via SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig in cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go.
2. cmdSetProcessGroup sets Pdeathsig (Linux) and the thread-migration caveat is handled (LockOSThread or equivalent) so the death signal actually fires; a test or documented rationale covers the non-Linux fallback.
3. `ddx work` startup reaps orphaned execute-bead harness children from prior dead workers scoped to the current project/workspace, and does NOT kill harness children of other live workers. Verified by a test that plants a fake orphaned harness process (parent gone) and a fake live-owned one and asserts only the orphan is reaped.
4. The docker-clone backend (attempt_backend.go:293) configures process-group/container cleanup so its nested harness is reaped on worker exit; covered by a test or, if container teardown already guarantees this, a documented justification.
5. Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown still terminates in-flight harness children (regression guard).
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
When a `ddx work` orchestrator dies abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM, host reboot of the supervising process) while a bead attempt is in flight, the spawned harness child (e.g. `claude --print -p --verbose --output-format stream-json ... <execute-bead>` or `codex exec --json ...`) is NOT reaped. It reparents to PID 1 and keeps running indefinitely. Observed 2026-05-27 on the dev host: a `claude --print` execute-bead child orphaned with PPID 1 for ~9h53m after its `ddx work` parent had gone — burning a harness session and (per the sibling fizeau leak) anchoring leaked subprocess trees. This is the orchestrator-side mirror of fizeau-50b6f055 (harness-runner-side leak).

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 — OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir spawns the harness via exec.Command(binary, args...) then cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd).
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 — cmdSetProcessGroup sets ONLY SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}. There is NO Pdeathsig. So the kernel does nothing automatically when the parent dies.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 — cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd) SIGKILLs the negative pgid, but it is only invoked from the executor's graceful paths (stopProcess at executor.go:190-194, triggered by idle/wall-clock timeout, auth/rate-limit pattern, or ctx cancellation at executor.go:231-255).
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 — the worker context is a signal.NotifyContext on os.Interrupt + SIGTERM only. Graceful shutdown works; SIGKILL and crashes cannot be caught, so the defer/stopProcess chain never runs and the child survives.
- Net: child harness lifetime is governed entirely by parent-run cleanup. If the parent does not get to run cleanup, the child orphans. There is no kernel-enforced parent-death signal and no startup reaper that adopts/kills harness children whose parent PID is gone.

SECONDARY VECTOR
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:293 — the docker-clone backend spawns `exec.CommandContext(ctx, "docker", ...)` WITHOUT cmdSetProcessGroup, so even graceful cleanup may not reach the nested harness inside the container. (worktree/local-clone backends at attempt_backend.go:120-122 / :182-184 share the executor.go:156 path and do get Setpgid.)

PROPOSED FIX
1. Primary (Linux): set SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig = SIGKILL (or SIGTERM) in cmdSetProcessGroup so the kernel signals the harness child when the parent thread dies, regardless of how the parent died. Note the well-known caveat: Pdeathsig keys off the spawning OS *thread*, so pin/guard against Go runtime thread migration (runtime.LockOSThread around Start, or re-check parent PID in the child path). Document the macOS/Windows gap (Pdeathsig is Linux-only) and the chosen fallback there.
2. Backstop: on `ddx work` startup, reap orphaned harness children from prior dead workers — e.g. when registering/claiming, scan for execute-bead harness processes whose parent is PID 1 (or whose recorded owner PID is gone) and SIGKILL their process groups, gated to this project/workspace to avoid nuking unrelated work. Ties to the existing lease-release / operator-attention machinery (cross-ref ddx-8f2e0ebf).
3. docker-clone backend: apply cmdSetProcessGroup to the docker invocation at attempt_backend.go:293 (and/or run with --rm + a container-stop on cleanup) so the container tree is reaped too.

NON-SCOPE
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works (executor.go:231-255); do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this bead is the ddx orchestrator -> harness-child boundary.

CROSS-REF: fizeau-50b6f055 (fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup), ddx-8f2e0ebf (lease release / operator-attention).
title: execute-bead: harness child orphans to PID 1 when ddx work parent dies abnormally (SIGKILL/crash) — no Pdeathsig, no startup reaper

description:
PROBLEM: The fix for lock scoping + metrics needs integration test proof that multiple concurrent workers do not block each other.

FIX: Create a multi-worker scenario test that:
1. Spawns 5+ concurrent 'ddx work --watch' daemons on a test repo
2. Files independent beads for each worker
3. Each bead runs execute-bead with a short harness subprocess
4. Measures lock hold time across all workers
5. Asserts p99 lock hold time stays under configured cap (10s for index, 30s for tracker)
6. Verifies that operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell never times out during the test

This test should live under cli/internal/agent/ as a *_test.go file.

DEPENDS ON: ddx-0b743e7e (lock scoping), ddx-d8c98e4c (metrics)

CROSS-REF: Original Fizeau incident: 5 concurrent workers (PIDs 87523, 88624, 89333, 90753, 949868) with cascading timeouts.

description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-cc298105 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store read surface to be typed and ctx-first, but the read methods still accept untyped variadic arguments. This keeps the local bead package API ambiguous and makes the ddx-464feba2 caller sweep too large to execute as one bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:80-84 declares BeadReader with ReadAll(args ...any), ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), and Get(args ...any).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:181-240 exposes Store.ReadAll(args ...any) and Store.ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), using storeCallContext from cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:599-625 exposes Store.Get(args ...any), using argument scanning for the bead ID.
- cli/internal/bead/archive.go:158-174 exposes Store.GetWithArchive(id string) and currently supplies context.Background() internally instead of accepting caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the read-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), Get(ctx context.Context, id string), and GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadReader in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only read-method callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests that prove canceled contexts are honored and GetWithArchive forwards the supplied context instead of creating a background context.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and mutation signature children.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadReader methods with the same ctx-first typed read signatures.
- All Go callsites for Store.ReadAll, Store.ReadAllFiltered, Store.Get, and Store.GetWithArchive pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
- Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreReadAll_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreReadAllFiltered_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreGet_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreGetWithArchive_UsesSuppliedContext.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: bead store: migrate read APIs to ctx-first signatures: part 2

description:
PROBLEM
6 call sites in cli/internal/agent/{execute_bead_loop.go, execute_bead_post_review.go, execute_bead_review_terminal.go, execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go} duplicate the pattern of parking a bead to status=proposed with an intake event.

ROOT CAUSE
parkBeadPostIntakeRejection exists in execute_bead_loop.go:4016-4050 and is called from 6+ locations, but it directly manipulates Store and appends events inline. The Store interface does not expose a single public method that encapsulates both the transition and the event atomically.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Extract a new Store.ParkToProposed(id, actor, outcome, reason, detail, at time.Time) error method that:
   - Calls TransitionLifecycle to set status=proposed with OperatorRequired=true.
   - Appends a structured intake event.
   - Returns any error.
2. Update parkBeadPostIntakeRejection to use the new Store.ParkToProposed (or deprecate parkBeadPostIntakeRejection entirely if it becomes redundant).
3. Replace all 6 agent call sites with the new Store.ParkToProposed method.
4. Verify no functional change: output beads must have identical status and events.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the intake rejection policy itself. Do not add new parameters or optional behavior.
title: refactor: extract Store.ParkToProposed helper to consolidate intake rejection

acceptance:
1. TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost run under both jsonl and axon subtests.
2. The package conformance helpers make the backend selector explicit instead of silently defaulting in only one path.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
description:
PROBLEM
The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE
- GraphQL client generation.
- Backend selection/config plumbing.
- Subscription integration smoke tests.

acceptance:
1. When the same bead wedges on consecutive claims (default threshold 2), the worker stops claiming it, transitions it to an operator-attention/proposed state, surfaces it via an event, and the loop continues to the next ready bead. Verified by TestSameBeadTwiceWedgeGuardStopsClaiming which injects two consecutive wedges.
2. The consecutive-wedge marker resets when the bead next makes real progress, so a bead that wedges once then succeeds is not sidelined. Verified by TestWedgeMarkerResetsOnProgress.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes
description:
PROBLEM
A single wedged bead can starve the queue: ddx bead ready showed 30 dispatchable beads while one single-threaded worker re-claimed and re-wedged the same bead (parent ddx-8f2e0ebf, criterion E). There is no per-bead consecutive-wedge guard — a freshly released wedged bead is immediately re-claimed and re-wedged.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: the claim/dispatch loop keeps no record of "this bead wedged on its previous claim", so it re-claims a just-released wedged bead. The only existing per-bead suppression is route-exclusion dedup at execute_bead_loop.go:4970-4978, which dedups failed (provider, model) routes — not wedge/timeout releases. A route-independent wedge (e.g. resolver hang from child ddx-d8970a7b) therefore recurs indefinitely.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Record a per-bead wedge marker when a bead is released due to route-resolution timeout (child ddx-d8970a7b) or watchdog fire (child ddx-dc23f001).
2. On the next claim cycle, if the same bead still carries a wedge marker from the immediately preceding claim (consecutive wedge), the worker does NOT re-claim it; it transitions the bead to a proposed/operator-attention state, surfaces it via an event, and continues to the next ready bead so the queue keeps draining.
3. Make the consecutive-wedge threshold configurable (default 2 consecutive wedges). Reset the marker when the bead next makes real progress (a resolved route / LLM call / commit), so a transient single wedge does not permanently sideline a bead.

NON-SCOPE
Timeout detection (child ddx-d8970a7b), watchdog (child ddx-dc23f001), operator-attention query surface + docs (child E). Reuses the release/event primitives from those children and the atomic release from child ddx-449baa1d. This is a decomposition child of ddx-8f2e0ebf.
notes: Salvaged from ddx-8f2e0ebf-signature wedge. Consecutive-wedge claim guard landed. Verified independently: TestSameBeadTwiceWedgeGuardStopsClaiming + TestWedgeMarkerResetsOnProgress pass; full go test ./internal/agent/... green. Landed fbdce1c23.
title: worker: stop claiming a bead that wedges on consecutive claims and surface it for operator review

description:
PROBLEM
Current deadcode RTA on 2026-05-15 still reports residual preclaim/profile/ratelimit/recovery helpers as unreachable from the production graph, so `ddx-83440482` cannot close from current-tree evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`.ddx/executions/20260515T210515-1a20052a/production-reachability-final.json` reports residual dead symbols across `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:368`, `profile_select.go:72`, `ratelimit_retry.go:73`, `recovery_decompose.go:107`, `recovery_reframe.go:40`, `repair_prompt.go:24`, `service_run.go:581`, and `triage_dispatch.go:25`.

PROPOSED FIX
Resolve every symbol currently listed for these files in the committed artifact. WIRE recovery/profile/ratelimit behavior that is required in the live worker path; DELETE orphaned or superseded helpers that no runtime surface still exercises. If any symbol still needs deferred investigation after ~15 minutes, annotate it with `// wiring:pending <follow-up-bead-id>` and file the narrower follow-up before closing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not touch candidate-cycle, compare, or execute-bead residuals; those clusters are tracked separately.
- Do not change the production-reachability protocol or checker implementation.

rationale: status: proposed
reason: Test infrastructure dependencies prevent completing deletions without extensive test rewrites

## Analysis

The 19 unreachable symbols fall into three categories:

1. **DUPLICATE FUNCTIONS (11)** - Superseded by attempt.go implementations:
   - ratelimit_retry.go: EvaluateRateLimitWait, IsRateLimitResult, hasIsolated429, ParseRetryAfter, ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr, RateLimitRetryConfig.resolved, ctxSleep, RunWithRateLimitRetry, BuildRateLimitRouteAttempt

2. **ORPHANED FUNCTIONS (2)** - Never called from production or tests:
   - service_run.go: TestProviderConnectivityViaService, ValidateEffortForRunViaService
   - profile_select.go: WarmProfileSnapshotForProject

3. **PRODUCTION INFRASTRUCTURE (6)** - Used in production paths but test-utilities:
   - preclaim_intake_hook.go: NewPreClaimIntakeHook (used from workers.go)
   - preclaim_intake_hook.go: decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult (test wrapper)
   - profile_select.go: SelectCheapestProfile (test utility)
   - recovery_decompose.go: NewDecomposePostLadderExhaustionHook (hook infrastructure)
   - recovery_reframe.go: NewReframePostLadderExhaustionHook (hook infrastructure)
   - triage_dispatch.go: IsGitUpdateRefCompareAndSwapFailure (test utility)

## Blocker

Deleting categories #2 and #3 breaks 15+ test functions that specifically test these deleted functions. The tests cannot be easily updated because:
- Many tests directly call these functions (not through mocks)
- Some test utilities (like decodePreClaimIntakePayloadResult) are called from 6+ test functions
- Removing test utilities would eliminate test coverage of edge cases

## Recommendation

This work should be split into:
1. **Phase A (Immediate)**: Delete only the 11 duplicate ratelimit_retry functions (with corresponding test file deletion)
2. **Phase B (Follow-up bead)**: Decide on test utility functions (keep as test-only or delete+refactor all tests)
3. **Phase C (Follow-up bead)**: Wire production infrastructure functions (NewPreClaimIntakeHook, hooks) into CLI reachability graph

AC #2 requirement (no deadcode hits for these files) cannot be met without deleting test utilities, which breaks AC #5 (tests must pass).

## Summary

Operator review needed:
- Accept PENDING status for test utilities and production infrastructure
- Create Phase B bead for test utility disposition
- Create Phase C bead for hooking infrastructure into production entry points

description:
PROBLEM
A drain worker on a full ready queue that never successfully claims (because every pre-claim attempt warns and skips) presents as healthy: loop.idle every ~50s, process alive, no error. Silent idle on a non-empty queue is a stuck state masquerading as healthy. The operator has no top-level signal that claim throughput has collapsed.

CONTEXT / WHERE
ddx work --watch is wired in cli/cmd/work.go; the drain loop is cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go. A worker-status surface already exists (cli/internal/workerstatus/ and a work_status command per cli/cmd/work_status_test.go). Claim attempts and their outcomes (claimed vs skipped/warn) pass through the loop each iteration.

PROPOSED FIX
Track claim attempt outcomes over a rolling window (default 10 attempts, configurable) in the drain loop. When the claim success rate over the window falls below a configurable threshold (default 0%, i.e. zero successes across the full window) while ready beads remain, surface a top-level operator-visible warning:
  - to stderr (a single, deduplicated warning line, not per-iteration spam), AND/OR
  - via the existing worker-status surface (cli/internal/workerstatus or ddx work status) so an operator can query it.
The window size and threshold must be configurable with documented defaults.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the decoder (ddx-606fe854), the consecutive-fingerprint escalation (ddx-d67f124e), the CI schema gate, or README docs. Only the rolling claim-success-rate signal.
title: ddx work --watch: surface low claim-success-rate over a rolling window

Assert EXACTLY ONE worker executes (one execute-bead attempt / one attempt_id), B never claims, and NO attempt.terminated/bead_closed_externally event is produced for the bead.

description:
PROBLEM: No test covers the STAGGERED contention case that actually occurred (ddx-d740539d): worker A claims and is alive but its lease aged past TTL (mid-intake / mid-long-op), and worker B then scans and reclaims -> both execute. The existing TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaimIntakeRunsOncePerBead only covers a same-instant race with a FRESH lease. Real-world signature: an attempt.terminated reason=bead_closed_externally event in .ddx/attachments/ddx-d740539d/events.jsonl after two attempts overlapped ~16.6 min.

ROOT CAUSE: missing end-to-end exclusivity regression for the stale-but-alive-lease scenario. This bead pins the contract that C1 (ddx-d333ad8a, intake heartbeat) + C2 (ddx-ba93de9e, PID-death takeover) must satisfy.

PROPOSED FIX: Add a chaos/integration regression simulating the exact scenario; it must FAIL before C1+C2 and PASS after.

NON-SCOPE: the fixes themselves (C1/C2).

description:
PROBLEM
Silent idle on a full queue is a stuck state masquerading as healthy. During the Fizeau idle (2026-05-18), ddx work --watch kept polling (resource.preflight ok -> loop.idle every ~50s) while 23 beads sat ready and zero were claimed; the commit log was empty for the same 30-minute window. From the outside the worker looked alive. There was no top-level operator-visible signal that the claim success rate had collapsed to 0%.

ROOT CAUSE
The watch loop emits loop.idle at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1513 but tracks no rolling claim-success metric, so a 0% claim rate over a full queue produces the same output as a legitimately empty queue. The --watch flag is defined at cli/cmd/work.go:88.

PROPOSED FIX (parent proposed-fix E)
Track claim attempts versus successes over a configurable rolling window (default 10 attempts). When the success rate over that window falls below a configurable threshold (e.g. 0% over 10 attempts), surface an operator-visible warning via stderr and/or a status surface (ddx bead status or a ddx work status view). Add --claim-rate-window and --claim-rate-threshold flags to ddx work.

NON-SCOPE
- Decoder coercion (sibling decoder child).
- CI schema drift gate (sibling child).
- Same-fingerprint warn escalation (sibling escalation child; complementary but distinct trigger).
- README documentation (sibling child).

CROSS-REF
Governing artifact: AR-2026-05-17 follow-up beads. Adjacent silent-failure bugs: ddx-57c40485, ddx-8f2e0ebf.
title: ddx work --watch: surface low claim-success-rate over a rolling window

description:
PROBLEM: `/agent` redirects to project `sessions` (`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/agent/+page.ts:5`), which then 302s to `runs?layer=run` (`sessions/+page.ts`) — a double redirect (two network hops) for every /agent visit.

ROOT CAUSE: `agent/+page.ts` targets the intermediate sessions route; the `resolveDefaultProjectRoute` helper (`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/routing/shellRoutes.ts:32`) only knows documents/sessions/beads/graph/personas/plugins, so a direct hop needs the helper extended (or bypassed).

PROPOSED FIX: Make `/agent` redirect in a SINGLE hop straight to `runs?layer=run`, preserving the node/project resolution. Extend `resolveDefaultProjectRoute` (shellRoutes.ts:32) to support the `runs?layer=run` target (or compute the final URL directly in agent/+page.ts). Preserve the correct destination when a project does not exist (don't regress the fallback).

acceptance:
1. Child bead for root SIGINT/SIGTERM context and user-facing shutdown message exists and is closed.
2. Child bead for interrupted attempt cleanup, unclaim, liveness cleanup, and regression tests exists and is closed.
3. `ddx work` and `ddx try` both print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully` on first Ctrl-C/SIGTERM.
4. Interrupted non-terminal beads are open/re-claimable after cleanup and are not parked behind `work-retry-after`.
5. Second-signal hard-abort behavior is documented and tested or explicitly documented as best-effort.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestSignal|TestInterrupt|TestCancel|TestShutdown|TestDisrupted|TestClaim" -count=1` passes.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM
Operators routinely stop long-running `ddx work` / `ddx try` with Ctrl-C. The governing specs now require the first SIGINT/SIGTERM to cancel work cooperatively, print `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully`, preserve available evidence, release non-terminal claims, and leave unfinished beads re-claimable. The implementation beads need to land as one coherent interrupt story.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/main.go:31 calls cmd.Execute, and cli/cmd/root.go:31 calls rootCmd.Execute without a signal-backed context, so terminal SIGINT/SIGTERM kills the process instead of driving cooperative cancellation.
- cli/cmd/work.go:37-40 documents signal as a stop condition, but there is no root signal handler that turns Ctrl-C into that condition.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 can classify context cancellation as disrupted, but normal Ctrl-C does not currently reach this path.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:423-434 and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:177-190 now define the intended first-signal behavior and user-facing message.

PROPOSED FIX
Coordinate two implementation slices: root CLI signal context/user messaging, and work/try attempt cleanup/reclaim tests. When both children close, verify `ddx work` and `ddx try` behave consistently under Ctrl-C.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement resumable worktrees by default. Interrupted work may be preserved as evidence, but the safe default is cleanup and retry from scratch.
- Do not change queue ordering, routing policy, review policy, or cost-cap policy.
- Do not remove legacy work/execute-bead compatibility commands.
notes:
RELATED QUEUE ALIGNMENT
- ddx-9228a484 (StopCondition enum) now depends on this epic so Signal semantics are implemented after first-signal cooperative cancellation is defined.
- ddx-9f20a4bd (external claim liveness) intersects with interrupted heartbeat/liveness cleanup; do not reintroduce beads.jsonl heartbeat writes while implementing the cleanup child.
- ddx-eccc6efb (server-spawn ddx work subprocess) should rely on the same root signal behavior once server workers exec ddx work; keep server-specific SIGTERM/SIGKILL escalation as a supervisor layer, not a second queue-drain implementation.
title: work: graceful interrupt shutdown for work and try

2. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists, ResolvePlugin returns the global path with layer=="global".

4. The skill-install-topology ADR file exists and documents the project→global→baked-in precedence; the "Persona System" precedence text in CLAUDE.md and cli/cmd/CLAUDE.md matches it.

description:
PROBLEM (epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2 — "Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows")

Skills/personas/plugins can live in two install layers (project-local and machine-global). Runtime resolution must deterministically prefer the project copy, fall back to the global copy, and only then use the baked-in default ddx plugin. This resolution function is the foundation every install/update/doctor surface depends on, so it is split out first.

ROOT CAUSE
- The resolution helper lives at cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)`. It must return path + layer ("project"|"global"|"baked-in") with the documented precedence and a non-nil error for any other unresolved name. The project path is computed via `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)` (resolve.go:30) which already handles in-tree vs convention mode; the global path via `ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (resolve.go:35). Baked-in is valid only for name=="ddx" (resolve.go:40).
- The governing decision (skill install topology) and the precedence documentation in CLAUDE.md must match this behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Ensure cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin` implements precedence project → global → baked-in exactly, returning empty path for the baked-in layer (callers use defaultplugin.FS()).
2. Land/refresh the skill-install-topology ADR (docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md or the equivalent ADR referenced by CLAUDE.md) describing the three-layer precedence.
3. Confirm the "Persona System" sections of CLAUDE.md and cli/cmd/CLAUDE.md document the precedence (project-local → ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx → baked-in default).

NON-SCOPE
- The `--global` install/update CLI surface (sibling child).
- init/doctor wiring (sibling child).
- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).
- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.
- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.

DEPS
- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on before this lands.
title: registry: plugin lookup precedence project→global→baked-in + skill-install-topology spec

acceptance:
1. `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
2. TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
3. TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.
description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-937de9fc is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Skills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:

- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. Lives under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/ with symlinks/copies into the agent-tier paths.
- **install-in-project**: written under ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/. In in-tree mode that is <project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. <project>/.claude/skills/ link target).

Today `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. There is no `--global` surface. Operators who want a single Claude Code install per machine (the common case) must repeat the install for every project.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/install.go and cli/cmd/update.go target ddxroot.Path() unconditionally.
- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation.
- The agent-tier paths (~/.claude/skills/...) are written only when the operator manually links them, or via the legacy install paths that were retired (per the user's `feedback_no_local_replace` and the home-directory-install retirement noted in CLAUDE.md).

PROPOSED FIX

1. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:
   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/.
   - Write the plugin content there.
   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/<name>, ~/.codex/skills/<name>, and any others the agent registry knows about.
   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.

2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/<name>/ and links into <project>/.claude/skills/<name> AND <project>/.agents/skills/<name>. In convention mode (no <project>/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into <project>/.ddx/plugins/.

3. `ddx init --global` is
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `ddx install <name>` (no --global, convention mode) installs to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/plugins/<name>/ and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins/ path.
- TestPluginLookup_PrefersProjectOverGlobal asserts that when both paths have the plugin, the project copy wins.
- TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal asserts that when the project copy is missing, the global copy is used.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
title: skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and: part 2

acceptance:
1. TestAxonBackend_CreateAndGet, TestAxonBackend_UpdateMutatesAndPersists, TestAxonBackend_ClaimAndUnclaim, TestAxonBackend_DepAddRemoveAndTree, TestAxonBackend_AppendEventSplitsIntoEventsCollection, and TestAxonBackend_JSONLImportExportRoundTrip pass against the real Axon-backed implementation.
2. backend_axon.go no longer uses local JSONL snapshots as the primary storage path.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.

description:
PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace local snapshot writes with real GraphQL CRUD, dependency, and event mutations using cli/internal/bead/axon/.
- Preserve the two-collection model from TD-030: ddx_beads and ddx_bead_events, with event_of links.
- Keep the existing backend interface surface working for Create, Get, Update, Claim, Close, DepAdd, DepRemove, AppendEvent, ExportTo, and Import.
- Preserve jsonl as the default backend path so day-1 behavior does not change.

NON-SCOPE
- Backend selection config plumbing.
- Package test matrix broadening.
- WebSocket subscription smoke tests.

description:
PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store read surface to be typed and ctx-first, but Store.Get still accepts untyped variadic arguments. This keeps the bead lookup API ambiguous and forces callers through argument scanning for both context and bead ID.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:80-84 declares BeadReader with Get(args ...any).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:599-625 exposes Store.Get(args ...any), using argument scanning for the bead ID and storeCallContext from cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only Store.Get to the typed ctx-first signature Get(ctx context.Context, id string). Update the corresponding BeadReader interface method in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only Get callsites across the repo to pass context and id explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests proving canceled contexts are honored and the id is supplied through the typed parameter rather than variadic scanning.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Get.
- ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, and GetWithArchive signature migration; those are assigned to sibling children.
- Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Immediate parent bead: ddx-cc298105.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id, mutation signature, list read, and GetWithArchive children.
title: bead store: make Get read API ctx-first

description:

Per cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:288, when AgentRunnerOverride is nil the pre-claim intake / AC-quality / post-attempt-triage hooks build a live qualityRunner that attempts route resolution and blocks until timeout.
